translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Men were once a community of one faith; but they differed (and followed different ways). Had it not been for the word proclaimed by your Lord before, their differences would have been resolved.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: Mankind were only one nation* and then they differed, and had it not been for a promise** from your Lord, the matters in which they differed would have been decided here itself. (* On one proper faith. ** The account on the Last Day.)

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Mankind were only one nation, then they fell into variance. But for a word that preceded from thy Lord, it had been decided between them already touching their differences.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And mankind were not but a single ccmmunity then they differed. And had not a word from thy Lord gone forth, it would have been decreed between them in respect of that wherein they differ.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Mankind were but one community (i.e. on one religion - Islamic Monotheism), then they differed (later), and had not it been for a Word that went forth before from your Lord, it would have been settled between them regarding what they differed.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Mankind was a single community; then they differed. Were it not for a prior decree from your Lord, the matters over which they had disputed would have been settled.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Once all men were but a single community; then they disagreed (and formulated different beliefs and rites). Had it not been that your Lord had already so ordained, a decisive judgement would have been made regarding their disagreements.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Mankind were but one community, then they differed (later); and had not it been for a Word that went forth before from your Lord, it would have been settled between them regarding what they differed.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Mankind were but one community; then they differed; and had it not been for a word that had already gone forth from thy Lord it had been judged between them in respect of that wherein they differ.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: (Before) mankind were but one nation, then they differed with one another. But for a Word that preceded from your Lord, it (the matters) over which they differed had already been decided.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And mankind was not but one community [united in religion], but [then] they differed. And if not for a word that preceded from your Lord, it would have been judged between them [immediately] concerning that over which they differ.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: All people (once) followed one belief. Then they began to follow different beliefs. Had not a word of your Lord (His decision to give every one time and free will) been decreed, God would already have settled their differences.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And people are naught but a single nation, so they disagree; and had not a word already gone forth from your Lord, the matter would have certainly been decided between them in respect of that concerning which they disagree.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Mankind was only one community, but then they differed, and had it not been for a prior word from your Lord, their differences would have been settled for them.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Mankind was but one nation, but differed (later). Had it not been for a word that went forth before from thy Lord, their differences would have been settled between them.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Men belonged to a single community, and God sent them messengers to give them happy tidings and warnings, and sent the Book with them containing the truth to judge between them in matters of dispute; but only those who received it differed after receiving clear proofs, on account of waywardness (and jealousies) among them. Then God by His dispensation showed those who believed the way to the truth about which they were differing; for God shows whom He please the path that is straight.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: Mankind was on one religion; so Allah sent Prophets giving glad tidings and warnings – and with them sent down the true Book to judge between mankind on their differences; and only those to whom it was given created disputes regarding the Book, after clear commands had come to them, due to hostility of one another; so Allah, by His command, made the truth clear to the believers, concerning their disputes; and Allah may guide whomever He wills to the Straight Path.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: The people were one nation; then God sent forth the Prophets, good tidings to bear and warning, and He sent down with them the Book with the truth, that He might decide between the people touching their differences; and only those who had been given it were at variance upon it, after the clear signs had come to them, being insolent one to another; then God guided those who believed to the truth, touching which they were at variance, by His leave; and God guides whomsoever He will to a straight path.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Mankind was one community; thereafter Allah raised prophets as bearers of glad tidings and warners, as He sent down the Book With truth that He may judge between mankind in that wherein they disputed. And none differed therein save those unto whom it was vouchsafed after the evidences had come to them, out of spite among themselves; then Allah guided those who believed unto the truth of that regarding which they differed, by His leave. Allah guideth whomsoever He listeth unto a path straight.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Mankind were one community and Allah sent Prophets with glad tidings and warnings, and with them He sent the Scripture in truth to judge between people in matters wherein they differed. And only those to whom (the Scripture) was given differed concerning it after clear proofs had come unto them through hatred, one to another. Then Allah by His Leave guided those who believed to the truth of that wherein they differed. And Allah guides whom He wills to a Straight Path.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Humanity used to be one community; then God sent the prophets, bringing good news and giving warnings. And He sent down with them the Scripture, with the truth, to judge between people regarding their differences. But none differed over it except those who were given it—after the proofs had come to them—out of mutual envy between them. Then God guided those who believed to the truth they had disputed, in accordance with His will. God guides whom He wills to a straight path.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: In the beginning all the people followed the same way. (Afterwards there came a change and differences arose). Then Allah sent Prophets to give good tidings to those who followed the Right Way and warnings to those who swerved from it. And He sent down with them the Book based on the Truth so that it should judge between the people concerning their differences. (Differences arose not because people were not given the knowledge of the Truth in the beginning, nay), differences arose between those very people who had been given clear teachings, and (for no other reasons than that) they wanted to tyrannize over one another. So Allah, by His leave, guided those who believed in the Prophets to the Truth about which they had differed; AIIah guides whomever He pleases to the Right Way.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Mankind was one community and Allah sent Prophets with glad tidings and warnings, and with them He sent down the Scripture in truth to judge between people in matters wherein they differed. And only those to whom (the Scripture) was given differed concerning it, after clear proofs had come unto them, through hatred, one to another. Then Allah by His leave guided those who believed to the truth of that wherein they differed. And Allah guides whom He wills to the straight path.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Mankind were one community, and Allah sent (unto them) prophets as bearers of good tidings and as warners, and revealed therewith the Scripture with the truth that it might judge between mankind concerning that wherein they differed. And only those unto whom (the Scripture) was given differed concerning it, after clear proofs had come unto them, through hatred one of another. And Allah by His Will guided those who believe unto the truth of that concerning which they differed. Allah guideth whom He will unto a straight path.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: The people were one nation. Then Allah sent forth Prophets to give them glad tidings and to warn them; and with them He sent down the Book with the truth, that He might rule between the people in that which they differ. Only those to whom it had been given differed about it after the clear verses had come to them, transgressing between themselves. Then Allah guided those who believed concerning that which they were at variance in the truth, by His permission. Allah guides whom He will to the Straight Path.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Mankind was [of] one religion [before their deviation]; then Allah sent the prophets as bringers of good tidings and warners and sent down with them the Scripture in truth to judge between the people concerning that in which they differed. And none differed over the Scripture except those who were given it - after the clear proofs came to them - out of jealous animosity among themselves. And Allah guided those who believed to the truth concerning that over which they had differed, by His permission. And Allah guides whom He wills to a straight path.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: At one time all people were only one nation. God sent Prophets with glad news and warnings. He sent the Book with them for a genuine purpose to provide the people with the ruling about disputed matters among them. No one disputed this matter except those who had already received evidence before. Their dispute was only because of their own hostility. To deal with this dispute, God, through His will, sent guidance to the believers. God guides to the right path whomever He wants.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: (All) people are a single nation; so Allah raised prophets as bearers of good news and as warners, and He revealed with them the Book with truth, that it might judge between people in that in which they differed; and none but the very people who were given it differed about it after clear arguments had come to them, revolting among themselves; so Allah has guided by His will those who believe to the truth about which they differed and Allah guides whom He pleases to the right path.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Mankind was once a single community, [but then people developed differences], so God sent prophets to them as bearers of good tidings and warning, and sent down with them the Book containing the truth, so that He might judge between their disputes. It was only those to whom it [the scripture] was given who disagreed about it after clear signs had come to them, because of rivalry between them. God by His will guided the believers to the truth about which the others had disputed. God guides whom He will to a straight path.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Mankind was one single nation, and Allah sent Messengers with glad tidings and warnings; and with them He sent the Book in truth, to judge between people in matters wherein they differed; but the People of the Book, after the clear Signs came to them, did not differ among themselves, except through selfish contumacy. Allah by His Grace Guided the believers to the Truth, concerning that wherein they differed. For Allah guided whom He will to a path that is straight.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: God had chosen Adam and Noah and the families of Abraham and 'Imran in preference to others.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: God chose Adam and Noah and the House of Abraham and the House of Imran above all beings, the

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Verily did Allah choose Adam and Nuh and the house of lbrahim and the house of 'lmran out of the worlds

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Allah chose Adam, Nuh (Noah), the family of Ibrahim (Abraham) and the family of 'Imran above the 'Alamin (mankind and jinns) (of their times).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: God chose Adam, and Noah, and the family of Abraham, and the family of Imran, over all mankind.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Truly Allah chose Adam and Noah and the descendants of Abraham and of 'Imran above all mankind.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Allah chose Adam, Nuh (Noah), the family of Ibrahim and the family of `Imran above the nations.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Lo! Allah preferred Adam and Noah and the Family of Abraham and the Family of 'Imran above (all His) creatures.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Indeed Allah chose Adam and Noah, and the progeny of Abraham and the progeny of Imran above all the nations;

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Allah chose Adam and Noah, the House of Abraham and the House of Imran above all worlds.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Indeed, Allah chose Adam and Noah and the family of Abraham and the family of 'Imran over the worlds -

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: God chose (and gave distinction to) Adam, Noah, the family of Abraham, and Imran over all the people of the world.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Surely Allah chose Adam and Nuh and the descendants of Ibrahim and the descendants of Imran above the nations.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: God chose Adam and Noah and the family of Abraham and the family of 'Imran above all His creatures.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Allah did choose Adam and Noah, the family of Abraham, and the family of 'Imran above all people,-

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: They were descendants of one another; and God hears all and knows everything.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: They are the descendants one of another; and Allah is the All Hearing, the All Knowing.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: seed of one another; God hears, and knows.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Progeny, one of the other, and Allah is Hearing, Knowing.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Offspring, one of the other, and Allah is the All-Hearer, All-Knower.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Offspring one of the other. God is Hearer and Knower.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: (for His messengership) - a people alike and the seed of one another. Allah is All-Hearing, All-Knowing.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Offspring, one of the other, and Allah is All-Hearer, All-Knower.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: They were descendants one of another. Allah is Hearer, Knower.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: They were the offspring of one another. Allah is Hearing, and Knowing.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Descendants, some of them from others. And Allah is Hearing and Knowing.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: They were the offspring of one another. God is All-hearing and All-seeing.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Offspring one of the other; and Allah is Hearing, Knowing.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: They are the offspring of one another. God hears all and knows all.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Offspring, one of the other: And Allah heareth and knoweth all things.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: He has laid down for you the (same) way of life and belief which He had commended to Noah, and which We have enjoined on you, and which We had bequeathed to Abraham, Moses and Jesus, so that they should maintain the order and not be divided among themselves. Heavy is to idolaters what you invite them to. God chooses whom He please for Himself, and guides to Himself whoever turns to Him.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: He has laid down for you as religion that He charged Noah with, and that We have revealed to thee, and that We charged Abraham with, Moses and Jesus: 'Perform the religion, and scatter not regarding it. Very hateful is that for the idolaters, that thou callest them to. God chooses unto Himself whomsoever He will, and He guides to Himself whosoever turns, penitent.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: He hath instituted for you in religion that which He had enjoined upon Nuh, and which We have revealed Unto thee, and which We had enjoined upon Ibrahim and Musa and Isa, saying: establish the religion, and be not divided therein. Grievous Unto the associaters is that Unto which thou callest them. Allah chooseth for Himself whomsoever He will and guideth Unto Himself whomsoever turneth in penitence.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: He (Allah) has ordained for you the same religion (Islam) which He ordained for Nuh (Noah), and that which We have inspired in you (O Muhammad SAW), and that which We ordained for Ibrahim (Abraham), Musa (Moses) and 'Iesa (Jesus) saying you should establish religion (i.e. to do what it orders you to do practically), and make no divisions in it (religion) (i.e. various sects in religion). Intolerable for the Mushrikun, is that to which you (O Muhammad SAW) call them. Allah chooses for Himself whom He wills, and guides unto Himself who turns to Him in repentance and in obedience.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He prescribed for you the same religion He enjoined upon Noah, and what We inspired to you, and what We enjoined upon Abraham, and Moses, and Jesus: “You shall uphold the religion, and be not divided therein.” As for the idolaters, what you call them to is outrageous to them. God chooses to Himself whom He wills, and He guides to Himself whoever repents.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: He has prescribed for you the religion which He enjoined upon Noah and which We revealed to you (O Muhammad), and which We enjoined upon Abraham and Moses and Jesus, commanding: “Establish this religion and do not split up regarding it.” What you are calling to is very hard upon those who associate others with Allah in His Divinity. Allah chooses for Himself whomsoever He pleases and guides to Himself whoever penitently turns to Him.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: He (Allah) has ordained for you the same religion which He ordained for Nuh, and that which We have revealed to you, and that which We ordained for Ibrahim, Musa and `Isa saying you should establish religion and make no divisions in it. Intolerable for the idolators is that to which you call them. Allah chooses for Himself whom He wills, and guides unto Himself who turns to Him in repentance.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: He hath ordained for you that religion which He commended unto Noah, and that which We inspire in thee (Muhammad), and that which We commended unto Abraham and Moses and Jesus, saying: Establish the religion, and be not divided therein. Dreadful for the idolaters is that unto which thou callest them. Allah chooseth for Himself whom He will, and guideth unto Himself him who turneth (toward Him).

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: He has prescribed for you the religion which He had enjoined upon Noah and which We have [also] revealed to you, and which We had enjoined upon Abraham, Moses and Jesus, declaring, ‘Maintain the religion, and do not be divided in it.’ Hard on the polytheists is that to which you summon them. Allah chooses for it whomever He wishes, and He guides to it whomever returns penitently [to Him].

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: He has made plain for you the Religion with which He charged Noah and that which We have revealed to you, and that with which We charged (Prophets) Abraham, Moses and Jesus, (saying): 'Establish the Religion and do not be divided therein' That which you invite them to is too overwhelming for the idolaters. Allah brings close to Himself whom He will, and guides to Him those who turn in repentance.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: He has ordained for you of religion what He enjoined upon Noah and that which We have revealed to you, [O Muhammad], and what We enjoined upon Abraham and Moses and Jesus - to establish the religion and not be divided therein. Difficult for those who associate others with Allah is that to which you invite them. Allah chooses for Himself whom He wills and guides to Himself whoever turns back [to Him].

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: He has plainly clarified the religion which is revealed to you and that which Noah, Abraham, Moses, and Jesus were commanded to follow (He has explained it) so that you would be steadfast and united in your religion. What you call the pagans to is extremely grave for them. God attracts to (the religion) whomever He wants and guides to it whoever turns to Him in repentance.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: He has made plain to you of the religion what He enjoined upon Nuh and that which We have revealed to you and that which We enjoined upon Ibrahim and Musa and Isa that keep to obedience and be not divided therein; hard to the unbelievers is that which you call them to; Allah chooses for Himself whom He pleases, and guides to Himself him who turns (to Him), frequently.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: God has ordained for you the same religion which He enjoined on Noah, and which We have revealed to you, and which We enjoined upon Abraham and Moses and Jesus, so that you should remain steadfast in religion and not become divided in it. What you call upon the polytheists to do is hard for them; God chooses for Himself whoever He pleases and guides towards Himself those who turn to Him.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: The same religion has He established for you as that which He enjoined on Noah - the which We have sent by inspiration to thee - and that which We enjoined on Abraham, Moses, and Jesus: Namely, that ye should remain steadfast in religion, and make no divisions therein: to those who worship other things than Allah, hard is the (way) to which thou callest them. Allah chooses to Himself those whom He pleases, and guides to Himself those who turn (to Him).

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: We have sent revelations to you as We sent revelations to Noah and the prophets (who came) after him; and We sent revelations to Abraham and Ishmael and Isaac and Jacob, and their offspring, and to Jesus and Job, and to Jonah and Aaron and Solomon, and to David We gave the Book of Psalms,

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: Indeed We sent a divine revelation to you (Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessing be upon him) as We did send divine revelations to Nooh (Noah) and the Prophets after him; and We sent divine revelations to Ibrahim (Abraham) and Ismael (Ishmael) and Ishaq (Isaac) and Yaqub (Jacob) and their offspring, and Eisa (Jesus) and Ayyub (Job) and Yunus (Jonah) and Haroon (Aaron) and Sulaiman (Solomon), and We bestowed the Zaboor (the Holy Book) upon Dawud (David).

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: We have revealed to thee as We revealed to Noah, and the Prophets after him, and We revealed to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, and the Tribes, Jesus and Job, Jonah and Aaron and Solomon, and We gave to David Psalms,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Verily We have revealed unto thee even as We revealed unto Nuh and the prophets after him, and as We revealed unto Ibrahim and Isma'il and Is-haq and Ya'qub and the tribes, and 'Isa and Ayyub and Yunus and Harun and Sulaiman, and unto Daud We vouchsafed a Scripture.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Verily, We have inspired you (O Muhammad SAW) as We inspired Nuh (Noah) and the Prophets after him; We (also) inspired Ibrahim (Abraham), Isma'il (Ishmael), Ishaque (Isaac), Ya'qub (Jacob), and Al-Asbat [the twelve sons of Ya'qub (Jacob)], 'Iesa (Jesus), Ayub (Job), Yunus (Jonah), Harun (Aaron), and Sulaiman (Solomon), and to Dawud (David) We gave the Zabur (Psalms).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: We have inspired you, as We had inspired Noah and the prophets after him. And We inspired Abraham, and Ishmael, and Isaac, and Jacob, and the Patriarchs, and Jesus, and Job, and Jonah, and Aaron, and Solomon. And We gave David the Psalms.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: (O Muhammad!) We have revealed to you as We revealed to Noah and the Prophets after him, and We revealed to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob and the offspring of Jacob, and Jesus and Job, and Jonah, and Aaron and Solomon, and We gave to David Psalms.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Verily, We have sent the revelation to you as We sent the revelation to Nuh and the Prophets after him; We (also) sent the revelation to Ibrahim, Isma`il, Ishaq, Ya`qub, and Al-Asbat, (the offspring of the twelve sons of Ya`qub) `Isa, Ayyub, Yunus, Harun, and Sulayman; and to Dawud We gave the Zabur.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Lo! We inspire thee as We inspired Noah and the prophets after him, as We inspired Abraham and Ishmael and Isaac and Jacob and the tribes, and Jesus and Job and Jonah and Aaron and Solomon, and as We imparted unto David the Psalms;

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: We have indeed revealed to you as We revealed to Noah and the prophets after him, and [as] We revealed to Abraham and Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, and the Tribes, Jesus and Job, Jonah, Aaron, and Solomon—and We gave David the Psalms—

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: We have revealed to you as We revealed to Noah and to the Prophets after him, and We revealed to (Prophets) Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob and the tribes, Jesus, Job, Jonah, Aaron, and Solomon, and We gave to David the Psalms.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Indeed, We have revealed to you, [O Muhammad], as We revealed to Noah and the prophets after him. And we revealed to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, the Descendants, Jesus, Job, Jonah, Aaron, and Solomon, and to David We gave the book [of Psalms].

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: (Muhammad), We have sent revelations to you just as were sent to Noah and the Prophets who lived after him and to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, his descendants, Jesus, Job, Jonah, Aaron, and Solomon. We gave the Psalms to David.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Surely We have revealed to you as We revealed to Nuh, and the prophets after him, and We revealed to Ibrahim and Ismail and Ishaq and Yaqoub and the tribes, and Isa and Ayub and Yunus and Haroun and Sulaiman and We gave to Dawood

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We have sent revelation to you [Prophet] as We did to Noah and the prophets who came after him, to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, and the Tribes, to Jesus, Job, Jonah, Aaron, and Solomon and David, to whom We gave the Psalms.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: We have sent thee inspiration, as We sent it to Noah and the Messengers after him: we sent inspiration to Abraham, Isma'il, Isaac, Jacob and the Tribes, to Jesus, Job, Jonah, Aaron, and solomon, and to David We gave the Psalms.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And We gave him Isaac and Jacob and guided them, as We had guided Noah before them, and of his descendants, David and Solomon and Job and Joseph and Moses and Aaron. Thus We reward those who are upright and do good.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And We gave to him Isaac and Jacob -- each one We guided, And Noah We guided before; and of his seed David and Solomon, Job and Joseph, Moses and Aaron -- even so We recompense the good-doers --

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And We bestowed upon him Is-haq and Ya'qub: each one We guided. And Nuh We had guided afore, and of his progeny Da-ud and Sulaiman and Ayyub and Yusuf and Musa and Harun. And thus We recompense the well-doers.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And We bestowed upon him Ishaque (Isaac) and Ya'qub (Jacob), each of them We guided, and before him, We guided Nuh (Noah), and among his progeny Dawud (David), Sulaiman (Solomon), Ayub (Job), Yusuf (Joseph), Musa (Moses), and Harun (Aaron). Thus do We reward the good-doers.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And We gave him Isaac and Jacob—each of them We guided. And We guided Noah previously; and from his descendants David, and Solomon, and Job, and Joseph, and Moses, and Aaron. Thus We reward the righteous.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And We bestowed upon Abraham (offspring) Ishaq (Isaac) and Ya'qub (Jacob) and each of them did We guide to the right way as We had earlier guided Noah to the right way; and (of his descendants We guided) Da'ud (David) and Sulayman (Solomon), Ayyub (Job), Yusuf (Joseph), Musa (Moses) and Harun (Aaron). Thus do We reward those who do good.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And We bestowed upon him Ishaq and Ya`qub, each of them We guided, and before him, We guided Nuh and among his progeny Dawud, Sulayman, Ayyub, Yusuf, Musa, and Harun. Thus do We reward the good-doers.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And We bestowed upon him Isaac and Jacob; each of them We guided; and Noah did We guide aforetime; and of his seed (We guided) David and Solomon and Job and Joseph and Moses and Aaron. Thus do We reward the good.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: And We gave him Isaac and Jacob and guided each of them. And Noah We had guided before, and from his offspring, David and Solomon, Job, Joseph, Moses and Aaron—thus do We reward the virtuous—

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: We gave him Isaac and Jacob and guided both; and We guided Noah before them, among his descendants were David and Solomon, Job, Joseph, Moses and Aaron as such, We recompense the gooddoers,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And We gave to Abraham, Isaac and Jacob - all [of them] We guided. And Noah, We guided before; and among his descendants, David and Solomon and Job and Joseph and Moses and Aaron. Thus do We reward the doers of good.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We gave (Abraham) Isaac and Jacob. Both had received Our guidance. Noah received Our guidance before Abraham and so did his descendants: David, Solomon, Job, Joseph, Moses, and Aaron. Thus is the reward for the righteous people.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And We gave to him Ishaq and Yaqoub; each did We guide, and Nuh did We guide before, and of his descendants, Dawood and Sulaiman and Ayub and Yusuf and Musa and Haroun; and thus do We reward those who do good (to others).

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We gave him Isaac and Jacob, each of whom We guided as We had guided Noah before. Among his descendants were David and Solomon, and Job, Joseph, Moses and Aaron. Thus do We reward the righteous.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: We gave him Isaac and Jacob: all (three) guided: and before him, We guided Noah, and among his progeny, David, Solomon, Job, Joseph, Moses, and Aaron: thus do We reward those who do good:

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Zachariah and John We guided, and guided Jesus and Elias who were all among the upright.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And (We guided) Zakaria and Yahya (John) and Eisa and Elias; all these are worthy of Our proximity.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Zachariah and John, Jesus and Elias; each was of the righteous;

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And also Zakariyya and Yahya and Isa and llyas: each one was of the righteous.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And Zakariya (Zachariya), and Yahya (John) and 'Iesa (Jesus) and Iliyas (Elias), each one of them was of the righteous.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And Zechariah, and John, and Jesus, and Elias—every one of them was of the upright.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: (And of his descendants We guided) Zakariya (Zachariah), Yahya (John), Isa (Jesus) and Ilyas (Elias): each one of them was of the righteous.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And Zakariyya, and Yahya and `Isa and Iliyas, each one of them was of the righteous.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And Zachariah and John and Jesus and Elias. Each one (of them) was of the righteous.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: and (Prophets) Zachariah, John, Jesus and Elias, each was of the righteous,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And Zechariah and John and Jesus and Elias - and all were of the righteous.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We also gave guidance to Zacharias, John, Jesus, and Elias, who were all pious people,

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And Zakariya and Yahya and Isa and Ilyas; every one was of the good;

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Zachariah, John, Jesus, and Elijah, every one of them was righteous,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And Zakariya and John, and Jesus and Elias: all in the ranks of the righteous:

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: We gave guidance to Ishmael, Elisha and Jonah and Lot; And We favoured them over the other people of the world,

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And Ismael (Ishmael) and Yasa’a (Elisha) and Yunus (Jonah) and Lut (Lot); and to each one during their times, We gave excellence over all others.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Ishmael and Elisha, Jonah and Lot-each one We preferred above all beings;

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And Ismai'l and Alyas'a and Yunus and Lut: each one of them We preferred above the worlds.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And Isma'il (Ishmael) and Al-Yas'a (Elisha), and Yunus (Jonah) and Lout (Lot), and each one of them We preferred above the 'Alamin (mankind and jinns) (of their times).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And Ishmael, and Elijah, and Jonah, and Lot—We favored each one of them over all other people.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: (And of his descendants We guided) Isma'il (Ishmael), al-Yasa' (Elisha), Yunus (Jonah), and Lut (Lot). And each one of them We favoured over all mankind.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And Isma`il and Al-Yasa`, and Yunus and Lut, and each one of them We preferred above the `Alamin (mankind and Jinns, of their times).

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And Ishmael and Elisha and Jonah and Lot. Each one (of them) did We prefer above (Our) creatures,

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: and Ishmael, Elisha, Jonah and Lot. Each We preferred above the worlds,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And Ishmael and Elisha and Jonah and Lot - and all [of them] We preferred over the worlds.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: and Ishmael, Elisha, Jonah, and Lot whom We exalted over all people.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And Ismail and Al-Yasha and Yunus and Lut; and every one We made to excel (in) the worlds:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Ishmael, Elisha, Jonah, and Lot -- We favoured each one of them above other people,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And Isma'il and Elisha, and Jonas, and Lot: and to all We gave favour above the nations:

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: As We did some of their fathers and progeny and brethren, and chose them, and showed them the right path.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And some of their ancestors and their descendants and their brothers; and We chose them and guided them to the Straight Path.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and of their fathers, and of their seed, and of their brethren; and We elected them, and We guided them to a straight path.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And also some of their fathers and their progeny and their brethren: We chose them and guided them onto the right path.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And also some of their fathers and their progeny and their brethren, We chose them, and We guided them to a Straight Path.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And of their ancestors, and their descendants, and their siblings—We chose them, and guided them to a straight path.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And likewise We elected for Our cause and guided on to a straight way some of their forefathers and their offspring and their brethren.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And also some of their fathers and their progeny and their brethren, We chose them, and We guided them to a straight path.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: With some of their forefathers and their offspring and their brethren; and We chose them and guided them unto a straight path.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: as We did their fathers, their descendants, and their brothers. We chose them and guided them to a Straight Path.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And [some] among their fathers and their descendants and their brothers - and We chose them and We guided them to a straight path.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: From their fathers, descendants, and brothers, We chose (certain) people and guided them to the right path.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And from among their fathers and their descendants and their brethren, and We chose them and guided them into the right way.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: and also some of their forefathers, their offspring, and their brothers: We chose them and guided them to a straight path.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: (To them) and to their fathers, and progeny and brethren: We chose them, and we guided them to a straight way.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: When We made the covenant with the prophets, and with you, as with Noah and Abraham, Moses and Jesus son of Mary, a binding covenant,

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And when We took compact from the Prophets, and from thee, and from Noah, and Abraham, Moses, and Jesus, Mary's son; We took from them a solemn compact,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And recall what time We took a bond from the prophets and from thee and from Nuh and Ibrahim and Musa and 'Isa son of Maryam. And We took from them a solemn bond;

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And (remember) when We took from the Prophets their covenant, and from you (O Muhammad SAW), and from Nuh (Noah), Ibrahim (Abraham), Musa (Moses), and 'Iesa (Jesus), son of Maryam (Mary). We took from them a strong covenant.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Recall that We received a pledge from the prophets, and from you, and from Noah, and Abraham, and Moses, and Jesus son of Mary. We received from them a solemn pledge.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And call to mind, (O Prophet), when We took the covenant from all Prophets; and also from you and Noah and Abraham, Moses, and Jesus the son of Mary. We took from them a solemn covenant

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And when We took from the Prophets their covenant, and from you, and from Nuh, Ibrahim, Musa, and `Isa son of Maryam. We took from them a strong covenant.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And when We exacted a covenant from the prophets, and from thee (O Muhammad) and from Noah and Abraham and Moses and Jesus son of Mary. We took from them a solemn covenant;

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: [Recall] when We took a pledge from the prophets, and from you and from Noah and Abraham and Moses and Jesus son of Mary, and We took from them a solemn pledge,

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: We took from the Prophets their covenant and from you (Prophet Muhammad), from Noah and Abraham, from Moses and Jesus the son of Mary. We took a solemn covenant from them,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And [mention, O Muhammad], when We took from the prophets their covenant and from you and from Noah and Abraham and Moses and Jesus, the son of Mary; and We took from them a solemn covenant.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We had a solemn covenant with you (Muhammad), and the Prophets: Noah, Abraham, Moses, and Jesus, the son of Mary.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And when We made a covenant with the prophets and with you, and with Nuh and Ibrahim and Musa and Isa, son of Marium, and We made with them a strong covenant

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We took a solemn pledge from the prophets, from you and Noah, Abraham, Moses and Jesus, the son of Mary -- We took a solemn pledge from all of them.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And remember We took from the prophets their covenant: As (We did) from thee: from Noah, Abraham, Moses, and Jesus the son of Mary: We took from them a solemn covenant:

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: We sent Noah and Abraham, and gave prophethood to their progeny and the Book, and some of them are well-directed, but many of them are disobedient.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And We sent Noah, and Abraham, and We appointed the Prophecy and the Book to be among their seed; and some of them are guided, and many of them are ungodly.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And assuredly We sent Nuh and Ibrahim, and We placed in the posterity of the twain the prophecy and the Book. Then of them some are guided ones, and many of them are transgressors.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And indeed, We sent Nuh (Noah) and Ibrahim (Abraham), and placed in their offspring Prophethood and Scripture, and among them there is he who is guided, but many of them are Fasiqun (rebellious, disobedient to Allah).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: We sent Noah and Abraham, and established in their line Prophethood and the Scripture. Some of them are guided, but many of them are sinners.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Indeed We sent forth Noah and Abraham and established in their line Prophecy and the Book. Then some of them embraced the guidance and many of them are wicked.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And indeed, We sent Nuh and Ibrahim, and placed in their offspring prophethood and Scripture. And among them there are some who are guided; but many of them are rebellious.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And We verily sent Noah and Abraham and placed the prophethood and the scripture among their seed, and among them there is he who goeth right, but many of them are evil-livers.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Certainly We sent Noah and Abraham and We ordained among their descendants prophethood and the Book. Some of them are [rightly] guided, and many of them are transgressors.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: We sent forth Noah and Abraham, and appointed the Prophethood and the Book to be given to their descendants. Some were guided, but many wereimpious.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And We have already sent Noah and Abraham and placed in their descendants prophethood and scripture; and among them is he who is guided, but many of them are defiantly disobedient.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We sent Noah and Abraham and placed prophethood and the Book among their offsprings, some of whom have the right guidance. However, most of them are evil doers.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And certainly We sent Nuh and Ibrahim and We gave to their offspring the (gift of) prophecy and the Book; so there are among them those who go aright, and most of them are transgressors.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We sent forth Noah and Abraham and bestowed upon their offspring prophethood and the Book. Some of them were rightly guided, but many others were transgressors.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And We sent Noah and Abraham, and established in their line Prophethood and Revelation: and some of them were on right guidance. But many of them became rebellious transgressors.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: These are (some of) those who were favoured by God among the prophets of the progeny of Adam, and of those We bore in the ark with Noah, and the offspring of Abraham and Israel, and of those We guided and We chose, for they bowed weeping in adoration when the revelations of Ar-Rahman were recited to them.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: These are they whom God has blessed among the Prophets of the seed of Adam, and of those We bore with Noah, and of the seed of Abraham and Israel, and of those We guided and chose. When the signs of the All-merciful were recited to them, they fell down prostrate, weeping.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: These are they whom Allah hath favoured, from among the prophets, of the progeny of Adam and of them whom We bare with Nuh, and of the progeny of Ibrahim and Isra'il, and of those whom We have guided and chosen; whenever the revelations of the Compassionate were rehearsed unto them, they fell down prostrating themselves and weeping. *Chapter: 19

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Those were they unto whom Allah bestowed His Grace from among the Prophets, of the offspring of Adam, and of those whom We carried (in the ship) with Nuh (Noah), and of the offspring of Ibrahim (Abraham) and Israel and from among those whom We guided and chose. When the Verses of the Most Beneficent (Allah) were recited unto them, they fell down prostrating and weeping.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: These are some of the prophets God has blessed, from the descendants of Adam, and from those We carried with Noah, and from the descendants of Abraham and Israel, and from those We guided and selected. Whenever the revelations of the Most Gracious are recited to them, they would fall down, prostrating and weeping.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: These are the Prophets upon whom Allah bestowed His favour from the seed of Adam, and from the seed of those whom We carried (in the Ark) with Noah, and from the seed of Abraham and Israel. They were those whom We guided and chose (for an exalted position). They were such that when the words of the Most Compassionate Lord were recited to them, they fell down in prostration, weeping.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Those were they unto whom Allah bestowed His grace from among the Prophets, of the offspring of Adam, and of those whom We carried (in the ship) with Nuh, and of the offspring of Ibrahim and Israel, and from among those whom We guided and chose. When the Ayat of the Most Gracious were recited unto them, they fell down prostrate and weeping.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: These are they unto whom Allah showed favour from among the prophets, of the seed of Adam and of those whom We carried (in the ship) with Noah, and of the seed of Abraham and Israel, and from among those whom We guided and chose. When the revelations of the Beneficent were recited unto them, they fell down, adoring and weeping.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: They are the ones whom Allah has blessed from among the prophets of Adam’s progeny, and from [the progeny of] those We carried with Noah, and from among the progeny of Abraham and Israel, and from among those that We guided and chose. When the signs of the All-beneficent were recited to them, they would fall down weeping in prostration.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: These are whom Allah has blessed among the Prophets from among the seed of Adam and of those whom We bore with Noah; the descendants ofAbraham, of Israel, and of those whom We have guided and chose. For when the verses of the Merciful were recited to them, they fell down prostrate, weeping.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Those were the ones upon whom Allah bestowed favor from among the prophets of the descendants of Adam and of those We carried [in the ship] with Noah, and of the descendants of Abraham and Israel, and of those whom We guided and chose. When the verses of the Most Merciful were recited to them, they fell in prostration and weeping.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: These were the Prophets from the offspring of Adam, from those who embarked with Noah and from the offspring of Abraham and Israel. God guided them and chose them for His favor. Whenever they would hear the revelations of the Beneficent God they would bow down in prostration with tears.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: These are they on whom Allah bestowed favors, from among the prophets of the seed of Adam, and of those whom We carried with Nuh, and of the seed of Ibrahim and Israel, and of those whom We guided and chose; when the communications of the Beneficent Allah were recited to them, they fell down making obeisance and weeping.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: These are the ones whom God has favoured: the prophets from among the descendants of Adam and of those whom We carried in the Ark with Noah; the descendants of Abraham, of Israel, and of those whom We have guided and chosen. For when the revelations of the Merciful were recited to them, they fell down, prostrating themselves and weeping.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Those were some of the prophets on whom Allah did bestow His Grace,- of the posterity of Adam, and of those who We carried (in the Ark) with Noah, and of the posterity of Abraham and Israel of those whom We guided and chose. Whenever the Signs of (Allah) Most Gracious were rehearsed to them, they would fall down in prostrate adoration and in tears.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: SENT NOAH to his people to warn them before the painful punishment came upon them.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: We sent Noah to his people, saying, 'Warn thy people, ere there come on them a painful chastisement.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Verily We! We sent Nuh Unto his people saying: warn thy people ere there come Unto them torment afflictive.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Verily, We sent Nuh (Noah) to his people (Saying): "Warn your people before there comes to them a painful torment."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: We sent Noah to his people: “Warn your people before there comes upon them a painful punishment.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: We sent Noah to his people (and directed him): “Warn your people before a grievous chastisement comes upon them.”

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Verily, We sent Nuh to his people (saying): "Warn your people before there comes to them a painful torment."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Lo! We sent Noah unto his people (saying): Warn thy people ere the painful doom come unto them.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Indeed We sent Noah to his people, [saying,] ‘Warn your people before a painful punishment overtakes them.’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: We sent Noah to his nation, saying: 'Give warning to your nation before a painful punishment overtakes them'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Indeed, We sent Noah to his people, [saying], "Warn your people before there comes to them a painful punishment."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We sent Noah to his people telling him, "Warn your people before a painful torment approaches them".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Surely We sent Nuh to his people, saying: Warn your people before there come upon them a painful chastisement.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We sent Noah forth to his people, saying, "Warn your people before there comes upon them a grievous punishment."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: We sent Noah to his People (with the Command): "Do thou warn thy People before there comes to them a grievous Penalty."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: He said: "O my people, I warn you clearly

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: He said, 'O my people, I am unto you a clear warner,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: He said: O my people! verily I am Unto you a warner manifest.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: He said: "O my people! Verily, I am a plain warner to you,

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He said, “O my people, I am to you a clear warner.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Noah said: “My people, I have certainly been sent as a clear warner to you,

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: He said: "O my people! Verily, I am a plain warner to you,"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: He said: O my people! Lo! I am a plain warner unto you

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: He said, ‘O my people! Indeed, I am a manifest warner to you.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: He said: 'My nation, I am a clear warner for you.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: He said, "O my people, indeed I am to you a clear warner,

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Noah said, "My people, I am warning you plainly.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: He said: O my people! Surely I am a plain warner to you:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: [Noah] said, "My people! I am but a plain warner to you,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: He said: "O my People! I am to you a Warner, clear and open:

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: That you should worship God and fear Him, and follow me

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “That you must worship Allah and fear Him, and obey me.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: saying, "Serve God, and fear Him, and obey you me,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Worship Allah, and fear Him, and obey me.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "That you should worship Allah (Alone), be dutiful to Him, and obey me,

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Worship God and reverence Him, and obey me.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: that you serve Allah and fear Him, and follow me;

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "That you should worship Allah, and have Taqwa of Him, and obey me,"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: (Bidding you): Serve Allah and keep your duty unto Him and obey me,

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Worship Allah, fear Him, and obey me.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: [Saying], 'Worship Allah, fear Him and obey me.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Worship God, have fear of Him and obey me.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: That you should serve Allah and be careful of (your duty to) Him and obey me:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: that you should worship God [alone] and be conscious of Him. Pay heed to me.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "That ye should worship Allah, fear Him and obey me:

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: That He may forgive some of your sins and prolong your term till an appointed time. Surely when God's appointed time is come it will not be put off, if only you knew!

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “He will forgive you some of your sins, and give you respite up to an appointed term; indeed the promise of Allah cannot be averted when it arrives; if only you knew.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and He will forgive you your sins, and defer you to a stated term; God's term, when it comes, cannot be deferred, did you but know."'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: He will forgive you your sins, and will defer you to an appointed term. Verily the term of Allah when it cometh shall not be deferred, if ye but knew.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "He (Allah) will forgive you of your sins and respite you to an appointed term. Verily, the term of Allah when it comes, cannot be delayed, if you but knew."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And He will forgive you of your sins, and reprieve you until a stated term. God’s term cannot be deferred once it has arrived, if you only knew.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: He will forgive your sins and will grant you respite until an appointed term. Indeed when Allah's appointed term comes, it cannot be deferred; if you only knew!”

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "He will forgive you of your sins and respite you to an appointed term. Verily, the term of Allah when it comes, cannot be delayed, if you but know."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: That He may forgive you somewhat of your sins and respite you to an appointed term. Lo! the term of Allah, when it cometh, cannot be delayed, if ye but knew.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: He will forgive you your sins and respite you till an appointed term. When the term of Allah arrives it cannot be deferred; if you but knew'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Allah will forgive you of your sins and delay you for a specified term. Indeed, the time [set by] Allah, when it comes, will not be delayed, if you only knew.' "

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: He will forgive your sins and give you a respite for an appointed time. When the time which God has appointed arrives, none will be able to postpone it. Would that you knew this!"

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: He will forgive you some of your faults and grant you a delay to an appointed term; surely the term of Allah when it comes is not postponed; did you but know!

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: He will forgive your sins and will grant you respite till an appointed time. When the time appointed by God arrives, it cannot be postponed, if you only knew."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "So He may forgive you your sins and give you respite for a stated Term: for when the Term given by Allah is accomplished, it cannot be put forward: if ye only knew."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: We sent Noah to his people, and he said: "O people worship God; you have no other god but He; for I fear the retribution of the great Day may fall on you."

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And We sent Noah to his people; and he said, 'O my people, serve God! You have no god other' than He; truly, I fear for you the chastisement of a dreadful day.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Assuredly We sent Nuh unto his people, and he said: O my people! worship Allah; no god ye have but He; verily I fear for you the torment of a mighty day.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Indeed, We sent Nuh (Noah) to his people and he said: "O my people! Worship Allah! You have no other Ilah (God) but Him. (La ilaha ill-Allah: none has the right to be worshipped but Allah). Certainly, I fear for you the torment of a Great Day!"

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: We sent Noah to his people. He said, “O my people! Worship God; you have no god other than Him. I fear for you the punishment of a tremendous Day.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Indeed We sent forth Noah to his people and he said: 'O my people! Serve Allah, you have no other god than Him Indeed I fear for you the chastisement of an awesome Day.'

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Indeed, We sent Nuh to his people and he said: "O my people! Worship Allah! You have no other god but Him. Certainly, I fear for you the torment of a Great Day!"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: We sent Noah (of old) unto his people, and he said: O my people! Serve Allah. Ye have no other Allah save Him. Lo! I fear for you the retribution of an Awful Day.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Certainly We sent Noah to his people. He said, ‘O my people, worship Allah! You have no other god besides Him. Indeed I fear for you the punishment of a tremendous day.’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: We sent forth Noah to his nation. He said: 'Worship Allah, my people for you have no god except He. I fear for you the punishment of a great Day.'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: We had certainly sent Noah to his people, and he said, "O my people, worship Allah; you have no deity other than Him. Indeed, I fear for you the punishment of a tremendous Day.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We sent Noah to his people. He told them, "Worship God for He is your only Lord. I am afraid of the punishment that you might suffer on the great Day (of Judgment)".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Certainly We sent Nuh to his people, so he said: O my people! serve Allah, you have no god other than Him; surely I fear for you the chastisement of a grievous day.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We sent Noah to his people. He said, "O my people, worship God; you have no other god but He. I fear for you the punishment of a dreadful Day,"

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: We sent Noah to his people. He said: "O my people! worship Allah! ye have no other god but Him. I fear for you the punishment of a dreadful day!

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: We sent Noah to his people (and he said): "I give you a clear warning.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And We sent Noah to his people: 'I am for you a warner, and a bearer of good tidings:

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And assuredly We sent Nuh unto his people saying: verily I am unto you a plain warner.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And indeed We sent Nuh (Noah) to his people (and he said): "I have come to you as a plain warner."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: We sent Noah to his people, “I am to you a clear warner.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: (Such were the circumstances) when We sent forth Noah to his people. (He said): 'I have been sent to you to warn you plainly

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And indeed We sent Nuh to his people (and he said): "I have come to you as a plain warner."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And We sent Noah unto his folk (and he said): Lo! I am a plain warner unto you.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Certainly We sent Noah to his people [to declare]: ‘Indeed I am a manifest warner to you.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: We sent Noah to his nation. (He said:) 'I am a warner for you, and a bearer of glad tidings.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And We had certainly sent Noah to his people, [saying], "Indeed, I am to you a clear warner

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We sent Noah to his people to give them the clear warning

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And certainly We sent Nuh to his people: Surely I am a plain warner for you:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We sent Noah to his people. He said, "I have come to you with a clear warning:

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: We sent Noah to his people (with a mission): "I have come to you with a Clear Warning:

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Do not worship anyone but God; for I fear the punishment of a dreadful day for you."

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Serve you none but God. I fear for you the chastisement of a painful day.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: That ye shall worship none except Allah; verily I fear for you the torment of a Day afflictive.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "That you worship none but Allah, surely, I fear for you the torment of a painful Day."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: “That you shall worship none but God. I fear for you the agony of a painful Day.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: that you may worship none but Allah or else I fear for you the chastisement of a Grievous Day.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "That you worship none but Allah; surely, I fear for you the torment of a painful Day."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: That ye serve none, save Allah. Lo! I fear for you the retribution of a painful Day.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Worship none but Allah. Indeed I fear for you the punishment of a painful day.’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Worship none except Allah. I fear for you the punishment of a painful Day'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: That you not worship except Allah. Indeed, I fear for you the punishment of a painful day."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: that they should not worship anyone besides God. (Noah warned them), "I am afraid that you will suffer the most painful torment".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: That you shall not serve any but Allah, surely I fear for you the punishment of a painful day.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: worship none but God. I fear lest punishment befall you on a woeful Day."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "That ye serve none but Allah: Verily I do fear for you the penalty of a grievous day."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: We sent Noah to his people. He said: "O my people, worship God, for you have no other god but He. Will you not take heed for yourselves?"

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And We sent Noah to his people; and he, said, 'O my people, serve God! You have no god other than He. Will you not be godfearing?"

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And assuredly We sent Nuh unto his people, and he said: O my people! worship Allah; for you there is no god but he; will ye not then fear Him!

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And indeed We sent Nuh (Noah) to his people, and he said: "O my people! Worship Allah! You have no other Ilah (God) but Him (Islamic Monotheism). Will you not then be afraid (of Him i.e. of His Punishment because of worshipping others besides Him)?"

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: We sent Noah to his people. He said, “O my people, worship God, you have no deity other than Him. Will you not take heed?”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: We sent Noah to his people, and he said: "My people! Serve Allah; you have no deity other than He. Do you have no fear?"

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And indeed We sent Nuh to his people, and he said: "O my people! Worship Allah! You have no other God but Him. Will you not then have Taqwa"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And We verily sent Noah unto his folk, and he said: O my people! Serve Allah. Ye have no other Allah save Him. Will ye not ward off (evil)?

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Certainly We sent Noah to his people, and he said, ‘O my people! Worship Allah! You have no other god besides Him. Will you not then be wary [of Him]?’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: We sent Noah to his nation. 'Worship Allah, my nation' he said, 'for you have no god except He. Will you not be cautious'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And We had certainly sent Noah to his people, and he said, "O my people, worship Allah; you have no deity other than Him; then will you not fear Him?"

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We sent Noah to his people who said, "My people, worship God for He is your only Lord. Will you then not have fear of Him?"

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And certainly We sent Nuh to his people, and he said: O my people! serve Allah, you have no god other than Him; will you not then guard (against evil)?

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We sent Noah to his people, and he said, "My people, worship God; you have no other deity except Him. Will you not fear Him?"

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: (Further, We sent a long line of prophets for your instruction). We sent Noah to his people: He said, "O my people! worship Allah! Ye have no other god but Him. Will ye not fear (Him)?"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: When their brother Noah said to them: "Will you not take heed?

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: when their brother Noah said to them, 'Will you not be godfearing?

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: When their brother Nuh said Unto them: fear ye not?

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: When their brother Nuh (Noah) said to them: "Will you not fear Allah and obey Him?

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Their brother Noah said to them, “Do you not fear?

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Recall when their brother Noah said to them: "Do you have no fear?

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: When their brother Nuh said to them: "Will you not have Taqwa"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: When their brother Noah said unto them: Will ye not ward off (evil)?

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: when Noah, their brother, said to them, ‘Will you not be wary [of Allah]?

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: when Noah, their brother, said to them: 'Will you not be cautious?

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: When their brother Noah said to them, "Will you not fear Allah?

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: span class="col">Their brother Noah asked them, "Why do you not fear God?

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: When their brother Nuh said to them: Will you not guard (against evil)?

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: When their brother Noah said to them, "Will you have no fear of God?

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Behold, their brother Noah said to them: "Will ye not fear (Allah)?

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: I have been sent as a trusted messenger to you.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “I am indeed a trustworthy Noble Messenger of Allah to you.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: I am for you a faithful Messenger,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Verily I am unto you an apostle trusted.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "I am a trustworthy Messenger to you.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: I am to you a faithful messenger.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: I am a trustworthy Messenger to you;

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "I am a trustworthy Messenger to you."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Lo! I am a faithful messenger unto you,

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: I am for you an honest Messenger,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Indeed, I am to you a trustworthy messenger.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: I am a trustworthy Messengers sent to you.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Surely I am a faithful apostle to you;

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: I am a trustworthy messenger for you:

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "I am to you a messenger worthy of all trust:

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: We sent Noah to his people, and he lived with them a thousand years minus fifty. Then they were caught by the deluge for they were evil.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Indeed, We sent Noah to his people, and he tarried among them a thousand years, all but fifty; so the Flood seized them, while they were evildoers.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And assuredly We sent Nuh unto his people. Then he tarried among them for a thousand years, save tifty years; and then deluge overtook them, while they were wrong-doers.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And indeed We sent Nuh (Noah) to his people, and he stayed among them a thousand years less fifty years [inviting them to believe in the Oneness of Allah (Monotheism), and discard the false gods and other deities], and the Deluge overtook them while they were Zalimun (wrong-doers, polytheists, disbelievers, etc.).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: We sent Noah to his people, and He stayed among them for a thousand years minus fifty years. Then the Deluge swept them; for they were wrongdoers.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: We did indeed send Noah to his people and he lived among them a thousand years save fifty. Eventually the Flood overtook them while they were engaged in wrongdoing.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And indeed We sent Nuh to his people, and he stayed among them a thousand years less fifty years; so the Deluge overtook them while they were wrongdoers.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And verily we sent Noah (as Our messenger) unto his folk, and he continued with them for a thousand years save fifty years; and the flood engulfed them, for they were wrong-doers.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Certainly We sent Noah to his people, and he remained with them for a thousand-less-fifty years. Then the flood overtook them while they were wrongdoers.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Indeed, We sent Noah to his nation, and he lived amongst them for a thousand years, less fifty (but they belied him), then the Flood seized them while they were harmdoers.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And We certainly sent Noah to his people, and he remained among them a thousand years minus fifty years, and the flood seized them while they were wrongdoers.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We sent Noah to his people and he lived with them for nine hundred and fifty years, then the flood engulfed them for their injustice.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And certainly We sent Nuh to his people, so he remained among them a thousand years save fifty years. And the deluge overtook them, while they were unjust.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We sent Noah to his people and he lived among them for fifty short of a thousand years; then the deluge overtook them, for they were wrongdoers.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: We (once) sent Noah to his people, and he tarried among them a thousand years less fifty: but the Deluge overwhelmed them while they (persisted in) sin.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: He said: "O Lord, I called my people night and day,

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: He said; 'My Lord, I have called my people by night and by day,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: He said: my Lord! verily have called my people night and day.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: He said: "O my Lord! Verily, I have called my people night and day (i.e. secretly and openly to accept the doctrine of Islamic Monotheism).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He said, “My Lord, I have called my people night and day.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: He said: “My Lord, I called my people by night and by day,

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: He said: O my Lord! Verily, I have called to my people night and day,

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: He said: My Lord! Lo! I have called unto my people night and day

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: He said, ‘My Lord! Indeed, I have summoned my people night and day

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: 'My Lord' said he, 'night and day I have called my nation,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: He said, "My Lord, indeed I invited my people [to truth] night and day.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Noah said, "My Lord, I have been preaching to my people, night and day,

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: He said: O my Lord! surely I have called my people by night and by day!

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: He said, "My Lord! I have called my people night and day

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: He said: "O my Lord! I have called to my People night and day:

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: But the more I called, they only ran the farther away.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “So for them, my calling them increased their fleeing away.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: but my calling has only increased them in flight.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And my calling hath only increased them in fleeing.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "But all my calling added nothing but to (their) flight (from the truth).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: But my call added only to their flight.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: but the more I called, the farther they fled.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: But all my calling added nothing but to (their) flight.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: But all my calling doth but add to their repugnance;

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: but my call has only increased them in running away.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: But my invitation increased them not except in flight.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: but it has had no effect on them except to make them run away.

translation.author: Shakir
