translation.text: But my call has only made them flee the more:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: but my pleas have only increased their aversion.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "But my call only increases (their) flight (from the Right).

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And every time I called them that You may forgive them, they thrust their fingers into their ears, and covered themselves with their garments, and became wayward, and behaved with downright insolence.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “And whenever I called them, so that You may forgive them, they always thrust their fingers into their ears, and covered themselves with their clothes, and remained stubborn and were extremely haughty.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And whenever I called them, that Thou mightest forgive them, they put their fingers in their ears, and wrapped them in their garments, and persisted, and waxed very proud.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And verily so oft as I call them, that Thou mayest forgive them, they place their fingers into their ears, and wrap themselves with their garments, and persist, and are stiff-necked.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "And verily! Every time I called unto them that You might forgive them, they thrust their fingers into their ears, covered themselves up with their garments, and persisted (in their refusal), and magnified themselves in pride.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Whenever I called them to Your forgiveness, they thrust their fingers into their ears, and wrapped themselves in their garments, and insisted, and became more and more arrogant.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And every time I called them so that You might forgive them, they thrust their fingers into their ears and wrapped up their faces with their garments and obstinately clung to their attitude, and waxed very proud.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And verily, every time I called unto them that You might forgive them, they thrust their fingers into their ears, covered themselves up with their garments, and persisted, and magnified themselves in pride.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And lo! whenever I call unto them that Thou mayst pardon them they thrust their fingers in their ears and cover themselves with their garments and persist (in their refusal) and magnify themselves in pride.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Each time I called them so that You might forgive them, they thrust their fingers in their ears and wrapped themselves in their garments, and persisted becomingvery proud.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And indeed, every time I invited them that You may forgive them, they put their fingers in their ears, covered themselves with their garments, persisted, and were arrogant with [great] arrogance.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Evert time I invite them to Your (guidance) so that You can forgive them, they put their fingers into their ears, cover their heads with their clothes, persist in their disbelief and display extreme arrogance.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And whenever I have called them that Thou mayest forgive them, they put their fingers in their ears, cover themselves with their garments, and persist and are puffed up with pride:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Every time I have called to them, so that You might forgive them, they have only thrust their fingers into their ears, covered themselves up with their garments, grown obstinate, and given themselves up to arrogance.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "And every time I have called to them, that Thou mightest forgive them, they have (only) thrust their fingers into their ears, covered themselves up with their garments, grown obstinate, and given themselves up to arrogance.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Then I called them loudly and more openly,

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “I then called them openly.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Then indeed I called them openly;

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Then verily have called Unto them publicly:

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "Then verily, I called to them openly (aloud);

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Then I called them openly.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Then I summoned them openly,

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Then verily, I called to them openly (aloud).

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And lo! I have called unto them aloud,

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: I called to them clearly,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Then I invited them publicly.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: "I preached to them aloud, in public.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Then surely I called to them aloud:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Then I called them openly,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "So I have called to them aloud;

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And declared to them in public and in private,

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “Then I also told them publicly and also spoke to them softly in private.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: then indeed I spoke publicly unto them, and I spoke unto them secretly,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Then verily spake Unto them openly, and secretly also I addressed them.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "Then verily, I proclaimed to them in public, and I have appealed to them in private,

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Then I appealed to them publicly, and I spoke to them privately.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: and preached to them in public, and also addressed them in secret.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Then verily, I proclaimed to them in public, and I have appealed to them in private.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And lo! I have made public proclamation unto them, and I have appealed to them in private.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: and indeed, I spoke to them both publicly and in secret:

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Then I announced to them and [also] confided to them secretly

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Then I conveyed the message to them, again, both in public and in private,

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Then surely I spoke to them in public and I spoke to them in secret:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: and spoke to them in public and in private."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "Further I have spoken to them in public and secretly in private,

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And I told them: 'Ask your Lord to forgive you. He is verily forgiving.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “I therefore told them, ‘Seek forgiveness from your Lord; He is indeed Most Forgiving.’

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and I said, "Ask you forgiveness of your Lord; surely He is ever All-forgiving,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And I said: ask forgiveness of your Lord; verily He is ever Most Forgiving.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "I said (to them): 'Ask forgiveness from your Lord; Verily, He is Oft-Forgiving;

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: I said, ‘Ask your Lord for forgiveness; He is Forgiving.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: I said to them: “Ask forgiveness from your Lord; surely He is Most Forgiving.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: I said (to them): Ask forgiveness from your Lord, verily, He is Oft-Forgiving;

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And I have said: Seek pardon of your Lord. Lo! He was ever Forgiving.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: "Ask for forgiveness of your Lord," I said. "Surely, He is the Forgiver.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And said, 'Ask forgiveness of your Lord. Indeed, He is ever a Perpetual Forgiver.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: and told them, "Ask forgiveness from your Lord; He is All-forgiving".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Then I said, Ask forgiveness of your Lord, surely He is the most Forgiving:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Then I said, "Ask forgiveness of your Lord. Surely He is the most forgiving.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "Saying, 'Ask forgiveness from your Lord; for He is Oft-Forgiving;

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: He will send you abundant rain from the sky,

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: ‘He will send down abundant rain for you from the sky.’

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and He will loose heaven upon you in torrents

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: He will send the heaven upon you copiously;

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: 'He will send rain to you in abundance;

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He will let loose the sky upon you in torrents.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: He will shower upon you torrents from heaven,

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: He will send rain to you Midrar,

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: He will let loose the sky for you in plenteous rain,

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: He will let loose the sky upon you in abundance

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: He will send [rain from] the sky upon you in [continuing] showers

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: He will send you abundant

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: He will send down upon you the cloud, pouring down abundance of rain:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: He will send down abundant rain from the sky for you,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "'He will send rain to you in abundance;

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And will give you increase of wealth and sons, and give you gardens and springs of water.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: ‘And will aid you with wealth and sons, and will create gardens for you and cause rivers to flow for you.’

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and will succour you with wealth and sons, and will appoint for you gardens, and will appoint for you rivers.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And He will add Unto you riches and children and will appoint Unto you gardens and will appoint Unto you rivers.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: 'And give you increase in wealth and children, and bestow on you gardens and bestow on you rivers."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And provide you with wealth and children, and allot for you gardens, and allot for you rivers.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: and will provide you with wealth and children, and will bestow upon you gardens and rivers.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And give you increase in wealth and children, and bestow on you gardens and bestow on you rivers.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And will help you with wealth and sons, and will assign unto you Gardens and will assign unto you rivers.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: and give to you wealth and sons, and provide you with gardens and rivers.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And give you increase in wealth and children and provide for you gardens and provide for you rivers.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: rain from the sky,

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And help you with wealth and sons, and make for you gardens, and make for you rivers.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: increase your wealth and sons; and grant you gardens and waterways.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "'Give you increase in wealth and sons; and bestow on you gardens and bestow on you rivers (of flowing water).

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: What has come upon you that you do not fear the majesty of God,

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: ‘What is the matter with you, that you do not desire honour from Allah?’

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: What ails you, that you look not for majesty in God,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: What aileth you that ye expect not in Allah majesty!

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: What is the matter with you, [that you fear not Allah (His punishment), and] you hope not for reward (from Allah or you believe not in His Oneness).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: What is the matter with you, that you do not appreciate God’s Greatness?

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: What is amiss with you that you do not look forward to the majesty of Allah

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: What is the matter with you, that you do not hope for any Waqar from Allah

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: What aileth you that ye hope not toward Allah for dignity

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: What is the matter with you that you do not want the Greatness of Allah,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: What is [the matter] with you that you do not attribute to Allah [due] grandeur

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: strengthen you by (providing) you wealth and children, and make gardens and streams for you. What is the matter with you that you are not afraid of the greatness of God

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: What is the matter with you that you fear not the greatness of Allah?

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: What is the matter with you that you deny the greatness of God,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "'What is the matter with you, that ye place not your hope for kindness and long-suffering in Allah,-

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Knowing that He has created you by various stages?

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: ‘Whereas it is He Who created you in different stages?’

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: seeing He created you by stages?

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And surely He hath created you by stages.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: While He has created you in (different) stages [i.e. first Nutfah, then 'Alaqah and then Mudghah, see (VV. 23:13, 14) the Quran].

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Although He created you in stages.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: when He has created you in stages?

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: While He has created you (in) Atwar (stages).

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: When He created you by (divers) stages?

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: He has created you by stages!

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: While He has created you in stages?

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: who has created you in several stages?

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And indeed He has created you through various grades:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: when He has created you through different stages of existence?

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "'Seeing that it is He that has created you in diverse stages?

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Do you not see how God has fashioned seven skies one above the other,

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: ‘Do you not see how Allah has created the seven heavens atop each other?’

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Have you not regarded how God created seven heavens one upon another,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Behold ye not in what wise Allah hath created seven heavens in storeys.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: See you not how Allah has created the seven heavens one above another,

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Do you not realize that God created seven heavens in layers?

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Do you not see how Allah has created seven heavens, one upon the other,

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: See you not how Allah has created the seven heavens in tiers

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: See ye not how Allah hath created seven heavens in harmony,

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Have you not seen how Allah created the seven heavens one above the other,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Do you not consider how Allah has created seven heavens in layers

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: "Have you not seen that God has created the seven heavens one above the other

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Do you not see how Allah has created the seven heavens, one above another,

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Do you not see how God has created the seven heavens one above another,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "'See ye not how Allah has created the seven heavens one above another,

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And has placed the moon therein, an illumination, and has placed the sun, a lighted lamp?

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: ‘And in them, has illuminated the moon, and made the sun a lamp?’

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and set the moon therein for a light and the sun for a lamp?

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And hath placed the moon therein for a light, and hath made the sun a lamp?

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And has made the moon a light therein, and made the sun a lamp?

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And He set the moon in their midst for light, and He made the sun a lamp.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: and has placed the moon in them as a light, and the sun as a radiant lamp?

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And has made the moon a light therein, and made the sun a lamp

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And hath made the moon a light therein, and made the sun a lamp?

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: setting in them the moon as a light and the sun as a lantern?

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And made the moon therein a [reflected] light and made the sun a burning lamp?

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: and placed therein the moon as a light

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And made the moon therein a light, and made the sun a lamp?

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: and made the moon therein a light, and made the sun a lamp,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "'And made the moon a light in their midst, and made the sun as a (Glorious) Lamp?

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: God produced you from the earth like a vegetable growth;

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: ‘And it is Allah Who made you grow like vegetation from the earth.’

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And God caused you to grow out of the earth,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And Allah hath caused you to grow from the earth as a growth.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And Allah has brought you forth from the (dust of) earth. [Tafsir At-Tabari, Vol. 29, Page 97].

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And God germinated you from the earth like plants.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And Allah has caused you to grow out of the earth so wondrously,

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And Allah has brought you forth from the (dust of) earth

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And Allah hath caused you to grow as a growth from the earth,

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Allah has caused you to grow out of the earth,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And Allah has caused you to grow from the earth a [progressive] growth.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: and the sun as a torch.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And Allah has made you grow out of the earth as a growth:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: how God has produced you from the earth and caused you to grow,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "'And Allah has produced you from the earth growing (gradually),

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: He will then return you back to it, and bring you out again.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: ‘And He will then take you back to it, and again remove you from it.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: then He shall return you into it, and bring you forth.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And thereafter He will cause you to return thereinto, and He will bring you forth a forthbringing.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Afterwards He will return you into it (the earth), and bring you forth (again on the Day of Resurrection)?

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Then He will return you into it, and will bring you out again.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: and He will later cause you to return to it and will then again bring you out of it.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Afterwards He will return you into it (the earth), and bring you forth.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And afterward He maketh you return thereto, and He will bring you forth again, a (new) forthbringing.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: and to it He will return you. Then, He will bring you forth.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Then He will return you into it and extract you [another] extraction.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: God made you grow from the earth. He will make you return to it and then take you out of it again.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Then He returns you to it, then will He bring you forth a (new) bringing forth:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: how He will then return you to it and bring you forth again,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "'And in the End He will return you into the (earth), and raise you forth (again at the Resurrection)?

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: God has made the earth for you a spreading,

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: ‘And it is Allah Who made the earth a bed for you.’

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And God has laid the earth for you as a carpet,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And Allah hath made the earth Unto you an expanse.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And Allah has made for you the earth wide spread (an expanse).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And God made the earth a spread for you.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Allah has made the earth a wide expanse for you

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And Allah has made for you the earth a wide expanse.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And Allah hath made the earth a wide expanse for you

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Allah has made the earth spread out for you

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And Allah has made for you the earth an expanse

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: God has spread out the earth

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And Allah has made for you the earth a wide expanse,

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: how God has spread the earth out for you

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "'And Allah has made the earth for you as a carpet (spread out),

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: So that you may walk upon its spacious paths.'

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: ‘So that you may tread the wide roads in it.’”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: that thereof you may thread ways, ravines."

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: That of it ye may traverse the ways open.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: That you may go about therein in broad roads.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: That you may travel its diverse roads.’”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: so that you may tread its spacious paths.”

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: That you may go about therein in broad roads.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: That ye may thread the valley-ways thereof.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: so that you walk in its spacious paths.'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: That you may follow therein roads of passage.' "

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: for you, so that you may walk along its wide roads".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: That you may go along therein in wide paths.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: so that you may walk along its spacious paths?"

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "'That ye may go about therein, in spacious roads.'"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: He said: "O my people, think. If I have a clear proof from my Lord, and He has bestowed on me His grace, though unknown to you, can we force it upon you when you are averse?

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: He said, 'O my people, what think you? If I stand upon a clear sign from my Lord, and He has given me mercy from Him, and it has been obscured for you, shall we compel you to it while you are averse to it?

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: He said: bethink ye, O my people! if I rested upon an evidence from my Lord, and a mercy hath come unto me from Him, and that hath been obscured unto you, Shall we make you adhere to it while ye are averse thereto?

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: He said: "O my people! Tell me, if I have a clear proof from my Lord, and a Mercy (Prophethood, etc.) has come to me from Him, but that (Mercy) has been obscured from your sight. Shall we compel you to accept it (Islamic Monotheism) when you have a strong hatred for it?

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He said, “O my people, Have you considered? If I stand on clear evidence from my Lord, and He has given me a mercy from Himself, but you were blind to it, can we compel you to accept it, even though you dislike it?”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Noah said: 'My people! If I base myself on a clear evidence from my Lord, and I have also been blessed by His mercy to which you have been blind, how can we force it upon you despite your aversion to it?

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: He said: "O my people! Tell me, if I have a clear proof from my Lord, and a mercy (prophethood) has come to me from Him, but that (mercy) has been obscured from your sight. Shall we compel you (to accept) it when you have a strong hatred for it

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: He said: O my people! Bethink you, if I rely on a clear proof from my Lord and there hath come unto me a mercy from His presence, and it hath been made obscure to you, can we compel you to accept it when ye are averse thereto?

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: He said, ‘O my people! Tell me, should I stand on a manifest proof from my Lord, and He has granted me His own mercy—though it should be invisible to you—shall we force it upon you while you are averse to it?

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: He said: 'What do you think my nation! If I have a clear proof from my Lord and He has given me mercy from Him, though it is hidden from you, can wecompel you to accept it when you hate it?

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: He said, "O my people have you considered: if I should be upon clear evidence from my Lord while He has given me mercy from Himself but it has been made unapparent to you, should we force it upon you while you are averse to it?

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Noah replied, "My people do you think - that if my Lord has sent me a miracle and granted me mercy but your ignorance has obscured them from your sight - we can force you to believe when you do not want to?

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: He said: O my people! tell me if I have with me clear proof from my Lord, and He has granted me mercy from Himself and it has been made obscure to you; shall we constrain you to (accept) it while you are averse from it?

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: He said, "O my people, tell me: if I have clear evidence from my Lord and He has favoured me with grace from Himself, which you have been unable to recognize, can we force it on you against your will?

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: He said: "O my people! See ye if (it be that) I have a Clear Sign from my Lord, and that He hath sent Mercy unto me from His own presence, but that the Mercy hath been obscured from your sight? shall we compel you to accept it when ye are averse to it?

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: I do not demand for it any wealth from you, O my people. My reward is with God. And I will not drive those away who believe. They have also to meet their Lord. But I see you are an ignorant people.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “And O my people! I do not ask any wealth from you for it; my reward is only upon Allah, and I am not going to repel the Muslims; indeed they will meet their Lord, but I find you people completely ignorant.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: O my people, I do not ask of you wealth for this; my wage falls only upon God. I will not drive away those who believe; they shall surely meet their Lord. But I see you are an ignorant people.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And, O my people, I ask not of you any riches therefor; my hire is not but with Allah. And I am not going to drive away those who have believed; verily they are going to meet their Lord; but I behold you a people ignorant!

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "And O my people! I ask of you no wealth for it, my reward is from none but Allah. I am not going to drive away those who have believed. Surely, they are going to meet their Lord, but I see that you are a people that are ignorant.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: “O my people! I ask of you no money for it. My reward lies only with God. And I am not about to dismiss those who believed; they will surely meet their Lord. And I see that you are ignorant people.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: My people! I seek no recompense from you. My recompense is only with Allah. Nor will I drive away those who believe. They are destined to meet their Lord. But I find you to be an ignorant people.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And O my people! I ask of you no wealth for it, my reward is from none but Allah. I am not going to drive away those who have believed. Surely, they are going to meet their Lord, but I see that you are a people that are ignorant.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And O my people! I ask of you no wealth therefor. My reward is the concern only of Allah, and I am not going to thrust away those who believe - Lo! they have to meet their Lord! - but I see you a folk that are ignorant.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: My nation, for this I do not ask you for your wealth for my wage is only with Allah. Nor will I drive away the believers, for they will surely meet their Lord. But, I can see that you are ignorant.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And O my people, I ask not of you for it any wealth. My reward is not but from Allah. And I am not one to drive away those who have believed. Indeed, they will meet their Lord, but I see that you are a people behaving ignorantly.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: My people I do not ask any payment for what I preach to you. No one except God has to give me any reward. I do not drive away those who have faith (in my teaching); they will all receive mercy from their Lord. I know that you are ignorant people.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And, O my people! I ask you not for wealth in return for it; my reward is only with Allah and I am not going to drive away those who believe; surely they shall meet their Lord, but I consider you a people who are ignorant:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: O my people, I do not ask you for any money for this; my reward comes only from God. I will not drive away those who believe; they shall surely meet their Lord. Yet I see that you are a people who act out of ignorance.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "And O my people! I ask you for no wealth in return: my reward is from none but Allah: But I will not drive away (in contempt) those who believe: for verily they are to meet their Lord, and ye I see are the ignorant ones!

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: "I have not gone astray, O my people," he said, "but have been sent by my Lord, the creator of all the worlds.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Said he, 'My people, there is no error in me; but I am a Messenger from the Lord of all Being.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: He said: O my people! not with me is the error, but I am an apostle from the Lord of the worlds.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: [Nuh (Noah)] said: "O my people! There is no error in me, but I am a Messenger from the Lord of the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns and all that exists)!

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He said, “O my people, I am not in error, but I am a messenger from the Lord of the Worlds.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: He said: 'O my people! There is no error in me, but I am a Messenger from the Lord of the universe.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: [Nuh] said: "O my people! There is no error in me, but I am a Messenger from the Lord of all that exists!

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: He said: O my people! There is no error in me, but I am a messenger from the Lord of the Worlds.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: He said, ‘O my people, I am not in error. Rather, I am an apostle from the Lord of all the worlds.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: 'I am not in error, my nation' he replied. 'I am a Messenger from the Lord of the Worlds

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: [Noah] said, "O my people, there is not error in me, but I am a messenger from the Lord of the worlds."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: (Noah) said, "My people, I am not in any error, rather I am a Messenger from the Lord of the Universe,

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: He said: O my people! there is no error in me, but I am an apostle from the Lord of the worlds.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Said [Noah], "O my people! I am not in error. Indeed, I am a messenger from the Lord of the Worlds,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: He said: "O my people! No wandering is there in my (mind): on the contrary I am a messenger from the Lord and Cherisher of the worlds!

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: I bring to you the messages of my Lord, and give you sincere advice, for I know from God what you do not know.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “Conveying to you the messages of my Lord and wishing good for you, and I know from Allah what you do not know.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: I deliver to you the Messages of my Lord, and I advise you sincerely; for I know from God that you know not.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: I preach unto you the messages of my Lord and I councel you good, and I know from Allah which ye know not.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "I convey unto you the Messages of my Lord and give sincere advice to you. And I know from Allah what you know not.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: “I deliver to you the messages of my Lord, and I advise you, and I know from God what you do not know.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: I convey to you the messages of my Lord, give you sincere advice, and I know from Allah that which you do not know.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: I convey unto you the Messages of my Lord and give sincere advice to you. And I know from Allah what you know not."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: I convey unto you the messages of my Lord and give good counsel unto you, and know from Allah that which ye know not.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: (sent) to convey to you the messages of my Lord and to advise you, for I know from Allah what you do not know.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: I convey to you the messages of my Lord and advise you; and I know from Allah what you do not know.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: sent to preach to you the message of my Lord and to give you good advice. I know what you do not know about God.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: I deliver to you the messages of my Lord, and I offer you good advice and I know from Allah what you do not know.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: I am conveying my Lord's messages to you and giving you sincere advice. I know things from God that you do not.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "I but fulfil towards you the duties of my Lord's mission: Sincere is my advice to you, and I know from Allah something that ye know not.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: So be fearful of God and listen to me.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “Therefore fear Allah, and obey me.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: so serve you God, and obey you me.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: So fear Allah and obey me.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "So fear Allah, keep your duty to Him, and obey me.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: So fear God, and obey me.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: so fear Allah and obey me.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "So have Taqwa of Allah, and obey me."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: So keep your duty to Allah, and obey me.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: so fear Allah, and obey me.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: So fear Allah and obey me.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Have fear of God and obey me.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Therefore guard against (the punishment of) Allah and obey me

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: fear God, and obey me.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "So fear Allah, and obey me.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: I ask no recompense of you for it. My reward is due from none but the Lord of all the worlds.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “And I do not ask from you any fee for it; my reward is only upon the Lord Of The Creation.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: I ask of you no wage for this; my wage falls only upon the Lord of all Being;

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And I ask of you no hire therefor; my hire is but with the Lord of the worlds.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "No reward do I ask of you for it (my Message of Islamic Monotheism), my reward is only from the Lord of the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns and all that exists).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: I ask of you no payment for this. My payment is only from the Lord of the Worlds.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: I seek of you no reward for this: my reward is with none except the Lord of the Universe.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "No reward do I ask of you for it; my reward is only from the Lord of all that exists."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And I ask of you no wage therefor; my wage is the concern only of the Lord of the Worlds.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: For this I ask of you no wage, for my wage falls only on the Lord of the Worlds.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And I do not ask you for it any payment. My payment is only from the Lord of the worlds.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: I ask no payment from you for my preaching. The Lord of the Universe will give me my reward.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And I do not ask you any reward for it; my reward is only with the Lord of the worlds:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: I ask of you no recompense for it, for my only reward is with the Lord of the Universe,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "No reward do I ask of you for it: my reward is only from the Lord of the Worlds:

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: So fear God and follow me."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “Therefore fear Allah, and obey me.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: so fear you God, and obey you me.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: So fear Allah and obey me.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "So keep your duty to Allah, fear Him and obey me."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: So fear God, and obey me.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: So fear Allah and obey me."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "So have Taqwa of Allah, and obey me."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: So keep your duty to Allah, and obey me.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: So fear Allah and obey me'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: So fear Allah and obey me."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Have fear of God and obey me."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: So guard against (the punishment of) Allah and obey me.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: so fear God and obey me."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "So fear Allah, and obey me."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: The leaders of the people who were unbelievers, replied: "We see that you are but a man like us, and see that none among us follows you but the meanest and immature of judgement, and do not see any excellence in you above us. In fact, we think you are a liar."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: The chiefs of his people, who were disbelievers, said, “We see that you are just a mortal like us, and we do not see anyone following you except the most lowly among us, without insight; and we do not find any merit in you above us – in fact, we consider you liars.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Said the Council of the unbelievers of his people, 'We see thee not other than a mortal like ourselves, and we see no? any following thee but the vilest of us, inconsiderately. We do not see you have over us any superiority; no, rather we think you are liars.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: The chiefs of those who disbelieved among his people said: we behold thee except as a human being like us, and we behold not any follow thee except the meanest of us, by an immature opinion; nor We behold in you any exellency over us; nay! we deem you liars.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: The chiefs of the disbelievers among his people said: "We see you but a man like ourselves, nor do we see any follow you but the meanest among us and they (too) followed you without thinking. And we do not see in you any merit above us, in fact we think you are liars."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: The notables who disbelieved among his people said, “We see in you nothing but a man like us, and we see that only the worst among us have followed you, those of immature judgment. And we see that you have no advantage over us. In fact, we think you are liars.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: The notables among Noah's own people, who had refused to follow him, responded: 'We merely consider you a human being like ourselves. Nor do we find among those who follow you except the lowliest of our folk, the men who follow you without any proper reason. We see nothing in you to suggest that you are any better than us. Rather, we believe you to be liars.'

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: The chiefs who disbelieved among his people said: "We see you but a man like ourselves, nor do we see any follow you but the meanest among us and they (too) followed you without thinking. And we do not see in you any merit above us, in fact we think you are liars."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: The chieftains of his folk, who disbelieved, said: We see thee but a mortal like us, and we see not that any follow thee save the most abject among us, without reflection. We behold in you no merit above us - nay, we deem you liars.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: The unbelieving Council of his nation said: 'We do not see you other than a human like ourselves. We see your followers are none but the lowliest amongst us, and their opinion is not to be considered. We do not see you superior to us, rather, we consider you liars'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: So the eminent among those who disbelieved from his people said, "We do not see you but as a man like ourselves, and we do not see you followed except by those who are the lowest of us [and] at first suggestion. And we do not see in you over us any merit; rather, we think you are liars."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: The disbelievers among his people said, "We do not believe that you are any better than the rest of us; we see that only the worthless hasty ones, the lowliest among us follow you. Thus, we do not think that you are superior to us, rather you are all liars."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: But the chiefs of those who disbelieved from among his people said: We do not consider you but a mortal like ourselves, and we do not see any have followed you but those who are the meanest of us at first thought and we do not see in you any excellence over us; nay, we deem you liars.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: The leaders of his people, who refused to acknowledge the truth, said, "We regard you only as a human being like ourselves. We do not see that anyone follows you but the lowliest of us, those of immature judgement. We see no superior merit in you; in fact we believe you are a liar."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: But the chiefs of the Unbelievers among his people said: "We see (in) thee nothing but a man like ourselves: Nor do we see that any follow thee but the meanest among us, in judgment immature: Nor do we see in you (all) any merit above us: in fact we think ye are liars!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: O my people, who will save me from God if I drive them away? Do you not understand?

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “And O my people! Who would rescue me from Allah if I repel them? So do you not think?”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: O my people, who would help me against God, if I drive you away?

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: O My people! who will succour me against Allah, if I drave them away! Admonished are ye not then?

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "And O my people! Who will help me against Allah, if I drove them away? Will you not then give a thought?

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: “O my people! Who will support me against God, if I dismiss them? Will you not give a thought?”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: My people! Were I to drive the men of faith away, who will protect me from (the chastisement of) Allah? Do you not understand even this much?

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And O my people! Who will help me against Allah, if I drove them away Will you not then give a thought

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And, O my people! who would deliver me from Allah if I thrust them away? Will ye not then reflect?

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Were I to drive you away my nation, who would help me from Allah? Will you not remember?

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And O my people, who would protect me from Allah if I drove them away? Then will you not be reminded?

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: My people, who would protect me against God if I were to drive these people away? Will you then not take heed?

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And, O my people! who will help me against Allah if I drive them away? Will you not then mind?

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: My people, who would support me against God if I were to drive them off? Will you not take heed?

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "And O my people! who would help me against Allah if I drove them away? Will ye not then take heed?

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: I say not that I have the treasures of God, or that I possess the knowledge of the unknown. I do not claim to be an angel, nor can I say that God will not bestow any good on those you disdain, for God is cognisant of what is in their hearts. If I say this, I will surely be unjust."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “And I do not say to you, ‘I have the treasures of Allah’ nor that ‘I gain knowledge of the hidden’, nor do I say that, ‘I am an angel’ nor do I say to those whom your sights consider lowly, that Allah will never give them any good; Allah well knows what is in their hearts; if I do so, I would then be of the unjust.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Will you not remember? I do not say to you, "I possess the treasuries of God"; I know not the Unseen; and I do not say, "I am an angel." Nor do I say to those your eyes despise, "God will not give them any good"; God knows best what is in their hearts. 'Surely in that case I should be among the evildoers.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And I say not unto you that with me are the treasures of Allah, nor that I know the Unseen; nor I say, I am an angel. And say not of those whom your eyes condemn that Allah shall not bestow on them good - -Allah knoweth best that which is in theeir souls. Verily in that case would be of the wrong-doers.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "And I do not say to you that with me are the Treasures of Allah, "Nor that I know the Ghaib (unseen); "nor do I say I am an angel, and I do not say of those whom your eyes look down upon that Allah will not bestow any good on them. Allah knows what is in their inner-selves (as regards belief, etc.). In that case, I should, indeed be one of the Zalimun (wrong-doers, oppressors, etc.)."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: “I do not say to you that I possess the treasures of God, nor do I know the future, nor do I say that I am an angel. Nor do I say of those who are despicable in your eyes that God will never give them any good. God is Aware of what lies in their souls. If I did, I would be one of the wrongdoers.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: I do not say to you that I possess Allah's treasures, nor that I have access to the realm beyond the ken of sense-perception, nor do I claim to be an angel. Nor do I say regarding those whom you look upon with disdain that Allah will not bestow any good upon them. Allah knows best what is in their hearts. Were I to say so I would be one of the wrongdoers.'

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And I do not say to you that with me are the treasures of Allah, nor that I know the Ghayb; nor do I say I am an angel, and I do not say of those whom your eyes look down upon that Allah will not bestow any good on them. Allah knows what is in their inner selves. In that case, I should, indeed be one of the wrongdoers.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: I say not unto you: "I have the treasures of Allah" nor "I have knowledge of the Unseen," nor say I: "Lo! I am an angel!" Nor say I unto those whom your eyes scorn that Allah will not give them good - Allah knoweth best what is in their hearts - Lo! then indeed I should be of the wrong-doers.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: I do not say to you that I possess the treasuries of Allah, and I do not know the unseen. I do not say I am an angel, nor do I say to those whom you despise, Allah will not give them any good. Allah knows best what is in their hearts. Indeed, if this were so then I would be amongst the harmdoers'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And I do not tell you that I have the depositories [containing the provision] of Allah or that I know the unseen, nor do I tell you that I am an angel, nor do I say of those upon whom your eyes look down that Allah will never grant them any good. Allah is most knowing of what is within their souls. Indeed, I would then be among the wrongdoers."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: I do not say that God's treasures belong to me, that I know the unseen, or that I am an angel. Nor do I say about those whom you disdain that God will not give them any reward. God knows best what is in their hearts, for then I would be unjust."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And I do not say to you that I have the treasures of Allah and I do not know the unseen, nor do I say that I am an angel, nor do I say about those whom your eyes hold in mean estimation (that) Allah will never grant them (any) good-- Allah knows best what is in their souls-- for then most surely I should be of the unjust.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: I do not say to you that I possess God's treasures, or that I have knowledge of the unseen, or that I am an angel. Nor do I say concerning those upon whom you look with contempt, that God will not bestow any good upon them -- God knows best what is in their hearts. If I did, I would certainly be one of the wrongdoers."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "I tell you not that with me are the treasures of Allah, nor do I know what is hidden, nor claim I to be an angel. Nor yet do I say, of those whom your eyes do despise that Allah will not grant them (all) that is good: Allah knoweth best what is in their souls: I should, if I did, indeed be a wrong-doer."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: They said: "O Noah, you have argued with us, and disputed at length; so bring that (retribution) you promise, if you speak the truth."

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: They said, 'Noah, thou hast disputed with us and make much disputation with us. Then bring us that thou promisest us, if thou speakest truly.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: They said: O Nuh! surely thou hast disputed with us and hath multiplied the disputation with us; now bring us that wherewith thou threatenest us, if thou be of the truth-tellers.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: They said: "O Nuh (Noah)! You have disputed with us and much have you prolonged the dispute with us, now bring upon us what you threaten us with, if you are of the truthful."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: They said, “O Noah, you have argued with us, and argued a great deal. Now bring upon us what you threaten us with, if you are truthful.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: They said: 'O Noah! Surely you have disputed with us and have prolonged your dispute. Now bring upon us the chastisement that you threaten us with; do so, if you are truthful.'

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: They said: "O Nuh! You have disputed with us, and much have you prolonged the dispute with us, now bring upon us what you threaten us with, if you are of the truthful."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: They said: O Noah! Thou hast disputed with us and multiplied disputation with us; now bring upon us that wherewith thou threatenest us, if thou art of the truthful.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: They said, ‘O Noah, you have disputed with us already, and you have disputed with us exceedingly. Now bring us what you threaten us with, should you be truthful.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: They said: 'O Noah, you have disputed, and disputed too much, with us. Bring (down) upon us that which you promised us, if what you say is true'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: They said, "O Noah, you have disputed us and been frequent in dispute of us. So bring us what you threaten us, if you should be of the truthful."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: They said, "Noah, you have argued with us a great deal. Bring down on us whatever torment with which you have been threatening us if what you say is true".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: They said: O Nuh! indeed you have disputed with us and lengthened dispute with us, therefore bring to us what you threaten us with, if you are of the truthful ones.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: "Noah," they replied, "you have argued with us, and argued to excess. Now bring down upon us what you threaten us with, if you speak the truth!?"

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: They said: "O Noah! thou hast disputed with us, and (much) hast thou prolonged the dispute with us: now bring upon us what thou threatenest us with, if thou speakest the truth!?"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: But the more I called, they only ran the farther away.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “So for them, my calling them increased their fleeing away.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: but my calling has only increased them in flight.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And my calling hath only increased them in fleeing.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "But all my calling added nothing but to (their) flight (from the truth).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: But my call added only to their flight.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: but the more I called, the farther they fled.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: But all my calling added nothing but to (their) flight.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: But all my calling doth but add to their repugnance;

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: but my call has only increased them in running away.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: But my invitation increased them not except in flight.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: but it has had no effect on them except to make them run away.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: But my call has only made them flee the more:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: but my pleas have only increased their aversion.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "But my call only increases (their) flight (from the Right).

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And every time I called them that You may forgive them, they thrust their fingers into their ears, and covered themselves with their garments, and became wayward, and behaved with downright insolence.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “And whenever I called them, so that You may forgive them, they always thrust their fingers into their ears, and covered themselves with their clothes, and remained stubborn and were extremely haughty.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And whenever I called them, that Thou mightest forgive them, they put their fingers in their ears, and wrapped them in their garments, and persisted, and waxed very proud.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And verily so oft as I call them, that Thou mayest forgive them, they place their fingers into their ears, and wrap themselves with their garments, and persist, and are stiff-necked.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "And verily! Every time I called unto them that You might forgive them, they thrust their fingers into their ears, covered themselves up with their garments, and persisted (in their refusal), and magnified themselves in pride.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Whenever I called them to Your forgiveness, they thrust their fingers into their ears, and wrapped themselves in their garments, and insisted, and became more and more arrogant.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And every time I called them so that You might forgive them, they thrust their fingers into their ears and wrapped up their faces with their garments and obstinately clung to their attitude, and waxed very proud.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And verily, every time I called unto them that You might forgive them, they thrust their fingers into their ears, covered themselves up with their garments, and persisted, and magnified themselves in pride.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And lo! whenever I call unto them that Thou mayst pardon them they thrust their fingers in their ears and cover themselves with their garments and persist (in their refusal) and magnify themselves in pride.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Each time I called them so that You might forgive them, they thrust their fingers in their ears and wrapped themselves in their garments, and persisted becomingvery proud.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And indeed, every time I invited them that You may forgive them, they put their fingers in their ears, covered themselves with their garments, persisted, and were arrogant with [great] arrogance.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Evert time I invite them to Your (guidance) so that You can forgive them, they put their fingers into their ears, cover their heads with their clothes, persist in their disbelief and display extreme arrogance.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And whenever I have called them that Thou mayest forgive them, they put their fingers in their ears, cover themselves with their garments, and persist and are puffed up with pride:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Every time I have called to them, so that You might forgive them, they have only thrust their fingers into their ears, covered themselves up with their garments, grown obstinate, and given themselves up to arrogance.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "And every time I have called to them, that Thou mightest forgive them, they have (only) thrust their fingers into their ears, covered themselves up with their garments, grown obstinate, and given themselves up to arrogance.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: The chiefs of his people, who did not believe, said: "He is only a man like you. He wishes to acquire ascendancy over you. If God had willed He would have sent down angels. We never heard this from our elders.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: So the disbelieving chieftains of his people said, “He is just a human like you, he wishes to become your leader; and had Allah willed, He would have sent down angels; We did not hear this in the case of our forefathers.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Said the Council of the unbelievers of his people, 'This is naught but a mortal like yourselves, who desires to gain superiority over you. And if God willed, He would have sent down angels. We never heard of this among, our fathers, the ancients.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Then said the chiefs of those who disbelieved among his people: this is no other than a human being like unto you, he seeketh to make himself superior unto; and if God had willed, He would save sent down angels; we have not heard of this among our fathers ancient.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: But the chiefs of those who disbelieved among his people said: "He is no more than a human being like you, he seeks to make himself superior to you. Had Allah willed, He surely could have sent down angels; never did we hear such a thing among our fathers of old.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: But the notables of his people, who disbelieved, said, “This is nothing but a human like you, who wants to gain superiority over you. Had God willed, He would have sent down angels. We never heard of this from our forefathers of old.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: But the notables among his people had refused to believe, and said: "This is none other than a mortal like yourselves who desires to attain superiority over you. Had Allah wanted (to send any Messengers) He would have sent down angels. We have heard nothing like this in the time of our forebears of old (that humans were sent as Messengers).

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: But the chiefs of his people who disbelieved said: "He is no more than a human being like you, he seeks to make himself superior to you. Had Allah willed, He surely could have sent down angels. Never did we hear such a thing among our fathers of old."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: But the chieftains of his folk, who disbelieved, said: This is only a mortal like you who would make himself superior to you. Had Allah willed, He surely could have sent down angels. We heard not of this in the case of our fathers of old.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: The unbelieving assembly of his nation said: 'This is but a human like you, who desires to gain superiority over you. Had Allah willed, He could have sent down angels. We never heard of this among our ancient forefathers.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: But the eminent among those who disbelieved from his people said, "This is not but a man like yourselves who wishes to take precedence over you; and if Allah had willed [to send a messenger], He would have sent down angels. We have not heard of this among our forefathers.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: The chiefs of the unbelievers said to the others, "He is a mere mortal like you. He wants only to be superior to you. Had God wanted He would have sent the angels (instead of him). We have never heard from our fathers anything like what he says.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And the chiefs of those who disbelieved from among his people said: He is nothing but a mortal like yourselves who desires that he may have superiority over you, and if Allah had pleased, He could certainly have sent down angels. We have not heard of this among our fathers of yore:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: The leaders of his people who denied the truth, said, "He is only a human being like yourselves who wants to make himself superior to you. If God had wished, He would have sent down angels. We never heard about this from our forefathers.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: The chiefs of the Unbelievers among his people said: "He is no more than a man like yourselves: his wish is to assert his superiority over you: if Allah had wished (to send messengers), He could have sent down angels; never did we hear such a thing (as he says), among our ancestors of old."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: He is only a man possessed; so wait and watch him for a time."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “He is not but a man insane, therefore wait for some time.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: He is naught but a man bedevilled; so wait on him for a time.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: He is only a man in whom is madness wherefore await for him for a season.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "He is only a man in whom is madness, so wait for him a while."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He is nothing but a man possessed. Just ignore him for a while.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: He is a person who has been seized with a little madness; so wait for a while (perhaps he will improve)."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "He is only a man in whom is madness, so wait for him a while."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: He is only a man in whom is a madness, so watch him for a while.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: He is nothing else except a man who is mad, so watch him for awhile.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: He is not but a man possessed with madness, so wait concerning him for a time."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: He is only an insane person. Wait for some time. Perhaps he will come to his senses."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: He is only a madman, so bear with him for a time.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: He is only a madman, so, as far as he is concerned wait for a while."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: (And some said): "He is only a man possessed: wait (and have patience) with him for a time."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: The people of Noah had denied before them, and had called Our votary a liar, and said: "He is possessed," and repulsed him.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: The people of Noah cried lies before them; they cried lies to Our servant, and said, 'A man possessed!' And he was rejected.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: There belied before them the people of Nuh. So they belied Our bondman Nuh and said: `a madman;' and moreover he was reproven.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: The people of Nuh (Noah) denied (their Messenger) before them, they rejected Our slave, and said: "A madman!" and he was insolently rebuked and threatened.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Before them the people of Noah disbelieved. They rejected Our servant, and said, “Crazy,” and he was rebuked.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Before them Noah's people also gave the lie (to his Message). They rejected Our servant as a liar, saying: “He is a madman”; and he was rebuffed.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: The people of Nuh denied (their Messenger) before them. They rejected Our servant and said: "A madman!" Wazdujir.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: The folk of Noah denied before them, yea, they denied Our slave and said: A madman; and he was repulsed.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: The people of Noah impugned before them. So they impugned Our servant and said, ‘A crazy man,’ and he was reviled.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: (Long) before them the nation of Noah belied. They belied Our worshiper saying: 'Mad' and he was reprimanded.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: The people of Noah denied before them, and they denied Our servant and said, "A madman," and he was repelled.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: The people of Noah, who lived before them, had also rejected (Our guidance). They rejected Our servant and said, "He is an insane person so let us drive him away".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Before them the people of Nuh rejected, so they rejected Our servant and called (him) mad, and he was driven away.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: The people of Noah denied [the truth] before them. They belied Our messenger saying, "He is mad!" He was rebuffed,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Before them the People of Noah rejected (their messenger): they rejected Our servant, and said, "Here is one possessed!", and he was driven out.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: They said: "Should we place our trust in you when only the condemned follow you?"

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: They said, “Shall we believe in you, whereas the abject people are with you?”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: They said, 'Shall we believe thee, whom the vilest follow?'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: They said: shall We believe in thee when the meanest follow thee?

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: They said: "Shall we believe in you, when the meanest (of the people) follow you?"

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: They said, “Shall we believe in you, when it is the lowliest who follow you?”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: They answered: "Shall we accept you even though it is the meanest of people who follow you?"

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: They said: "Shall we believe in you, when the inferior follow you"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: They said: Shall we put faith in thee, when the lowest (of the people) follow thee?

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: They replied: 'Are we to believe you whom the lowliest follow'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: They said, "Should we believe you while you are followed by the lowest [class of people]?"

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: They said, "Should we believe in you when no one has followed you except the lowest ones".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: They said: Shall we believe in you while the meanest follow you?

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: They replied, "Are we to believe in you when your followers are but the lowest of the low?"

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: They said: "Shall we believe in thee when it is the meanest that follow thee?"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: He said: "It is not for me to know what they were doing.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: He said, 'What knowledge have I of that they have been doing?

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: He said: I have no knowledge of that which they have been Working.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: He said: "And what knowledge have I of what they used to do?

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He said, “What do I know about what they do?

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Noah said: "What knowledge do I have about their deeds?

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: He said: "And what knowledge have I of what they used to do"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: He said: And what knowledge have I of what they may have been doing (in the past)?

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: He said, ‘What do I know as to what they used to do?

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: He said: 'I have no knowledge of what they have done.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: He said, "And what is my knowledge of what they used to do?

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: (Noah) said, "I have no knowledge of their deeds.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: He said: And what knowledge have I of what they do?

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: He said, "What knowledge do I have of their doings?

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: He said: "And what do I know as to what they do?

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: They said: "If you do not desist, O Noah, you will be stoned to death."

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: They said, 'If thou givest not over, Noah, thou shalt assuredly be one of the stoned.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: They said: if thou desistest not, thou shalt surely be of those stoned.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: They said: "If you cease not, O Nuh (Noah)! You will surely be among those stoned (to death)."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: They said, “If you do not refrain, O Noah, you will be stoned.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: They said: "O Noah! If you do not desist, you will certainly become one of the accursed."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: They said: "If you cease not, O Nuh you will surely be among those stoned."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: They said: If thou cease not, O Noah, thou wilt surely be among those stoned (to death).

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: They said, ‘Noah, if you do not desist, you will certainly be stoned [to death].’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: 'Noah' they replied, 'if you do not desist you shall be of those stoned'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: They said, "If you do not desist, O Noah, you will surely be of those who are stoned."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: They said, "Noah, if you do not desist, you will, certainly, be stoned to death."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: They said: If you desist not, O Nuh, you shall most certainly be of those stoned to death.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: They said, "Noah, if you do not desist, you will be stoned."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: They said: "If thou desist not, O Noah! thou shalt be stoned (to death)."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: But they did not listen to me," Noah said, "and followed him whose wealth and children only added to his ruin.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Noah said, 'My Lord, they have rebelled against me, and followed him whose wealth and children increase him only in loss,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Nuh said. my Lord! verily they have disobeyed me and followed them whose riches and children have only increased them in loss.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
