translation.text: Nuh (Noah) said: "My Lord! They have disobeyed me, and followed one whose wealth and children give him no increase but only loss.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Noah said, “My Lord, they have defied me, and followed him whose wealth and children increase him only in perdition.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Noah said: “My Lord, they did not pay heed to what I said, and followed those (nobles) whose possession of wealth and children has led them to an even greater loss.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Nuh said: "My Lord! They have disobeyed me, and followed one whose wealth and children give him no increase but loss."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Noah said: My Lord! Lo! they have disobeyed me and followed one whose wealth and children increase him in naught save ruin;

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Noah said, ‘My Lord! They have disobeyed me, following someone whose wealth and children only add to his loss,

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Noah said: 'My Lord, they have rebelled against me and followed he whose wealth and offspring increase him only in loss,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Noah said, "My Lord, indeed they have disobeyed me and followed him whose wealth and children will not increase him except in loss.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Noah said, "Lord, they have disobeyed me and followed those whose wealth and children will only bring about destruction for them.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Nuh said: My Lord! surely they have disobeyed me and followed him whose wealth and children have added to him nothing but loss.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Noah supplicated, "Lord, they have disobeyed me, and followed those whose wealth and children have only added to their ruin;

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Noah said: "O my Lord! They have disobeyed me, but they follow (men) whose wealth and children give them no increase but only Loss.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And they contrived a plot of great magnitude,

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “And they carried out a very sinister scheme.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and have devised a mighty device

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And who have plotted a tremendous plot

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "And they have plotted a mighty plot.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And they schemed outrageous schemes.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: They contrived a plot of great magnitude.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "And they have plotted a mighty plot."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And they have plotted a mighty plot,

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: they have devised a mighty plot,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And they conspired an immense conspiracy.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: They have arrogantly plotted evil plans against me,

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And they have planned a very great plan.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: and they have hatched a mighty plot,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "And they have devised a tremendous Plot.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And said: 'Do not abandon your gods, and do not abandon Wadda or Suwa', or Yaghuth, Ya'uq or Nasr.'

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “And they said, ‘Do not ever abandon your Gods – and never abandon Wadd, or Suwa – or Yaghuth or Yauq or Nasr.’”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and have said, "Do not leave your gods, and do not leave Wadd, nor Suwa', Yaghuth, Ya'uq, neither Nasr."

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And who have said: ye shall not leave your gods; nor Shall ye leave Wadd nor Suwa', nor Yaghuth, nor Ya'uq nor Nasr.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "And they have said: 'You shall not leave your gods, nor shall you leave Wadd, nor Suwa', nor Yaghuth, nor Ya'uq, nor Nasr (names of the idols);

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And they said, “Do not give up your gods; do not give up Wadd, nor Souwa, nor Yaghoos, and Yaooq, and Nassr.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: They said: “Do not abandon your deities; do not abandon Wadd, nor Suwa, nor Yaghuth, nor Yauq, nor Nasr.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "And they have said: `You shall not leave your gods, nor shall you leave Wadd, nor Suwa`, nor Yaghuth, and Ya`uq and Nasr.' "

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And they have said: Forsake not your gods. Forsake not Wadd, nor Suwa', nor Yaghuth and Ya'uq and Nasr.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: and said: 'Do not renounce your gods, do not leave waddan or suwa'an or yaghutha, or ya'uqa, or nasra'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And said, 'Never leave your gods and never leave Wadd or Suwa' or Yaghuth and Ya'uq and Nasr.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: and have said to each other, 'Do not give-up your idols. Do not renounce Wadd, Suwa`, Yaghuth, Ya`uq and Nasr (names of certain idols).

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And they say: By no means leave your gods, nor leave Wadd, nor Suwa; nor Yaghus, and Yauq and Nasr.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: and they said [to their followers], "Do not ever abandon your deities: abandon neither Wadd, nor Suwa', and neither Yaghuth, nor Yauq nor Nasr!"

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "And they have said (to each other), 'Abandon not your gods: Abandon neither Wadd nor Suwa', neither Yaguth nor Ya'uq, nor Nasr';-

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And they misled many. So do not give the evil-doers increase but in error.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And they have led many astray. Increase Thou not the evildoers save in error!'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And surely they have misled many. And increase Thou these wrong-doers in naught save error.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "And indeed they have led many astray. And (O Allah): 'Grant no increase to the Zalimun (polytheists, wrong-doers, and disbelievers, etc.) save error."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: They have misled many, so do not increase the wrongdoers except in confusion.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: They have misled many. So do not enable these evildoers to increase in anything except straying (from the Right Way).”

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "And indeed they have led many astray. And (O Allah): `Grant no increase to the wrongdoers save error."'

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And they have led many astray, and Thou increasest the wrong-doers in naught save error.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: and already they have led many astray. Do not increase the wrongdoers in anything but error.’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And they have led many astray. (Noah supplicated saying): 'Allah, do not increase the harmdoers except in error'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And already they have misled many. And, [my Lord], do not increase the wrongdoers except in error."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: They have misled many and the unjust will achieve nothing but more error".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And indeed they have led astray many, and do not increase the unjust in aught but error.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: They have led many astray; so lead the wrongdoers to further error."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "They have already misled many; and grant Thou no increase to the wrong-doers but in straying (from their mark)."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: God advances the example of Noah's wife and the wife of Lot for those who do not believe. They were married to Our two pious devotees, but they were unfaithful to them, and even (the apostles) could not avail them in the least against God; and it was said to them: "Enter Hell with those (who are condemned) to enter it."

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: God has struck a similitude for the unbelievers -- the wife of Noah, and the wife of Lot; for they were under two of Our righteous servants, but they betrayed them, so they availed them nothing whatsoever against God; so it was said, 'Enter, you two, the Fire with those who enter.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Allah propoundeth for those who disbelieve the similitude of the wife of Nuh and the wife of Lut. They were under two of our righteous bondmen; then they defrauded them. Wherefore the twain availed them naught against Allah, and it was said: enter ye twain the Fire with those who enter.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Allah sets forth an example for those who disbelieve, the wife of Nuh (Noah) and the wife of Lout (Lot). They were under two of our righteous slaves, but they both betrayed their (husbands by rejecting their doctrine) so they [Nuh (Noah) and Lout (Lot)] benefited them (their respective wives) not, against Allah, and it was said: "Enter the Fire along with those who enter!"

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: God illustrates an example of those who disbelieve: the wife of Noah and the wife of Lot. They were under two of Our righteous servants, but they betrayed them. They availed them nothing against God, and it was said, “Enter the Fire with those who are entering.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Allah has set forth for the unbelievers the parable of the wives of Noah and Lot. They were wedded to two of Our righteous servants, but each acted treacherously with her husband, and their husbands could be of no avail to them against Allah. The two of them were told: “Enter the Fire with all the others who enter it.”

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Allah sets forth an example for those who disbelieve: the wife of Nuh and the wife of Lut. They were under two of our righteous servants, but they both betrayed them. So, they availed them not against Allah and it was said: "Enter the Fire along with those who enter!"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Allah citeth an example for those who disbelieve: the wife of Noah and the wife of Lot, who were under two of Our righteous slaves yet betrayed them so that they (the husbands) availed them naught against Allah and it was said (unto them): Enter the Fire along with those who enter.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Allah cites an example of the faithless: the wife of Noah and the wife of Lot. They were under two of our righteous servants, yet they betrayed them. So they did not avail them in any way against Allah, and it was said [to them], ‘Enter the Fire, along with those who enter [it].’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Allah has given as an example for the unbelievers, the wife of Noah and the wife of Lot. They were married to two of Our righteous worshipers, but theybetrayed them. It was not for (Noah and Lot) to avail them a thing. It was said to them both: 'Enter the Fire with those who shall enter'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Allah presents an example of those who disbelieved: the wife of Noah and the wife of Lot. They were under two of Our righteous servants but betrayed them, so those prophets did not avail them from Allah at all, and it was said, "Enter the Fire with those who enter."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: God has told the disbelievers the story of the wives of Noah and Lot as a parable. They were married to two of Our righteous servants but were unfaithful to them. Nothing could protect them from the (wrath) of God and they were told to enter hell fire with the others.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Allah sets forth an example to those who disbelieve the wife of Nuh and the wife of Lut: they were both under two of Our righteous servants, but they acted treacherously towards them so they availed them naught against Allah, and it was said: Enter both the fire with those who enter.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: As an example to those who are bent on denying the truth, God cited the wife of Noah and the wife of Lot, who were married to two of Our righteous servants, but who betrayed them. So they could not help them against God, and they were told, "Enter the Fire along with all the others."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Allah sets forth, for an example to the Unbelievers, the wife of Noah and the wife of Lut: they were (respectively) under two of our righteous servants, but they were false to their (husbands), and they profited nothing before Allah on their account, but were told: "Enter ye the Fire along with (others) that enter!"

translation.author: Dr. Muhammad Muhsin
translation.text: Narrated Ibn Abbas: All the idols which were worshipped by the people of Noah were worshipped by the Arabs later on. As for the idol Wadd, it was worshipped by the tribe of Kalb at Daumat-al-Jandal; Suwa' was the idol of (the tribe of) Murad and then by Ban, Ghutaif at Al-Jurf near Saba; Yauq was the idol of Hamdan, and Nasr was the idol of Himyr, the branch of Dhi-al-Kala.' The names (of the idols) formerly belonged to some pious men of the people of Noah, and when they died Satan inspired their people to (prepare and place idols at the places where they used to sit, and to call those idols by their names. The people did so, but the idols were not worshipped till those people (who initiated them) had died and the origin of the idols had become obscure, whereupon people began worshipping them.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Recount to them the story of Noah when he said to his people: "O people, if you find my staying with you and warning through God's signs, unbearable to you, know that I have reposed my trust in God. So plan your move, and call your associates, and make certain of your plan; then do whatever you intend against me, allowing me no respite.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And recite to them the story of Noah when he said to his people, 'My people, if my standing here is grievous to you and my reminding you of the signs of God, in God have I put my trust; so resolve on your affair, with your associates, then let not your affair be a worry to you, but make decision unto me, and respite me not.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And rehearse thou unto them the story of Nuh, when he said unto his people: O my people! if my standing forth and my admonishment with the commandments of Allah be hard upon you, then on Allah I rely; so devise your affair, ye and your associate-gods and let not your affair be dubious unto you, then have it decreed against me, and respite me not.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And recite to them the news of Nuh (Noah). When he said to his people: "O my people, if my stay (with you), and my reminding (you) of the Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) of Allah is hard on you, then I put my trust in Allah. So devise your plot, you and your partners, and let not your plot be in doubt for you. Then pass your sentence on me and give me no respite.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And relate to them the story of Noah, when he said to his people, “O my people, if my presence among you and my reminding you of God’s signs is too much for you, then in God I have put my trust. So come to a decision, you and your partners, and do not let the matter perplex you; then carry out your decision on me, and do not hold back.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And narrate to them the story of Noah when he said to his people: 'My people! If my living in your midst and my effort to shake you out of heedlessness by reciting to you the revelations of Allah offend you, then remember that I have put all my trust in Allah. So draw up your plan in concert with those whom you associate with Allah in His divinity, leaving no part of it obscure, and then put it into effect against me, and give me no respite.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And recite to them the news of Nuh. When he said to his people: "O my people, if my stay (with you), and my reminding (you) of the Ayat of Allah is hard on you, then I put my trust in Allah. So devise your plot, you and your partners, and let not your plot be in confusion for you. Then pass your sentence on me and give me no respite.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Recite unto them the story of Noah, when he told his people: O my people! If my sojourn (here) and my reminding you by Allah's revelations are an offence unto you, in Allah have I put my trust, so decide upon your course of action you and your partners. Let not your course of action be in doubt for you. Then have at me, give me no respite.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Relate to them the account of Noah when he said to his people, ‘O my people! If my stay [among you] be hard on you and [also] my reminding you of Allah’s signs, [for my part] I have put my trust in Allah. So conspire together, along with your partners, leaving nothing vague in your plan, then carry it out against me without giving me any respite.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And recite to them the story of Noah. He said to his nation: 'O my nation, if my standing here, reminding you of the verses of Allah grieves you, and in Allah I have put my trust; decide upon your affair with your associates, then do not let your affair worry you. Come to a decision about me, and give me no respite.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And recite to them the news of Noah, when he said to his people, "O my people, if my residence and my reminding of the signs of Allah has become burdensome upon you - then I have relied upon Allah. So resolve upon your plan and [call upon] your associates. Then let not your plan be obscure to you. Then carry it out upon me and do not give me respite.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: (Muhammad), tell them the story of Noah who told his people, "Even if my belief and my preaching of the revelation of God seem strange to you, I put my trust in Him. Unite yourselves and seek help from your idols. You should not regret what you want to do, but should execute your plans against me without delay

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And recite to them the story of Nuh when he said to his people: O my people! if my stay and my reminding (you) by the communications of Allah is hard on you-- yet on Allah do I rely-- then resolve upon your affair and (gather) your associates, then let not your affair remain dubious to you, then have it executed against me and give me no respite:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Tell them the story of Noah. He said to his people, "My people, if my presence among you and my preaching to you of God's revelations offends you, know that I put my trust in God, so agree on your course of action, along with your partner-gods, and let no hesitation deflect you from it, then put it into effect against me, and give me no respite.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Relate to them the story of Noah. Behold! he said to his people: "O my people, if it be hard on your (mind) that I should stay (with you) and commemorate the signs of Allah,- yet I put my trust in Allah. Get ye then an agreement about your plan and among your partners, so your plan be on to you dark and dubious. Then pass your sentence on me, and give me no respite.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: If you turn away from me (remember) I do not ask any recompense from you. My reward is with God; I have been commanded to be one of those who submit to Him."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “Then if you turn away, I do not ask any fee from you; my reward is only upon Allah, and I am commanded to be of the Muslims.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Then if you turn your backs, I have not asked you for any wage; my wage falls only on God, and I have been commanded to be of those that surrender.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: If then ye turn away, I have asked of you no hire, my hire is only with Allah, and I am commanded to be of those who submit.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "But if you turn away [from accepting my doctrine of Islamic Monotheism, i.e. to worship none but Allah], then no reward have I asked of you, my reward is only from Allah, and I have been commanded to be one of the Muslims (those who submit to Allah's Will)."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: “But if you turn away, I have not asked you for any wage. My wage falls only on God, and I was commanded to be of those who submit.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: When you turned your back on my admonition (what harm did you cause me?) I had asked of you no reward, for my reward lies only with Allah, and I am commanded to be of those who totally submit (to Allah)

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "But if you turn away, then no reward have I asked of you, my reward is only from Allah, and I have been commanded to be of the Muslims."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: But if ye are averse I have asked of you no wage. My wage is the concern of Allah only, and I am commanded to be of those who surrender (unto Him).

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Then, if you turn your backs, I have not asked of you a wage. My wage falls only on Allah. I am commanded to be from those who surrender (Muslims)'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And if you turn away [from my advice] then no payment have I asked of you. My reward is only from Allah, and I have been commanded to be of the Muslims."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: If you turn away from my preaching, it will not harm me; I shall receive my reward from God who has commanded me to become a Muslim."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: But if you turn back, I did not ask for any reward from you; my reward is only with Allah, and I am commanded that I should be of those who submit.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: If you turn away from me, remember I demand no recompense from you. Only God will reward me and I have been commanded to submit completely to Him."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "But if ye turn back, (consider): no reward have I asked of you: my reward is only due from Allah, and I have been commanded to be of those who submit to Allah's will (in Islam)."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: He replied: "Only God will bring it on you if He please, and you cannot prevail against Him.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: He said, 'God will bring you it if He will; you cannot frustrate Him.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: He said: only Allah will bring it on you if He will, and ye shall not escape.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: He said: "Only Allah will bring it (the punishment) on you, if He will, and then you will escape not.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He said, “It is God who will bring it upon you, if He wills, and you will not be able to escape.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Noah said: 'Only Allah will bring it upon you if He so wills, and you will be utterly unable to frustrate that.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: He said: "Only Allah will bring it (the punishment) on you, if He wills, and then you will escape not.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: He said: Only Allah will bring it upon you if He will, and ye can by no means escape.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: He said, ‘Allah will indeed bring it on you if He wishes, and you cannot frustrate [Him].

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: He replied: 'Allah will bring it (down) upon you if He will; you will never frustrate Him.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: He said, "Allah will only bring it to you if He wills, and you will not cause [Him] failure.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: (Noah) replied, "God will bring torment down on you whenever He wants and you will not be able to make His (plans) fail.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: He said: Allah only will bring it to you if He please, and you will not escape:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: He said, "It is God who will bring it down upon you, if He wishes, and you will not be able to escape.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: He said: "Truly, Allah will bring it on you if He wills,- and then, ye will not be able to frustrate it!

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Even if I wish to advise you aright, my counsel will not profit you if God intend that you go astray, for He is your Lord and to Him you will return."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “And my advice will not benefit you if I wish you good, when Allah wills to keep you astray; He is your Lord and to Him you will return.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And my sincere counsel will not profit you, if I desire to counsel you sincerely, if God desires to pervert you; He is your Lord, and unto Him you shall be returned.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Nor would my good counsel profit you, even though I wished to give you good counsel, if Allah willed to keep you astray. He is your Lord, and unto Him ye shall be returned.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "And my advice will not profit you, even if I wish to give you good counsel, if Allah's Will is to keep you astray. He is your Lord! and to Him you shall return."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: “My advice will not benefit you, much as I may want to advise you, if God desires to confound you. He is your Lord, and to Him you will be returned.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: If I want to give you good advice that will not profit you if Allah Himself has decided to let you go astray. He is your Lord, and to Him will you be returned.'

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "And my advice will not profit you, even if I wish to give you good counsel, if Allah's will is to keep you astray. He is your Lord! and to Him you shall return."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: My counsel will not profit you if I were minded to advise you, if Allah's will is to keep you astray. He is your Lord and unto Him ye will be brought back.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Nor will my sincere counsel benefit you if I sincerely desire to counsel you if Allah desires to lead you astray. He is your Lord, and to Him you shall return'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And my advice will not benefit you - although I wished to advise you - If Allah should intend to put you in error. He is your Lord, and to Him you will be returned."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: My advice will be of no benefit to you if God wants to let you go astray. He is your Lord and to Him you will all return."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And if I intend to give you good advice, my advice will not profit you if Allah intended that He should leave you to go astray; He is your Lord, and to Him shall you be returned.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: My advice will not benefit you, no matter how sincerely I want to advise you, if God lets you go astray. He is your Lord and you will all return to Him."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "Of no profit will be my counsel to you, much as I desire to give you (good) counsel, if it be that Allah willeth to leave you astray: He is your Lord! and to Him will ye return!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Do they say you have fabricated it? Tell them: "If I have fabricated it, then mine is the guilt; but I am clear of what you are guilty."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: What! They dare say that, “He has fabricated it”? Say, “If I may have fabricated it, then my sin is upon me, and I am unconcerned with your sins.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: (Or do they say, 'He has forged it'? Say: 'If I have forged it, upon me falls my sin; and I am quit of the sins you do.')

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Or say they: he hath fabricated it? Say thou: on me then be my guilt, and I am quit of that whereof ye are guilty.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Or they (the pagans of Makkah) say: "He (Muhammad SAW) has fabricated it (the Quran)." Say: "If I have fabricated it, upon me be my crimes, but I am innocent of (all) those crimes which you commit."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Or do they say, “He made it up?” Say, “If I made it up, upon me falls my crime, and I am innocent of the crimes you commit.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: (O Muhammad!) Do they say that he himself has forged this message? Tell them: 'If I have forged this, the guilt of it will fall upon me, but I am not responsible for the crimes you are committing.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Or they (the pagans of Makkah) say: "He has fabricated it." Say: "If I have fabricated it, upon me be my crimes, but I am innocent of (all) those crimes which you commit.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Or say they (again): He hath invented it? Say: If I have invented it, upon me be my crimes, but I am innocent of (all) that ye commit.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Or do they say: 'He has fabricated it (himself)' Say: 'If I had fabricated it, then the sin rests upon me. I reject the sins you do'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Or do they say [about Prophet Muhammad], "He invented it"? Say, "If I have invented it, then upon me is [the consequence of] my crime; but I am innocent of what [crimes] you commit."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Do they say that Muhammad has falsely ascribed (the Quran) to God? (Muhammad), tell them "Had I falsely ascribed it to God, I shall be responsible for my own sins. I am certainly not responsible for whatever sins you commit!

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Or do they say: He has forged it? Say: If I have forged it, on me is my guilt, and I am clear of that of which you are guilty.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: If they say, "He has invented it himself," say to them, "If I have indeed invented this myself, then may I be punished for my sin; I am innocent of the crimes that you commit."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Or do they say, "He has forged it"? Say: "If I had forged it, on me were my sin! and I am free of the sins of which ye are guilty!

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: "O my Lord," prayed (Noah), "help me against them, for they accuse me of lies."

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: He said, 'O my Lord, help me, for that they cry me lies.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Nuh said: my Lord! vindicate me, for they belie me.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: [Nuh (Noah)] said: "O my Lord! Help me because they deny me."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He said, “My Lord, help me, for they have rejected me.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Noah said: "My Lord! Come to my help at their accusation that I am lying."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: He said: "O my Lord! Help me because they deny me."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: He said: My Lord! Help me because they deny me.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: He said, ‘My Lord! Help me, for they impugn me.’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: He (Noah) said: 'Lord, help me, for they belie me'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: [Noah] said, "My Lord, support me because they have denied me."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Noah prayed, "Lord, help me; they have called me a liar".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: He said: O my Lord! help me against their calling me a liar.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Noah said, "My Lord, help me! for they have rejected me,"

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: (Noah) said: "O my Lord! help me: for that they accuse me of falsehood!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: So he prayed to his Lord: "I am helpless, deliver me."

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And so he called unto his Lord, saying, 'I am vanquished; do Thou succour me!'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Thereupon he prayed Unto his Lord: verily am overcome, so vindicate me.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Then he invoked his Lord (saying): "I have been overcome, so help (me)!"

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: So he appealed to his Lord, “I am overwhelmed, so help me.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Then he called upon His Lord: “Verily I am vanquished; so come You to my aid.”

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Then he invoked his Lord (saying): "I have been overcome, so help (me)!"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: So he cried unto his Lord, saying: I am vanquished, so give help.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Thereat he invoked his Lord, [saying,] ‘I have been overcome, so help [me].’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Then he supplicated to his Lord, (saying): 'I am overcome, help me'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: So he invoked his Lord, "Indeed, I am overpowered, so help."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Noah prayed, "Lord, help me; I am defeated".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Therefore he called upon his Lord: I am overcome, come Thou then to help.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: so he cried out to his Lord, saying, "I am overcome, so help me!"

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Then he called on his Lord: "I am one overcome: do Thou then help (me)!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: He prayed: "O Lord, verily my people accuse me of lies.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: He said, 'My Lord, my people have cried me lies,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: He said: my Lord! verily my people have belied me.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: He said: "My Lord! Verily, my people have belied me.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He said, “My Lord, my people have denied me.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: He said: "My Lord! My people have branded me a liar.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: He said: "My Lord! Verily, my people have denied me."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: He said: My Lord! Lo! my own folk deny me.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: He said, ‘My Lord! Indeed my people have impugned me.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: He said: 'My Lord, my nation have belied me.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: He said, "My Lord, indeed my people have denied me.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Then Noah said, "Lord, my people have rejected me.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: He said: My Lord! Surely my people give me the lie!

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Noah said, "My Lord, my people have rejected me,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: He said: "O my Lord! truly my people have rejected me.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: So adjudge between me and them conclusively, and save me and the believers who are with me."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “Therefore make a decisive judgement between me and them, and rescue me and the believers along with me.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: so give true deliverance between me and them, and deliver me and the believers that are with me.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: So decide Thou between me and them with a decision, and deliver me and those who are with me of the believers.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Therefore judge You between me and them, and save me and those of the believers who are with me."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: So judge between me and them decisively, and deliver me and the believers who are with me.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: So pass a clear judgement between me and them and rescue me and the believers with me."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "Therefore judge You between me and them, and save me and those of the believers who are with me."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Therefor judge Thou between us, a (conclusive) judgment, and save me and those believers who are with me.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: So open between me and them an opening, and save me and the believers who are with me'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Then judge between me and them with decisive judgement and save me and those with me of the believers."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Judge among us and save me and the believers with me".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Therefore judge Thou between me and them with a (just) judgment, and deliver me and those who are with me of the believers.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: therefore, judge decisively between me and them; and save me and the believers who are with me."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "Judge Thou, then, between me and them openly, and deliver me and those of the Believers who are with me."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Noah said: "O Lord, do not leave a single habitation of unbelievers on the earth.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And Noah said, 'My Lord, leave not upon the earth of the unbelievers even one.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And Nuh said: my Lord! leave not upon the earth of the infidels one inhabitant.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And Nuh (Noah) said: "My Lord! Leave not one of the disbelievers on the earth!

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Noah said, “My Lord, do not leave of the unbelievers a single dweller on earth.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Noah said: “My Lord, do not leave out of these unbelievers even a single dweller on earth,

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And Nuh said: "My Lord! Leave not one of the disbelievers on the earth Dayyar!"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And Noah said: My Lord! Leave not one of the disbelievers in the land.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: And Noah said, ‘My Lord! ‘Do not leave on the earth any inhabitant from among the faithless.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And Noah (supplicated) saying: 'My Lord, do not leave a single unbeliever upon the earth.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And Noah said, "My Lord, do not leave upon the earth from among the disbelievers an inhabitant.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Noah said, "Lord, do not leave a single disbeliever on earth;

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And Nuh said: My Lord! leave not upon the land any dweller from among the unbelievers:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Noah prayed, "O my Lord! Do not leave on earth a single one of those who deny the truth --

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And Noah, said: "O my Lord! Leave not of the Unbelievers, a single one on earth!

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: If you leave them, they will lead Your creatures astray, and beget but iniquitous and ungrateful offspring.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “Indeed, if You spare them, they will mislead your bondmen – and their descendants, if any, will be none except the wicked, very ungrateful.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Surely, if Thou leavest them, they will lead Thy servants astray, and will beget none but unbelieving libertines.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Verily if Thou shouldst leave them, they Will mislead Thine bondmen and will beget not save sinning infidels,

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "If You leave them, they will mislead Your slaves, and they will beget none but wicked disbelievers."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: If You leave them, they will mislead your servants, and will breed only wicked unbelievers.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: for certainly if You should leave them (alive), they will mislead Your servants, and will beget none but sinners and utter unbelievers.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "If You leave them, they will mislead Your servants, and they will beget none but wicked disbelievers."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: If Thou shouldst leave them, they will mislead Thy slaves and will beget none save lewd ingrates.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Surely, if You leave them they will mislead Your worshipers and father none but the immoral, and unbelievers.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Indeed, if You leave them, they will mislead Your servants and not beget except [every] wicked one and [confirmed] disbeliever.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: if You do, they will mislead Your servants and will only give birth to ungrateful sinners.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: For surely if Thou leave them they will lead astray Thy servants, and will not beget any but immoral, ungrateful (children)

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: if You leave any, they will misguide Your servants, and they will beget none but sinners and deniers of truth --

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "For, if Thou dost leave (any of) them, they will but mislead Thy devotees, and they will breed none but wicked ungrateful ones.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: O Lord, forgive me, my parents, and any one who enters my house as a believer, and all believing men and women, and do not give the evil-doers increase save in ruin.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “O my Lord! Forgive me, and my parents, and the believers who are in my house, and all other Muslim men and Muslim women; and do not increase anything for the unjust except ruin.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: My Lord, forgive me and my parents and whosoever enters my house as a believer, and the believers, men and women alike; and do Thou not increase the evildoers save in ruin!'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: My Lord! forgive me and my parents and him who entereth my house as a believer, and believing men and believing women, and increase not the wrong-doers in aught save ruin!

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "My Lord! Forgive me, and my parents, and him who enters my home as a believer, and all the believing men and women. And to the Zalimun (polytheists, wrong-doers, and disbelievers, etc.) grant You no increase but destruction!"

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: My Lord! Forgive me and my parents, and anyone who enters my home in faith, and all the believing men and believing women; and do not increase the wrongdoers except in perdition.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: My Lord, forgive me and my parents, and whoever enters my house as a believer, and forgive all believers, both men and women, and do not increase the wrong-doers in anything except perdition.”

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "My Lord! Forgive me, and my parents, and him who enters my home as a believer, and all the believing men and women. And to the wrongdoers grant You no increase but destruction!") Allah says,

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: My Lord! Forgive me and my parents and him who entereth my house believing, and believing men and believing women, and increase not the wrong-doers in aught save ruin.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Forgive me, My Lord, and my parents, and whosoever seeks refuge in my house as a believer, and the believers men and women alike, and do not increase the harmdoers except in ruin'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: My Lord, forgive me and my parents and whoever enters my house a believer and the believing men and believing women. And do not increase the wrongdoers except in destruction."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Lord, forgive me, my parents, the believers who have entered my home and all believing men and women. Give nothing to the unjust but destruction.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: My Lord! forgive me and my parents and him who enters my house believing, and the believing men and the believing women; and do not increase the unjust in aught but destruction!

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Lord! Forgive me and my parents and every true believer who enters my house, forgive all the believing men and believing women; and bestow no increase upon the wrongdoers except in ruin?

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "O my Lord! Forgive me, my parents, all who enter my house in Faith, and (all) believing men and believing women: and to the wrong-doers grant Thou no increase but in perdition!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And Noah was informed through revelation: "Apart from those who have come to believe already not one of your people is going to believe. So grieve not for what they are doing.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And it was revealed to Noah, saying, 'None of thy people shall believe but he who has already believed; so be thou not distressed by that they may be doing.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And it was revealed unto Nuh: verily none of thy people will believe save those that have believed already; so be not distressed for that which they have been doing.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And it was inspired to Nuh (Noah): "None of your people will believe except those who have believed already. So be not sad because of what they used to do.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And it was revealed to Noah: “None of your people will believe, except those who have already believed, so do not grieve over what they do.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: It was revealed to Noah that no more of your people, other than those who already believe, will ever come to believe. So do not grieve over their deeds,

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And it was revealed to Nuh: "None of your people will believe except those who have believed already. So be not sad because of what they used to do.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And it was inspired in Noah, (saying): No-one of thy folk will believe save him who hath believed already. Be not distressed because of what they do.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: It was revealed to Noah: ‘None of your people will believe except those who already have faith; so do not sorrow for what they used to do.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And it was revealed to Noah: 'None of your nation will believe except whosoever has already believed. Do not distress yourself with what they may do.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And it was revealed to Noah that, "No one will believe from your people except those who have already believed, so do not be distressed by what they have been doing.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: It was revealed to Noah that besides those who had already accepted his faith, no one from his people would ever believe him. He was told not to be disappointed about what his people had done,

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And it was revealed to Nuh: That none of your people will believe except those who have already believed, therefore do not grieve at what they do:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: God's will was revealed to Noah, "No more of your people will believe in you than those who already believe; do not grieve, therefore, over what they have been doing.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: It was revealed to Noah: "None of thy people will believe except those who have believed already! So grieve no longer over their (evil) deeds.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Build an ark under Our eye and as We instruct. Do not plead for those who have been wicked, for they shall certainly be drowned."

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Make thou the Ark under Our eyes, and as We reveal; and address Me not concerning those who have done evil; they shall be drowned.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And make thou the ark under Our eyes and Our Revelation; and address Me not regarding those that have done wrong; verily they are to be drowned.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "And construct the ship under Our Eyes and with Our Inspiration, and address Me not on behalf of those who did wrong; they are surely to be drowned."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: “And build the Ark, under Our eyes, and with Our inspiration, and do not address Me regarding those who did wrong; they are to be drowned.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: and build the Ark under Our eyes and Our direction. And do not supplicate Me concerning those who have engaged in wrong-doing. They are doomed to be drowned.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "And construct the ship under Our Eyes and with Our revelation, and address Me not on behalf of those who did wrong; they are surely to be drowned."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Build the ship under Our eyes and by Our inspiration, and speak not unto Me on behalf of those who do wrong. Lo! they will be drowned.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Build the ark before Our eyes and by Our revelation, and do not plead with Me for those who are wrongdoers: they shall indeed be drowned.’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Build the Ark with Our seeing (and protection), and as We reveal. Do not speak to Me concerning the evildoers; they shall be drowned'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And construct the ship under Our observation and Our inspiration and do not address Me concerning those who have wronged; indeed, they are [to be] drowned."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: but to build the Ark under the supervision and guidance of his Lord. He was also told not to address any words to Him concerning the unjust for they were all to be drowned.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And make the ark before Our eyes and (according to) Our revelation, and do not speak to Me in respect of those who are unjust; surely they shall be drowned.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Build the Ark under Our eyes and in accordance with Our revelation. Do not plead with Me concerning the evil-doers. They shall certainly be drowned."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "But construct an Ark under Our eyes and Our inspiration, and address Me no (further) on behalf of those who are in sin: for they are about to be overwhelmed (in the Flood)."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: So he built the ark; and when groups of his people passed by him, they scoffed at him. He said to them: "Though you laugh at us (now), we shall laugh at you, as you are laughing at us.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: So he was making the Ark; and whenever a council of his people passed by him they scoffed at him, He said, 'If you scoff at us, we shall surely scoff at you, as you scoff

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And he was making the ark; and whenever the chiefs of his people passed by him, they scoffed at him. He said: if ye scoff at us, verily we also scoff at you even as ye scoff at us.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And as he was constructing the ship, whenever the chiefs of his people passed by him, they made a mockery of him. He said: "If you mock at us, so do we mock at you likewise for your mocking.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: As he was building the ark, whenever some of his people passed by him, they ridiculed him. He said, “If you ridicule us, we will ridicule you, just as you ridicule.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: As Noah was building the Ark, whenever the leading men of his nation passed by him, they would scoff at him. He said: 'If you scoff at us, we too scoff at you in like manner.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And as he was constructing the ship, whenever the chiefs of his people passed by him, they mocked at him. He said: "If you mock at us, so do we mock at you likewise for your mocking."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And he was building the ship, and every time that chieftains of his people passed him, they made mock of him. He said: Though ye make mock of Us, yet We mock at you even as ye mock;

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: As he was building the ark, whenever the elders of his people passed by him, they would ridicule him. He said, ‘If you ridicule us [today], we shall ridicule you [tomorrow] just as you ridicule us [now].

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Whenever an assembly of his nation passed by him as he was building the Ark, they mocked him. Whereupon he said: 'If you mock us, we shall indeedmock you, just as you mock.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And he constructed the ship, and whenever an assembly of the eminent of his people passed by him, they ridiculed him. He said, "If you ridicule us, then we will ridicule you just as you ridicule.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: (Noah) started to build the Ark but whenever some of his people passed by, they would mock him. He in return would reply, "Mock us, but just as you mock us, we, too, will mock you.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And he began to make the ark; and whenever the chiefs from among his people passed by him they laughed at him. He said: If you laugh at us, surely we too laugh at you as you laugh (at us).

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: So he began to build the Ark, and whenever leaders of his people passed by, they scoffed at him. He said, "If you are scoffing at us, we shall scoff at you [and your ignorance], just as you scoff at us:

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Forthwith he (starts) constructing the Ark: Every time that the chiefs of his people passed by him, they threw ridicule on him. He said: "If ye ridicule us now, we (in our turn) can look down on you with ridicule likewise!

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: So We asked him to build the ark under Our eyes and guidance, (and said): "When Our command is issued and the source of water boils over, put a pair of every species in it, and your family except those for whom Our sentence has been passed already; and do not speak to Me for those who are wicked: They will be drowned.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Then We said to him, 'Make thou the Ark under Our eyes and as We reveal, and then, when Our command comes and the Oven boils, insert in it two of every kind and thy, family -- except for him against whom the word already has been spoken; and address Me not concerning those who have done evil; they shall be drowned.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Then We revealed unto him, saying: build the ark under Our eyes and Our Revelation; then when Our command cometh and the oven boileth over, make way therein of every pair two, and thy household save him thereof against whom there hath already gone forth the word; and address me not in respect of those who have done wrong; verily they are to be drowned.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: So We inspired him (saying): "Construct the ship under Our Eyes and under Our Revelation (guidance). Then, when Our Command comes, and the oven gushes forth water, take on board of each kind two (male and female), and your family, except those thereof against whom the Word has already gone forth. And address Me not in favour of those who have done wrong. Verily, they are to be drowned.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: So We inspired him: “Build the Ark under Our observation and by Our inspiration. And when Our decree comes to pass, and the oven boils over, load into it two pairs of every kind, together with your family, except those of them against whom the word has already been pronounced. And do not speak to me concerning those who did wrong; for they are to be drowned.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Thereupon We revealed to him, saying: "Build the Ark under Our eyes and according to Our revelation. And when Our command comes to pass and the oven boils over, take on board a pair each from every species, and also take your household except those of them against whom sentence has already been passed and do not plead to Me on behalf of the wrong-doers. They are doomed to be drowned.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: So, We revealed to him: "Construct the ship under Our Eyes and under Our revelation. Then, when Our command comes, and (water) gushes forth from the oven, take on board of each kind two, and your family, except those thereof against whom the Word has already gone forth. And address Me not in favor of those who have done wrong. Verily, they are to be drowned."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Then We inspired in him, saying: Make the ship under Our eyes and Our inspiration. Then, when Our command cometh and the oven gusheth water, introduce therein of every (kind) two spouses, and thy household save him thereof against whom the Word hath already gone forth. And plead not with Me on behalf of those who have done wrong. Lo! they will be drowned.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: So We revealed to him: ‘Build the ark before Our eyes and by Our revelation. When Our edict comes and the oven gushes [a stream of water], bring into it a pair of every kind [of animal], and your family, except those of them against whom the decree has gone beforehand, and do not plead with Me for those who are wrongdoers: they shall indeed be drowned.’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: We said to him: 'Make the Ark under Our Eye, and Our Revelation, and then when Our command comes and the oven gushes water, take onboard a pair from every kind and your family, except him against whom the decision has already been passed. Do not address Me concerning those who have done evil; they shall be drowned.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: So We inspired to him, "Construct the ship under Our observation, and Our inspiration, and when Our command comes and the oven overflows, put into the ship from each [creature] two mates and your family, except those for whom the decree [of destruction] has proceeded. And do not address Me concerning those who have wronged; indeed, they are to be drowned.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We inspired him, saying, "Build the Ark before Our eyes and by the instruction of Our revelation. When our decree comes to pass and water comes forth from the Oven, embark in the Ark with a pair of every kind of animals and your family except those already doomed (to perish). Do not plead with Me for the unjust; they will be drowned."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: So We revealed to him, saying: Make the ark before Our eyes and (according to) Our revelation; and when Our command is given and the valley overflows, take into it of every kind a pair, two, and your followers, except those among them against whom the word has gone forth, and do not speak to Me in respect of those who are unjust; surely they shall be drowned.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: then We revealed Our will to him: "Build the Ark under Our watchful eye according to Our instructions. When Our command comes, and waters gush up out of the earth, take on board pairs of every species and members of your household, except for any of them on whom sentence has already been passed -- do not plead with Me for those who have done wrong: they shall be drowned.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: So We inspired him (with this message): "Construct the Ark within Our sight and under Our guidance: then when comes Our Command, and the fountains of the earth gush forth, take thou on board pairs of every species, male and female, and thy family- except those of them against whom the Word has already gone forth: And address Me not in favour of the wrong-doers; for they shall be drowned (in the Flood).

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And We opened up the flood gates of the sky with water pouring down in torrents,

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: We therefore opened the gates of heaven, with water flowing furiously.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Then We opened the gates of heaven unto water torrential,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Then We opened the portals of heaven with water poured out.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: So We opened the gates of heaven with water pouring forth.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: So We opened the floodgates of heaven with water pouring down.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Thereupon We opened the gates of the sky for water to pour down,

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: So, We opened the gates of the heaven with water Munhamir.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Then opened We the gates of heaven with pouring water

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: We opened the Gates of Heaven with torrential water

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Then We opened the gates of the heaven with rain pouring down

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We opened the gates of the sky and water started to pour down.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: So We opened the gates of the cloud with water pouring

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: So We opened the gates of the sky with water pouring down in torrents,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: So We opened the gates of heaven, with water pouring forth.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And We opened up the springs of the earth; and the waters met for a decreed end.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And caused springs to gush out from the earth, so that the two waters met totalling a quantity that had been destined.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and made the earth to gush with fountains, and the waters met for a matter decreed.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And We made the earth break forth with springs; SO that the water met for an affair already decreed.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And We caused the earth to gush forth with springs. So the waters (of the heaven and the earth) met for a matter predestined.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And We made the earth burst with springs, and the waters met for a purpose already destined.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: and We made the earth burst forth with springs, and all this water converged to fulfil that which had been decreed.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And We caused springs to gush forth from the earth. So, the waters met for a matter predestined.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And caused the earth to gush forth springs, so that the waters met for a predestined purpose.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: and caused the earth to gush with springs, so that the waters met for a predestined matter.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And caused the earth to burst with springs, and the waters met for a matter already predestined.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We caused the earth to burst forth with springs so that the waters could come together for a predestined purpose.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And We made water to flow forth in the land in springs, so the water gathered together according to a measure already ordained.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: and We caused the earth to burst with gushing springs: so that the waters met for a purpose which had been decreed.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And We caused the earth to gush forth with springs, so the waters met (and rose) to the extent decreed.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: When Our command was issued and the waters gushed forth from the source, We said: "Take into (the ark) a pair of every species, and members of your family other than those against whom the sentence has been passed already, and those who come to believe." But only a few believed in him.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Until, when Our command came, and the Oven boiled, We said, 'Embark in it two of every kind, and thy family -- except for him against whom the word has already been spoken and whosoever believes.' And there believed not with him except a few.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Thus were they employed until when Our decree came and the oven boiled over, We said: carry thereon of every kind two, and thy household except him thereof against whom the word hath already gone forth, and whosoever hath believed. And there had not believed with him save a few.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: (So it was) till then there came Our Command and the oven gushed forth (water like fountains from the earth). We said: "Embark therein, of each kind two (male and female), and your family, except him against whom the Word has already gone forth, and those who believe. And none believed with him, except a few."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Until, when Our command came, and the volcano erupted, We said, “Board into it a pair of every kind, and your family—except those against whom the sentence has already been passed—and those who have believed.” But those who believed with him were only a few.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Thus it was until Our command came to pass and the oven boiled over. We said: 'Take into the Ark a pair of every species; and take your own family except those who have already been declared (as unworthy); and also take everyone who believes. But those who, along with him, had believed were indeed just a few.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: (So it was) till when Our command came and the oven gushed forth (water like fountains from the earth). We said: "Embark therein, of each kind two (male and female), and your family -- except him against whom the Word has already gone forth -- and those who believe. And none believed with him, except a few."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: (Thus it was) till, when Our commandment came to pass and the oven gushed forth water, We said: Load therein two of every kind, a pair (the male and female), and thy household, save him against whom the word hath gone forth already, and those who believe. And but a few were they who believed with him.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: When Our edict came and the oven gushed [a stream of water], We said, ‘Carry in it a pair of every kind [of animal], along with your family—except those [of them] against whom the edict has already been given—and those who have faith.’ And none believed with him except a few.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And when Our command came and the oven gushed (forth with water), We said (to Noah): 'Take on board (the Ark) a pair from every species and yourfamily, except he of whom the word has already been spoken, and those who believe. And none except a few believed with him.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: [So it was], until when Our command came and the oven overflowed, We said, "Load upon the ship of each [creature] two mates and your family, except those about whom the word has preceded, and [include] whoever has believed." But none had believed with him, except a few.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: When at last Our decree was fulfilled, water gushed forth from the Oven (in Noah's house). We told him to carry in the Ark a pair (male and female) from every species, his family - except those who were destined to perish - and the believers. No one believed in him, except a few.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Until when Our command came and water came forth from the valley, We said: Carry in it two of all things, a pair, and your own family-- except those against whom the word has already gone forth, and those who believe. And there believed not with him but a few.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: When Our command came, and water gushed forth in torrents, We said to Noah, "Take into the Ark a pair from every species, and your own family -- except those on whom the sentence has already been passed, and all the true believers." But only a few believed along with him.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: At length, behold! there came Our command, and the fountains of the earth gushed forth! We said: "Embark therein, of each kind two, male and female, and your family - except those against whom the word has already gone forth,- and the Believers." but only a few believed with him.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And (Noah) said: "Embark. In the name of God be its course and mooring. My Lord is surely forgiving and kind."

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: He said, 'Embark in it! In God's Name shall be its course and its berthing. Surely my Lord is All-forgiving, All-compassionate.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And he said: embark therein; in the name of Allah be its course and its anchorage, verily my Lord is Forgiving, Merciful.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And he [Nuh (Noah)] said: "Embark therein, in the Name of Allah will be its moving course and its resting anchorage. Surely, my Lord is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful." (Tafsir At-Tabari, Vol. 12, Page 43)

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He said, “Embark in it. In the name of God shall be its sailing and its anchorage. My Lord is indeed Forgiving and Merciful.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Noah said: ' Embark in it. In the name of Allah is its sailing and its anchorage. My Lord is Ever Forgiving, Most Merciful.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And he said: "Embark therein: in the Name of Allah will be its (moving) course and its (resting) anchorage. Surely, my Lord is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And he said: Embark therein! In the name of Allah be its course and its mooring. Lo! my Lord is Forgiving, Merciful.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: He said, ‘Board it: In the Name of Allah it shall set sail and cast anchor. Indeed my Lord is all-forgiving, all-merciful.’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: He (Noah) said: 'Embark. In the Name of Allah will be its course and berthing. Indeed, my Lord is Forgiving, the Most Merciful'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And [Noah] said, "Embark therein; in the name of Allah is its course and its anchorage. Indeed, my Lord is Forgiving and Merciful."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: (Noah) said, "Embark in it. It will sail in the name of God, in His Name it will sail and in His Name it will cast anchor. My Lord is All-forgiving and All-merciful".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And he said: Embark in it, in the name of Allah be its sailing and its anchoring; most surely my Lord is Forgiving, Merciful.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Noah said, "Embark on it. In the name of God, it shall set sail and cast anchor. Truly, my Lord is forgiving and merciful."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: So he said: "Embark ye on the Ark, In the name of Allah, whether it move or be at rest! For my Lord is, be sure, Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: When you and those with you have boarded the ark, say: 'All praise be to God who has delivered us from the people who were sinful.'

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Then, when thou art seated in the Ark and those with thee, say, "Praise belongs to God, who has delivered us from the people of the evildoers."

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And when thou art settled, thou and those with thee, in the ark, say thou: praise unto Allah who hath delivered us from the wrong doing people.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And when you have embarked on the ship, you and whoever is with you, then say: "All the praises and thanks be to Allah, Who has saved us from the people who are Zalimun (i.e. oppressors, wrong-doers, polytheists, those who join others in worship with Allah, etc.).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Then, when you and those with you are settled in the Ark, say, “Praise be to God, who has saved us from the wrongdoing people.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And then when you and those accompanying you are firmly seated in the Ark, say: "Thanks be to Allah Who has delivered us from the wrong-doing people."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "And when you have embarked on the ship, you and whoever is with you, then say: `All the praise be to Allah, Who has saved us from the people who are wrongdoers."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And when thou art on board the ship, thou and whoso is with thee, then say: Praise be to Allah Who hath saved us from the wrongdoing folk!

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: ‘When you, and those who are with you, are settled in the ark, say, ‘‘All praise belongs to Allah, who has delivered us from the wrongdoing lot.’’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And when you and all those with you are seated in the Ark, say: 'Praise be to Allah who has saved us from the harmdoing nation'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And when you have boarded the ship, you and those with you, then say, 'Praise to Allah who has saved us from the wrongdoing people.'

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: When all of you settle in the Ark, say, "Only God Who has saved us from the unjust people deserves all praise".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And when you are firmly seated, you and those with you, in the ark, say: All praise is due to Allah who delivered us from the unjust people:

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: When you and all your followers have settled in the Ark, say, "Praise be to God who has delivered us from a wicked people,"

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And when thou hast embarked on the Ark - thou and those with thee,- say: "Praise be to Allah, Who has saved us from the people who do wrong."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: It sailed on waves like mountains (high), and Noah called to his son who was separated from him: "Embark with us, O my son, and be not one of those who do not believe."

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: So it ran with them amid waves like mountains; and Noah called to his son, who was standing apart, 'Embark with us, my son, and be thou not with the unbelievers!'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And it moved on with them amidst waves like mountains. And Nuh called out his son, and he was apart: O my son! embark with us, and be not with the infidels.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: So it (the ship) sailed with them amidst the waves like mountains, and Nuh (Noah) called out to his son, who had separated himself (apart), "O my son! Embark with us and be not with the disbelievers."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And so it sailed with them amidst waves like hills. And Noah called to his son, who had kept away, “On my son! Embark with us, and do not be with the disbelievers.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: The Ark sailed along with them amid mountain-like waves. Noah, spotting his son at a distance, called out to him: 'My son. embark with us. and do not be with the unbelievers.'

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: So it (the ship) sailed with them amidst waves like mountains, and Nuh called out to his son, who had separated himself (apart): "O my son! Embark with us and be not with the disbelievers."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And it sailed with them amid waves like mountains, and Noah cried unto his son - and he was standing aloof - O my son! Come ride with us, and be not with the disbelievers.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: And it sailed along with them amid waves [rising] like mountains. Noah called out to his son, who stood aloof, ‘O my son! ‘Board with us, and do not be with the faithless!’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And so it (the Ark) ran with them amidst the mountainous waves, and Noah cried out to his son, who was standing apart, 'Embark with us, my son, do not be with the unbelievers'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And it sailed with them through waves like mountains, and Noah called to his son who was apart [from them], "O my son, come aboard with us and be not with the disbelievers."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: When the Ark sailed on with them amid the mountainous waves, Noah called out to his son who kept away from them, "My son, embark with us. Do not stay with the unbelievers."

translation.author: Shakir
