translation.text: And it moved on with them amid waves like mountains; and Nuh called out to his son, and he was aloof: O my son! embark with us and be not with the unbelievers.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: The Ark sailed along with them through mountainous waves. Noah called out to his son who stood apart, "O my son! Embark with us and do not be among the deniers!"

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: So the Ark floated with them on the waves (towering) like mountains, and Noah called out to his son, who had separated himself (from the rest): "O my son! embark with us, and be not with the unbelievers!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: "I shall go up a mountain," he said, "which will keep me from the water." "There is no getting away," said Noah, "from the decree of God today, except for those on whom be His mercy." And a wave came between them, and he was among those who were drowned.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: He said, 'I will take refuge in a mountain, that shall defend me from the water.' Said he, 'Today there is no defender from God's command but for him on whom He has mercy.' And the waves came between them, and he was among the drowned.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: He said: I shall betake me to a mountain which will defend me from the water. Nuh said: there is no defender to-day from the decree of Allah save for one on whom He hath mercy. And a wave intervened betwixt the twain; so he was of the drowned.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: The son replied: "I will betake myself to a mountain, it will save me from the water." Nuh (Noah) said: "This day there is no saviourfrom the Decree of Allah except him on whom He has mercy." And a wave came in between them, so he (the son) was among the drowned.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He said, “I will take refuge on a mountain—it will protect me from the water.” He said, “There is no protection from God’s decree today, except for him on whom He has mercy.” And the waves surged between them, and he was among the drowned.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: The son replied: 'I will go to a mountain for refuge and it will save me from the water.' Noah said: 'None can save anyone today from the command of Allah except those on whom He may have mercy.' Thereupon a wave swept in between the two and he was drowned.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: The son replied: "I will betake myself to some mountain, it will save me from the water." Nuh said: "This day there is no savior from the decree of Allah except him on whom He has mercy." And waves came in between them, so he (the son) was among the drowned.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: He said: I shall betake me to some mountain that will save me from the water. (Noah) said: This day there is none that saveth from the commandment of Allah save him on whom He hath had mercy. And the wave came in between them, so he was among the drowned.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: He said, ‘I shall take refuge on a mountain; it will protect me from the flood.’ He said, ‘There is none today who can protect from Allah’s edict, except someone upon whom He has mercy.’ Then the waves came between them, and he was among those who were drowned.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: But he replied: 'I shall seek refuge on a mountain, which will protect me from the water' He (Noah) said: 'Today, there is no defender from the command of Allah, except those to whom He has mercy' And the waves came between them, and he was drowned.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: [But] he said, "I will take refuge on a mountain to protect me from the water." [Noah] said, "There is no protector today from the decree of Allah, except for whom He gives mercy." And the waves came between them, and he was among the drowned.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: His son replied, "I shall climb up a mountain and this will save me from the flood." Noah said, "No one can escape on this day from God's command except those on whom He has mercy." The waves separated Noah from his son who was then drowned with the rest (of the unbelievers).

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: He said: I will betake myself for refuge to a mountain that shall protect me from the water. Nuh said: There is no protector today from Allah's punishment but He Who has mercy; and a wave intervened between them, so he was of the drowned.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: He replied, "I shall seek refuge on a mountain, which will save me from the water." Noah cried, "Today there is no refuge for anyone from God's command except for those to whom He shows mercy!" Thereupon, a wave swept in between them, and Noah's son was among those who were drowned.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: The son replied: "I will betake myself to some mountain: it will save me from the water." Noah said: "This day nothing can save, from the command of Allah, any but those on whom He hath mercy! "And the waves came between them, and the son was among those overwhelmed in the Flood.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: But We bore him on a (vessel made) of planks and oakum,

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And We bore him upon a well-planked vessel well-caulked

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And We bare him on a thing of planks and nails.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And We carried him on a (ship) made of planks and nails,

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And We carried him on a craft of planks and nails.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And We bore Noah on the vessel built of planks and nails,

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And We carried him on a (ship) made of planks and nails (Dusur)

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And We carried him upon a thing of planks and nails,

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: We bore him on a vessel made of planks and nails,

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: We carried him in a well built, watertight vessel made from planks (of wood)

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And We carried him on a [construction of] planks and nails,

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We carried him, (Noah), on a vessel built with boards fixed together with nails,

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And We bore him on that which was made of planks and nails

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We bore him on an [ark] which, made of planks and nails,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: But We bore him on an (Ark) made of broad planks and caulked with palm-fibre:

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Which sailed right under Our eyes: A recompense for him who had been denied.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: Sailing in front of Our sight; as a reward for the sake of one who was rejected.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: running before Our eyes-a recompense for him denied.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Moving forward under Our eyes: a requital for him who had been rejected.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Floating under Our Eyes, a reward for him who had been rejected!

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Sailing before Our eyes; a reward for him who was rejected.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: which sailed on under Our supervision: a reward for him who had been shown ingratitude.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Floating under Our Eyes, a reward for him who had been rejected!

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: That ran (upon the waters) in Our sight, as a reward for him who was rejected.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: that ran on under Our Eyes, a recompense for him because he had been disbelieved.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Sailing under Our observation as reward for he who had been denied.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: which floated on the water before Our very eyes. The flood was a recompense for the deeds of the disbelievers.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Sailing, before Our eyes, a reward for him who was denied.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: floated on under Our eyes: a vindication of him who had been rejected.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: She floats under our eyes (and care): a recompense to one who had been rejected (with scorn)!

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: When the water rose in flood, We bore you in the ark,

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Lo, when the waters rose, We bore you in the running ship

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Verily We! when the water rose, We bare you upon the traversing ark.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Verily! When the water rose beyond its limits [Nuh's (Noah) Flood], We carried you (mankind) in the floating [ship that was constructed by Nuh (Noah)].

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: When the waters overflowed, We carried you in the cruising ship.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Verily when the water rose to great heights, We bore you upon a floating vessel (i.e. the Ark)

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Verily, when the water rose beyond its limits, We carried you in the ship.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Lo! when the waters rose, We carried you upon the ship

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Indeed when the Flood rose high, We carried you in a floating ark,

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And when the waters rose (high) We carried you in the sailing (Ark),

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Indeed, when the water overflowed, We carried your ancestors in the sailing ship

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: When the flood rose high and covered the whole land, We carried you in the Ark.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Surely We bore you up in the ship when the water rose high,

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: But We bore you away in the Ark, when the waters rose high,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: We, when the water (of Noah's Flood) overflowed beyond its limits, carried you (mankind), in the floating (Ark),

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: In order to make it a warning for you, and that the ear retentive may preserve it.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: In order to make it a remembrance for you, and in order that the ears that store may remember.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: that We might make it a reminder for you and for heeding ears to hold.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: That We might make it Unto you an admonition, and thot it might be retained by the retaining ears.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: That We might make it a remembrance for you, and the keen ear (person) may (hear and) understand it.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: To make it a lesson for you—so that retaining ears may retain it.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: so that We might make it an instructive event for you, and retentive ears might preserve its memory.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: That We might make it an admonition for you and that it might be retained by the retaining ears.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: That We might make it a memorial for you, and that remembering ears (that heard the story) might remember.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: making it a Reminder for you, for all attentive ears to retain.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: That We might make it for you a reminder and [that] a conscious ear would be conscious of it.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: as a lesson for you, but only attentive ears will retain it.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: So that We may make it a reminder to you, and that the retaining ear might retain it.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: so that We might make it a reminder for you and so that attentive ears might retain it.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: That We might make it a Message unto you, and that ears (that should hear the tale and) retain its memory should bear its (lessons) in remembrance.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: That We bore their progeny in the laden ark is a sign for them;

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And a sign for them is that We carried their seed in the laden ship,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And a sign Unto them is that We bear their offspring in a laden ship.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And an Ayah (sign) for them is that We bore their offspring in the laden ship [of Nuh (Noah)].

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Another sign for them is that We carried their offspring in the laden Ark.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Another Sign for them is that We carried all their offspring in the laden vessel

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And an Ayah for them is that We bore their offspring in the laden ship.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And a token unto them is that We bear their offspring in the laden ship,

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: A sign for them is that We carried their progeny in the laden ship,

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And a sign for them is that We carried their offspring in the laden Ark (of Noah).

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And a sign for them is that We carried their forefathers in a laden ship.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: how We carried them and their offspring inside the laden Ark

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And a sign to them is that We bear their offspring in the laden ship.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Another sign for them is that We carried their offspring in the laden Ark.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And a Sign for them is that We bore their race (through the Flood) in the loaded Ark;

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And We made similar vessels for them to ride.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And We have created for them similar ships, in which they now ride.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and We have created for them the like of it whereon they ride;

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And We have created for them of the like thereUnto whereon they ride.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And We have created for them of the like thereunto, so on them they ride.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And We created for them the like of it, in which they ride.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: and then created for them other vessels like those on which they ride.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And We have created for them of the like thereunto, on which they ride.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And have created for them of the like thereof whereon they ride.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And We have created for them the like of it in which they board.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And We created for them from the likes of it that which they ride.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: and created for them similar things to ride.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And We have created for them the like of it, what they will ride on.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We have created for them the like of it in which they ride.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And We have created for them similar (vessels) on which they ride.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Then it was said: "O earth, swallow back your water; and, O sky, desist." And the water subsided, and the decree was accomplished. The ark came to rest on Judi (Mount Ararat), and it was said: "Away with the cursed people!"

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And it was said, 'Earth, swallow thy waters; and, heaven, abate!' And the waters subsided, the affair was accomplished, and the Ark settled on El-Judi, and it was said: 'Away with the people of the evildoers!'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And it was said: O earth! swallow up thy water, and cease, O heaven! And the water abated; and fulfilled was the decree. And it rested upon the Judi; and it was said: away with the wrong-doing people!

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And it was said: "O earth! Swallow up your water, and O sky! Withhold (your rain)." And the water was diminished (made to subside) and the Decree (of Allah) was fulfilled (i.e. the destruction of the people of Nuh (Noah). And it (the ship) rested on Mount Judi, and it was said: "Away with the people who are Zalimun (polytheists and wrong-doing)!"

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And it was said, “O earth, swallow your waters,” and “O heaven, clear up.” And the waters receded, and the event was concluded, and it settled on Judi, and it was proclaimed: “Away with the wicked people.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And the command was given: 'Earth! Swallow up your water'; and: 'Heaven! Abate!' So the water subsided, the command was fulfilled, and the Ark settled on Mount Judi, and it was said: 'Away with the wrong-doing folk!'

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And it was said: "O earth! Swallow up your water, and O sky! Withhold (your rain)." And the water was made to subside and the decree was fulfilled. And it rested on (Mount) Judi, and it was said: "Away with the people who are wrongdoing!"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And it was said: O earth! Swallow thy water and, O sky! be cleared of clouds! And the water was made to subside. And the commandment was fulfilled. And it (the ship) came to rest upon (the mount) Al-Judi and it was said: A far removal for wrongdoing folk!

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Then it was said, ‘O earth, swallow your water! O sky, leave off!’ The waters receded; the edict was carried out, and it settled on [Mount] Judi. Then it was said, ‘Away with the wrongdoing lot!’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And it was said: 'Earth, swallow up your waters. Heaven, cease' The water subsided and the matter was accomplished. And the Ark came to rest upon (the mountain of) AlJudi, and it was said: 'Be gone, evildoing nation'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And it was said, "O earth, swallow your water, and O sky, withhold [your rain]." And the water subsided, and the matter was accomplished, and the ship came to rest on the [mountain of] Judiyy. And it was said, "Away with the wrongdoing people."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Then the earth was told to swallow-up its water and the sky was ordered to stop raining. The water abated and God's command had been fulfilled. The Ark came to rest on Mount Judi. A voice said, "The unjust people are far away from the mercy of God."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And it was said: O earth, swallow down your water, and O cloud, clear away; and the water was made to abate and the affair was decided, and the ark rested on the Judi, and it was said: Away with the unjust people.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: A voice cried out, "Earth, swallow up your waters. O sky, cease your rain." The waters receded. The command was fulfilled. The Ark came to rest on Mount Judi. It was said, "Away with the wrong-doing people."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Then the word went forth: "O earth! swallow up thy water, and O sky! Withhold (thy rain)!" and the water abated, and the matter was ended. The Ark rested on Mount Judi, and the word went forth: "Away with those who do wrong!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And say: 'O Lord, disembark me in a welcome place; You are the best of deliverers.'

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And say, "'O my Lord, do Thou harbour me in a blessed harbour, for Thou art the best of harbourers."

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And say thou, my Lord! cause me to land at a landing blest, and thou art the Best of these who bring to land.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And say: "My Lord! Cause me to land at a blessed landing-place, for You are the Best of those who bring to land."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And say, “My Lord, land me with a blessed landing, as you are the best of transporters.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And say: "My Lord! Make my landing a blessed landing, for You are the Best of those Who can cause people to land in safety."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "And say: `My Lord! Cause me to land at a blessed landing place, for You are the Best of those who bring to land."'

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And say: My Lord! Cause me to land at a blessed landing-place, for Thou art Best of all who bring to land.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: And say, ‘‘My Lord! Land me with a blessed landing, for You are the best of those who bring ashore.’’ ’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And say: 'Lord, let me land a blessed landing. You are the Best of harborers'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And say, 'My Lord, let me land at a blessed landing place, and You are the best to accommodate [us].' "

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Say, "Lord, grant us a blessed landing from the Ark; You are the One who provides the safest landing".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And say: O my Lord! cause me to disembark a blessed alighting, and Thou art the best to cause to alight.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: and say, 'My Lord, let me land with Your blessing in a blessed landing place. You alone can provide the best landings.'"

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And say: "O my Lord! enable me to disembark with thy blessing: for Thou art the Best to enable (us) to disembark."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: (And the Lord) said: "O Noah, disembark with peace and safety from Us and blessings on you and the people with you. As for some (of them), We shall bestow advantages for a time, then send a grievous punishment on them."

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: It was said, 'Noah, get thee down in peace from Us, and blessings upon thee and on the nations of those with thee; and nations -- We shall give them enjoyment, then there shall visit them from Us a painful chastisement.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: It was said: Nuh! get thou down with peace from us and blessings upon thee and the communities with thee. And there shall be communities whom we shall let enjoy themselves, and thereafter there shall befall them from us a torment afflictive.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: It was said: "O Nuh (Noah)! Come down (from the ship) with peace from Us and blessings on you and on the people who are with you (and on some of their off spring), but (there will be other) people to whom We shall grant their pleasures (for a time), but in the end a painful torment will reach them from Us."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: It was said, “O Noah, disembark with peace from Us; and with blessings upon you, and upon communities from those with you. And other communities We will grant prosperity, and then a painful torment from Us will befall them.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: It was said: 'Noah! Disembark, with Our peace, and with blessings upon you and upon those who are with you. There are also people whom We shall allow to enjoy themselves for a while, and then a painful chastisement from Us shall afflict them.'

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: It was said: "O Nuh! Come down (from the ship) with peace from Us and blessings on you and on the people who are with you (and on some of their offspring), but (there will be other) people to whom We shall grant their pleasures (for a time), but in the end a painful torment will reach them from Us."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: It was said (unto him): O Noah! Go thou down (from the mountain) with peace from Us and blessings upon thee and some nations (that will spring) from those with thee. (There will be other) nations unto whom We shall give enjoyment a long while and then a painful doom from Us will overtake them.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: It was said, ‘O Noah! Disembark in peace from Us and with [Our] blessings upon you and upon nations [to descend] from those who are with you, and nations whom We shall provide for, then a painful punishment from Us shall befall them.’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: It was said: 'O Noah, descend with peace from Us and blessings on you and on the nations of those with you; and nations We shall give them enjoyment, and then from Us they shall be visited with a painful punishment'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: It was said, "O Noah, disembark in security from Us and blessings upon you and upon nations [descending] from those with you. But other nations [of them] We will grant enjoyment; then there will touch them from Us a painful punishment."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Noah was told, "Get down from the Ark. Your Lord's peace and blessings are upon you and your followers. Your Lord will grant favors to other nations and then afflict them with a painful torment."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: It was said: O Nuh! descend with peace from Us and blessings on you and on the people from among those who are with you, and there shall be nations whom We will afford provisions, then a painful punishment from Us shall afflict them.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: God said, "Noah, go ashore in peace; Our blessings upon you and upon the people who are with you and upon some of the descendants of those who are with you. [As for the unrighteous] We shall grant provision for a time, then a grievous punishment from Us shall afflict them."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: The word came: "O Noah! Come down (from the Ark) with peace from Us, and blessing on thee and on some of the peoples (who will spring) from those with thee: but (there will be other) peoples to whom We shall grant their pleasures (for a time), but in the end will a grievous penalty reach them from Us."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Noah called on his Lord and said: "O Lord, my son is surely a member of my family, and verily Your promise is true, as You are the most just of all judges."

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And Noah called unto his Lord, and said, 'O my Lord, my son is of my family, and Thy promise is surely the truth. Thou art the justest of those that judge.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And Nuh cried unto his Lord, and said: my Lord! verily my son is of my household, and verily Thine promise is the truth: and Thou art the Greatest of the rulers.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And Nuh (Noah) called upon his Lord and said, "O my Lord! Verily, my son is of my family! And certainly, Your Promise is true, and You are the Most Just of the judges."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And Noah called to his Lord. He said, “O My Lord, my son is of my family, and Your promise is true, and You are the Wisest of the wise.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And Noah called out to his Lord, saying: 'My Lord! My son is of my family. Surely Your promise is true, and You are the greatest of those who judge.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And Nuh called upon his Lord and said, "O my Lord! Verily, my son is of my family! And certainly, Your promise is true, and You are the Most Just of the judges."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And Noah cried unto his Lord and said: My Lord! Lo! my son is of my household! Surely Thy promise is the truth and Thou are the Most Just of Judges.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Noah called out to his Lord, and said, ‘My Lord! My son is indeed from my family. Your promise is indeed true, and You are the fairest of all judges.’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Noah called out to his Lord, saying: 'O Lord, my son was of my family, and Your promise is surely the truth. You are the most Just of judges'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And Noah called to his Lord and said, "My Lord, indeed my son is of my family; and indeed, Your promise is true; and You are the most just of judges!"

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Noah prayed to his Lord saying, "Lord, my son is a member of my family. Your promise is always true and you are the best Judge".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And Nuh cried out to his Lord and said: My Lord! surely my son is of my family, and Thy promise is surely true, and Thou art the most just of the judges.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Noah called out to his Lord, saying, "My Lord, my son was a part of my family. Your promise was surely true. You are the most just of all judges."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And Noah called upon his Lord, and said: "O my Lord! surely my son is of my family! and Thy promise is true, and Thou art the justest of Judges!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: "O Noah", He answered, "truly he is not of your family. He is surely the outcome of an unrighteous act. So ask Me not of what you do not know. I warn you not to be one of the ignorant."

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Said He, 'Noah, he is not of thy family; it is a deed not righteous. Do not ask of Me that whereof thou hast no knowledge. I admonish thee, lest thou shouldst be among the ignorant.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: He said: O Nuh! verily he is not of thy household; verily he is of unrighteous Conduct; wherefore ask Me not that whereof thou hast no knowledge; verily I exhort thee not to be of the ignorants.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: He said: "O Nuh (Noah)! Surely, he is not of your family; verily, his work is unrighteous, so ask not of Me that of which you have no knowledge! I admonish you, lest you be one of the ignorants."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He said, “O Noah, he is not of your family. It is an unrighteous deed. So do not ask Me about something you know nothing about. I admonish you, lest you be one of the ignorant.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: In response Noah was told: 'Most certainly he is not of your family; verily he is of unrighteous conduct. So do not ask of Me for that concerning which you have no knowledge. I admonish you never to act like the ignorant ones.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: He said: "O Nuh! Surely, he is not of your family; verily, his work is unrighteous, so ask not of Me that of which you have no knowledge! I admonish you, lest you should be one of the ignorant."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: He said: O Noah! Lo! he is not of thy household; lo! he is of evil conduct, so ask not of Me that whereof thou hast no knowledge. I admonish thee lest thou be among the ignorant.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Said He, ‘O Noah! Indeed, He is not of your family. Indeed, he is [personification of] unrighteous conduct. So do not ask Me [something] of which you have no knowledge. I advise you lest you should be among the ignorant.’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: He said: 'Noah, he is not of your family. It is not a good deed. Do not ask Me about things of which you have no knowledge. I reproach you lest you become among the ignorant'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: He said, "O Noah, indeed he is not of your family; indeed, he is [one whose] work was other than righteous, so ask Me not for that about which you have no knowledge. Indeed, I advise you, lest you be among the ignorant."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: His Lord replied, "He is not one of your family. He is a man of unrighteous deeds. Do not ask me about that which you have no knowledge. I advise you not to become an ignorant person."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: He said: O Nuh! surely he is not of your family; surely he is (the doer of) other than good deeds, therefore ask not of Me that of which you have no knowledge; surely I admonish you lest you may be of the ignorant

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: God said, "Noah, he was not one of your family. For, indeed, he was unrighteous in his conduct. Do not question Me about something of which you have no knowledge; I admonish you lest you become like an ignorant man."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: He said: "O Noah! He is not of thy family: For his conduct is unrighteous. So ask not of Me that of which thou hast no knowledge! I give thee counsel, lest thou act like the ignorant!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: "Preserve me, O Lord," said (Noah), "from asking You that of which I have no knowledge. If You do not forgive me and have mercy on me I shall be among those who perish."

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: He said, 'My Lord, I take refuge with Thee, lest I should ask of Thee that whereof I have no knowledge; for if Thou forgivest me not, and hast not mercy on me, I shall be among the losers.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Nuh said: my Lord! verily take refuge with Thee lest I may ask Thee that whereof I have no knowledge. And if Thou forgivest me not and hast not mercy on me, I shall be of the losers.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Nuh (Noah) said: "O my Lord! I seek refuge with You from asking You that of which I have no knowledge. And unless You forgive me and have Mercy on me, I would indeed be one of the losers."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He said, “O My Lord, I seek refuge with You, from asking You about what I have no knowledge of. Unless You forgive me, and have mercy on me, I will be one of the losers.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Noah said: 'My Lord! I take refuge with You that I should ask you for that concerning which I have no knowledge. And if You do not forgive me and do not show mercy to me, I shall be among the losers.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Nuh said: "O my Lord! I seek refuge with You from asking You that of which I have no knowledge. And unless You forgive me and have mercy on me, I would indeed be one of the losers."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: He said: My Lord! Lo! in Thee do I seek refuge (from the sin) that I should ask of Thee that whereof I have no knowledge. Unless Thou forgive me and have mercy on me I shall be among the lost.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: He said, ‘My Lord! I seek Your protection lest I should ask You something of which I have no knowledge. If You do not forgive me and have mercy upon me I shall be among the losers.’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: He said: 'My Lord, I seek refuge with You from asking You of that which I have no knowledge. If You do not forgive me and have mercy on me, I shall be among the losers'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: [Noah] said, "My Lord, I seek refuge in You from asking that of which I have no knowledge. And unless You forgive me and have mercy upon me, I will be among the losers."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Noah said, "Lord, I ask You to prevent me from asking You ignorant questions and beg you for pardon and mercy or else I shall certainly be lost".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: He said: My Lord! I seek refuge in Thee from asking Thee that of which I have no knowledge; and if Thou shouldst not forgive me and have mercy on me, I should be of the losers.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: He said, "My Lord, I take refuge with You from asking You something of which I have no knowledge. If You do not forgive me and show me mercy, I shall be one of the losers."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Noah said: "O my Lord! I do seek refuge with Thee, lest I ask Thee for that of which I have no knowledge. And unless thou forgive me and have Mercy on me, I should indeed be lost!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: "Preserve me, O Lord," said (Noah), "from asking You that of which I have no knowledge. If You do not forgive me and have mercy on me I shall be among those who perish."

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: He said, 'My Lord, I take refuge with Thee, lest I should ask of Thee that whereof I have no knowledge; for if Thou forgivest me not, and hast not mercy on me, I shall be among the losers.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Nuh said: my Lord! verily take refuge with Thee lest I may ask Thee that whereof I have no knowledge. And if Thou forgivest me not and hast not mercy on me, I shall be of the losers.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Nuh (Noah) said: "O my Lord! I seek refuge with You from asking You that of which I have no knowledge. And unless You forgive me and have Mercy on me, I would indeed be one of the losers."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He said, “O My Lord, I seek refuge with You, from asking You about what I have no knowledge of. Unless You forgive me, and have mercy on me, I will be one of the losers.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Noah said: 'My Lord! I take refuge with You that I should ask you for that concerning which I have no knowledge. And if You do not forgive me and do not show mercy to me, I shall be among the losers.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Nuh said: "O my Lord! I seek refuge with You from asking You that of which I have no knowledge. And unless You forgive me and have mercy on me, I would indeed be one of the losers."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: He said: My Lord! Lo! in Thee do I seek refuge (from the sin) that I should ask of Thee that whereof I have no knowledge. Unless Thou forgive me and have mercy on me I shall be among the lost.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: He said, ‘My Lord! I seek Your protection lest I should ask You something of which I have no knowledge. If You do not forgive me and have mercy upon me I shall be among the losers.’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: He said: 'My Lord, I seek refuge with You from asking You of that which I have no knowledge. If You do not forgive me and have mercy on me, I shall be among the losers'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: [Noah] said, "My Lord, I seek refuge in You from asking that of which I have no knowledge. And unless You forgive me and have mercy upon me, I will be among the losers."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Noah said, "Lord, I ask You to prevent me from asking You ignorant questions and beg you for pardon and mercy or else I shall certainly be lost".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: He said: My Lord! I seek refuge in Thee from asking Thee that of which I have no knowledge; and if Thou shouldst not forgive me and have mercy on me, I should be of the losers.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: He said, "My Lord, I take refuge with You from asking You something of which I have no knowledge. If You do not forgive me and show me mercy, I shall be one of the losers."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Noah said: "O my Lord! I do seek refuge with Thee, lest I ask Thee for that of which I have no knowledge. And unless thou forgive me and have Mercy on me, I should indeed be lost!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: This is news of the Unknown We reveal to you, which neither you nor your people knew before. So endure with patience. The future is for those who keep away from evil and follow the straight path.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: These are the tidings of the hidden, which We reveal to you (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him); you did not know them nor did your people, before this; therefore patiently endure; indeed the excellent fate is for the pious.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: (That is of the tiding's of the Unseen, that We reveal to thee; thou didst not know it, neither thy people, before this. So be patient; the issue ultimate is to the godfearing.)

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: That is of the stories of the unseen: We Reveal it unto thee: thou knewest it not, nor thy nation knew it ere this. So be thou patient; verily the happy end is for the God-fearing.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: This is of the news of the unseen which We reveal unto you (O Muhammad SAW), neither you nor your people knew them before this. So be patient. Surely, the (good) end is for the Muttaqun (pious - see V. 2:2)

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: These are some stories from the past that we reveal to you. Neither you, nor your people knew them before this. So be patient. The future belongs to the pious.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: We reveal to you these accounts of matters that are beyond the reach of human perception. Neither you nor your people knew about them before this. Be, then, patient. Surely, the good end is for the God-fearing.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: This is of the news of the Unseen which We reveal unto you; neither you nor your people knew it before this. So be patient. Surely, the (good) end is for those who have Taqwa.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: This is of the tidings of the Unseen which We inspire in thee (Muhammad). Thou thyself knewest it not, nor did thy folk (know it) before this. Then have patience. Lo! the sequel is for those who ward off (evil).

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: That is from the news of the unseen which We reveal to you; neither you nor your nation knew this before now. Have patience; the outcome is for the cautious.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: That is from the news of the unseen which We reveal to you, [O Muhammad]. You knew it not, neither you nor your people, before this. So be patient; indeed, the [best] outcome is for the righteous.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: That which We have revealed to you (Muhammad) is news of the unseen. This was not known to you and to your people. Have patience. The pious will triumph in the end.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: These are announcements relating to the unseen which We reveal to you, you did not know them-- (neither) you nor your people-- before this; therefore be patient; surely the end is for those who guard (against evil).

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: These are tidings of the unseen that We reveal to you, [O Prophet], which you did not know before, nor did your people, so be patient: the future belongs to the God-fearing.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Such are some of the stories of the unseen, which We have revealed unto thee: before this, neither thou nor thy people knew them. So persevere patiently: for the End is for those who are righteous.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: But they called him a liar, and We saved him and those with him in the Ark, and drowned the others who rejected Our signs, for they were a people purblind.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: But they cried him lies; so We delivered him, and those with him, in the Ark, and We drowned those who cried lies to Our signs; assuredly they were a blind people.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Then they belied him; there upon We delivered him and those with him in the ark, and drowned those who belied Our signs; verily they were a people blind.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: But they belied him, so We saved him and those along with him in the ship, and We drowned those who belied Our Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.). They were indeed a blind people.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: But they called him a liar. So We saved him and those with him in the Ark, and We drowned those who rejected Our revelations. They were blind people.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: But they charged him with falsehood. Thereupon We delivered Noah and those who were with him in the Ark, and caused those who rejected Our signs as false to be drowned. Surely they were a blind folk.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: But they belied him, so We saved him and those along with him in the Fulk, and We drowned those who belied Our Ayat. They were indeed a blind people.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: But they denied him, so We saved him and those with him in the ship, and We drowned those who denied Our tokens. Lo! they were blind folk.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: But they denied him. So We delivered him and those who were with him in the ark, and We drowned those who denied Our signs. Indeed, they were a blind lot.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: (But) they belied him, so We saved him and those who were with him in the Ark, and We drowned those who belied Our verses. Surely, they were a blind nation.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: But they denied him, so We saved him and those who were with him in the ship. And We drowned those who denied Our signs. Indeed, they were a blind people.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: They accused him of telling lies. So We saved him and his companions in an ark and drowned those who called Our revelations mere lies. They were, no doubt, a blind people.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: But they called him a liar, so We delivered him and those with him in the ark, and We drowned those who rejected Our communications; surely they were a blind people.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: But they denied him, so We saved him and those with him in the Ark, and We drowned those who rejected Our signs. They were indeed a blind people.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: But they rejected him, and We delivered him, and those with him, in the Ark: but We overwhelmed in the flood those who rejected Our signs. They were indeed a blind people!

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Even then they denied him; so We saved him and those with him, in the ark, and established them in the land, and drowned those who denied Our signs. So think of the fate of those who were warned (and took no heed).

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: But they cried him lies; so We delivered him, and those with him, in the Ark, and We appointed them as viceroys, and We drowned those who cried lies to Our signs; then behold how was the end of them that were warned!

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: So they belied him; then We delivered him and those with him in the ark, and We made them successors, while We drowned those who belied Our signs. Behold then what like hath been the end of those who were warned.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: They denied him, but We delivered him, and those with him in the ship, and We made them generations replacing one after another, while We drowned those who belied Our Ayat (proofs, evidences, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.). Then see what was the end of those who were warned.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: But they denounced him, so We saved him and those with him in the Ark, and We made them successors, and We drowned those who rejected Our signs. So consider the fate of those who were warned.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: But they rejected Noah, calling him a liar. So We saved him and those who were with him in the Ark, and made them successors (to the authority in the land), and drowned all those who had rejected Our signs as false. Consider, then, the fate of those who had been warned (and still did not believe).

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: They denied him, but We delivered him, and those with him in the ship, and We made them generations, replacing one after another, while We drowned those who belied Our Ayat. Then see what was the end of those who were warned.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: But they denied him, so We saved him and those with him in the ship, and made them viceroys (in the earth), while We drowned those who denied Our revelations. See then the nature of the consequence for those who had been warned.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: But they impugned him. So We delivered him and those who were with him in the ark and We made them the successors, and We drowned those who denied Our signs. So observe how was the fate of those who were warned!

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: But they belied him. Therefore We saved him and those who were with him in the Ark, and We made them caliphs, and We drowned those who belied Our verses. See what was the end of those who were warned!

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And they denied him, so We saved him and those with him in the ship and made them successors, and We drowned those who denied Our signs. Then see how was the end of those who were warned.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: They rejected Noah. Then We saved him and his people in the Ark to make them the successors of the rest. The others, who had called Our revelations lies, were drowned. Consider the fate of those who (rejected) Our warnings!

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: But they rejected him, so We delivered him and those with him in the ark, and We made them rulers and drowned those who rejected Our communications; see then what was the end of the (people) warned.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Then they rejected him; then We delivered him and those with him in the Ark, and We made them successors; while We drowned the others who belied Our signs. Observe then the fate of those who had been forewarned.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: They rejected Him, but We delivered him, and those with him, in the Ark, and We made them inherit (the earth), while We overwhelmed in the flood those who rejected Our Signs. Then see what was the end of those who were warned (but heeded not)!

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: So adjudge between me and them conclusively, and save me and the believers who are with me."

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: “Therefore make a decisive judgement between me and them, and rescue me and the believers along with me.”

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: so give true deliverance between me and them, and deliver me and the believers that are with me.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: So decide Thou between me and them with a decision, and deliver me and those who are with me of the believers.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Therefore judge You between me and them, and save me and those of the believers who are with me."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: So judge between me and them decisively, and deliver me and the believers who are with me.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: So pass a clear judgement between me and them and rescue me and the believers with me."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "Therefore judge You between me and them, and save me and those of the believers who are with me."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Therefor judge Thou between us, a (conclusive) judgment, and save me and those believers who are with me.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: So open between me and them an opening, and save me and the believers who are with me'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Then judge between me and them with decisive judgement and save me and those with me of the believers."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Judge among us and save me and the believers with me".

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Therefore judge Thou between me and them with a (just) judgment, and deliver me and those who are with me of the believers.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: therefore, judge decisively between me and them; and save me and the believers who are with me."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "Judge Thou, then, between me and them openly, and deliver me and those of the Believers who are with me."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: So We delivered him and those with him in the loaded ark,

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: So We delivered him, and those with him, in the laden ship,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Wherefore We delivered him and those with him in the laden ark.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And We saved him and those with him in the laden ship.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: So We delivered him and those with him in the laden Ark.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Thereafter We rescued him and those who were with him in the laden Ark

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And We saved him and those with him in the laden ship.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And We saved him and those with him in the laden ship.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Thereupon We delivered him and those who were with him in the laden ark.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: We saved him and those who were with him in the laden ship,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: So We saved him and those with him in the laden ship.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We saved him and those who were with him in a fully laden Ark,

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: So We delivered him and those with him in the laden ark.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: So We saved him, and those who were with him in the laden ark,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: So We delivered him and those with him, in the Ark filled (with all creatures).

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And drowned the rest of them.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: Then after it, We drowned the rest.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: then afterwards We drowned the rest.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Then We drowned the rest thereafter.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Then We drowned the rest (disbelievers) thereafter.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Then We drowned the rest.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: and drowned the rest.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Then We drowned the rest thereafter.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Then afterward We drowned the others.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: afterwards, We drowned the rest.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Then We drowned thereafter the remaining ones.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: and drowned the others.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Then We drowned the rest afterwards

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: and drowned the rest.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Thereafter We drowned those who remained behind.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: But We saved him and those with him in the ark, and made it a sign for the creatures of the world.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Yet We delivered him, and those who were in the ship, and appointed it for a sign unto all beings.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Then We delivered him and those in the ark, and made it a sign unto the worlds.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Then We saved him and those with him in the ship, and made it (the ship) as an Ayah (a lesson, a warning, etc.) for the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns and all that exists).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: But We saved him, together with the company of the Ark, and We made it a sign for all peoples.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Then We rescued Noah together with the people in the Ark and made it (that is, the Ark) a lesson for all people.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Then We saved him and the Companions of the Boat, and made it an Ayah for all people.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And We rescued him and those with him in the ship, and made of it a portent for the peoples.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Then We delivered him and those who were in the Ark, and made it a sign for all the nations.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: But We saved him and all who were in the Ark, and made (the event) a sign to the worlds.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: But We saved him and the companions of the ship, and We made it a sign for the worlds.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We saved Noah and the people in the Ark and made (their case) a miracle for the world.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: So We delivered him and the inmates of the ark, and made it a sign to the nations.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: But We saved him and those who were with him in the Ark and made the event a sign for mankind.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: But We saved him and the companions of the Ark, and We made the (Ark) a Sign for all peoples!

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Remember Noah when he called to Us before this. We heard him and saved him and those with him from great distress;

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And Noah -- when he called before, and We answered him, and delivered him and his people from the great distress,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And Nuh! recall what time he cried aforetime; so we answered him and delivered him and his house hold from a calamity mighty.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And (remember) Nuh (Noah), when he cried (to Us) aforetime. We listened to his invocation and saved him and his family from great distress.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And Noah, when he called before. So We answered him, and delivered him and his family from the great disaster.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: We bestowed the same favour upon Noah. Recall, when he cried to Us before; We accepted his prayer and delivered him and his household from the great distress

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And (remember) Nuh, when he cried (to Us) aforetime. We answered to his invocation and saved him and his family from the great distress.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And Noah, when he cried of old, We heard his prayer and saved him and his household from the great affliction.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: And before that Noah, when he called out, We responded to him and delivered him and his family from the great agony.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And (remember) Noah, when he supplicated to Us, We answered him, and We saved him and his nation from great distress,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And [mention] Noah, when he called [to Allah] before [that time], so We responded to him and saved him and his family from the great flood.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We answered the prayer of Noah who had prayed to Us before and saved him and his followers from the great disaster.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And Nuh, when he cried aforetime, so We answered him, and delivered him and his followers from the great calamity.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Before him Noah cried out to Us, and We heard his prayer. We saved him and all his household from a great distress.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: (Remember) Noah, when he cried (to Us) aforetime: We listened to his (prayer) and delivered him and his family from great distress.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And We helped him against the people who rejected Our signs as lies. They were a wicked people indeed, so We drowned them one and all.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And We helped him against the people who denied Our signs; indeed they were evil people, We therefore drowned them all.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and We helped him against the people who cried lies to Our signs; surely they were an evil people, so We drowned them all together.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And We succoured him against the people who belied Our signs; verily they were a people evil, so we drowned them all.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: We helped him against people who denied Our Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.). Verily, they were a people given to evil. So We drowned them all.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And We supported him against the people who rejected Our signs. They were an evil people, so We drowned them all.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: and We helped him against a people who rejected Our signs as false. They were indeed an evil people and so We drowned them all.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: We helped him against the people who denied Our Ayat. Verily, they were a people given to evil. So We drowned them all.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And delivered him from the people who denied Our revelations. Lo! they were folk of evil, therefor did We drown them all.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: and helped him against the nation who had belied Our signs. They were an evil nation; We drowned them all.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And We saved him from the people who denied Our signs. Indeed, they were a people of evil, so We drowned them, all together.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We helped him against the people who said Our revelations were mere lies. They were a bad people and We drowned them all.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And We helped him against the people who rejected Our communications; surely they were an evil people, so We drowned them all.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We helped him against his people who rejected Our revelations. They were surely a wicked people, so We drowned them all.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: We helped him against people who rejected Our Signs: truly they were a people given to Evil: so We drowned them (in the Flood) all together.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: We had drowned the people of Noah when they had accused the apostles of lies, and turned them into an example for men. We have prepared a painful punishment for the wicked.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And the people of Noah, when they cried lies to the Messengers, We drowned them, and made them to be a sign to mankind; and We have prepared for the evildoers a painful chastisement.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And the people of Nuh! When they belied the apostles We drowned them, and made them a sign unto mankind. And We have gotten ready unto the wrong-doers a torment afflictive,

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And Nuh's (Noah) people, when they denied the Messengers We drowned them, and We made them as a sign for mankind. And We have prepared a painful torment for the Zalimun (polytheists and wrong-doers, etc).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And the people of Noah: when they rejected the messengers, We drowned them, and made them a lesson for mankind. We have prepared for the wrongdoers a painful retribution.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: The same was the case with the people of Noah when they charged the Messenger with imposture: We drowned them and made them a sign of warning for entire mankind, and We have prepared a painful chastisement for the unjust.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And Nuh's people, when they denied the Messengers, We drowned them, and We made them as a sign for mankind. And We have prepared a painful torment for the wrongdoers.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And Noah's folk, when they denied the messengers, We drowned them and made of them a portent for mankind. We have prepared a painful doom for evil-doers.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: And Noah’s people, We drowned them when they impugned the apostles, and We made them a sign for mankind, and We have prepared for the wrongdoers a painful punishment.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: The nation of Noah, We drowned them when they belied their Messenger, and made of them a sign to the nation. For the harmdoers We have prepared a painful punishment

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And the people of Noah - when they denied the messengers, We drowned them, and We made them for mankind a sign. And We have prepared for the wrongdoers a painful punishment.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We drowned the people of Noah because of their rejection of the Messengers and made them evidence of the Truth for mankind. We have prepared a painful torment for the unjust ones.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And the people of Nuh, when they rejected the apostles, We drowned them, and made them a sign for men, and We have prepared a painful punishment for the unjust;

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We drowned the people of Noah also when they rejected their messengers and We made them an example to all mankind. We have prepared a painful punishment for the wrongdoers,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And the people of Noah,- when they rejected the messengers, We drowned them, and We made them as a Sign for mankind; and We have prepared for (all) wrong-doers a grievous Penalty;-

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Afterwards We drowned the others.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Then afterwards We drowned the rest.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Then We drowned the others.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Then We drowned the other (disbelievers and polytheists, etc.).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Then We drowned the others.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Thereafter We caused the others to be drowned.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Then We drowned the others.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Then We did drown the others.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Then We drowned the rest.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Afterwards We drowned the others.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Then We drowned the disbelievers.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We drowned all the others (besides Noah and his people).

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Then We drowned the others

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We drowned the rest.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Then the rest we overwhelmed in the Flood.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: They were drowned because of their habitual sinfulness, and sent to Hell, and did not find any helper other than God.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: Because of their sins they were drowned and then put into the fire; so they did not find any supporter for themselves against Allah. (Punishment in the grave is proven by this verse.)

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And because of their transgressions they were drowned, and admitted into a Fire, for they found not, apart from God, any to help them.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And because of their misdeeds they were drowned, and then they were made to enter a Fire. Then they found not for themselves, beside Allah, any helpers.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Because of their sins they were drowned, then were made to enter the Fire, and they found none to help them instead of Allah.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Because of their wrongs, they were drowned, and were hurled into a Fire. They did not find apart from God any helpers.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And so they were drowned on account of their sins, and then cast into the Fire, and did not find any other than Allah, to come forth to their help.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Because of their sins they were drowned, then were made to enter the Fire. And they found none to help them instead of Allah.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Because of their sins they were drowned, then made to enter a Fire. And they found they had no helpers in place of Allah.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: So because of their sins they were drowned and admitted into the Fire. They found none to help them other than Allah.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Because of their sins they were drowned and put into the Fire, and they found not for themselves besides Allah [any] helpers.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Because of their sins, they were drowned and made to enter hell. They could find no one to help them besides God.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Because of their wrongs they were drowned, then made to enter fire, so they did not find any helpers besides Allah.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: They were drowned and sent to Hell for their misdeeds; they found no one to help them against God.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Because of their sins they were drowned (in the flood), and were made to enter the Fire (of Punishment): and they found- in lieu of Allah- none to help them.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: (So had We destroyed) the people of Noah before them: They were surely a sinful people.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And the people of Noah before; surely they were an ungodly people.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And the people of Nuh We destroyed aforetime; verily they were a people transgressing.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: (So were) the people of Nuh (Noah) before them. Verily, they were a people who were Fasiqun (rebellious, disobedient to Allah).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And before that, the people of Noah. They were immoral people.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Before all these We destroyed the people of Noah: they were a wicked people.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: (So were) the people of Nuh before them. Verily, they were a people who were rebellious.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And the folk of Noah aforetime. Lo! they were licentious folk.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: And the people of Noah aforetime. Indeed, they were a transgressing lot.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And before them the nation of Noah, indeed they were a debauched nation.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And [We destroyed] the people of Noah before; indeed, they were a people defiantly disobedient.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: The people of Noah who lived before them were also evil doing people.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And the people of Nuh before, surely they were a transgressing people.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: [We destroyed] the people of Noah before them. They were certainly a sinful people.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: So were the People of Noah before them for they wickedly transgressed.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Like the people of Noah before them, who were surely oppressors and rebellious;

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and the people of Noah before -- certainly they did exceeding evil, and were insolent

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And also the people of Nuh aforetime. Verily they were even greater wrong-doers and more contumacious.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And the people of Nuh (Noah) aforetime, verily, they were more unjust and more rebellious and transgressing [in disobeying Allah and His Messenger Nuh (Noah)].

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And the people of Noah before that; for they were most unjust and most oppressive.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: and that He it is Who destroyed the people of Noah before for they were much given to iniquity and transgression.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And the people of Nuh aforetime. Verily, they were more unjust and more rebellious and transgressing.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And the folk of Noah aforetime, Lo! they were more unjust and more rebellious;

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: and the people of Noah before that; indeed they were more unjust and rebellious;

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: and before them the nation of Noah, they exceeded in evil and were insolent.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And the people of Noah before. Indeed, it was they who were [even] more unjust and oppressing.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: and the people of Noah; they were the most unjust and rebellious people.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And the people of Nuh before; surely they were most unjust and inordinate;

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: and before them the people of Noah who were even more unjust and insolent;

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And before them, the people of Noah, for that they were (all) most unjust and most insolent transgressors,

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And We left it as a sign. Is there anyone who will be warned?

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And We left it for a sign. Is there any that will remember?

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And assuredly We left it for a sign. Is there then anyone who would be admonished?

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And indeed, We have left this as a sign, then is there any that will remember (or receive admonition)?

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And We left it as a sign. Is there anyone who would take heed?

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: And We left the Ark as a Sign. Is there, then, any who will take heed?

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And indeed, We have left this as a sign. Then is there any that will remember

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And verily We left it as a token; but is there any that remembereth?

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Certainly We have left it as a sign; so is there anyone who will be admonished?

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: We have left it as a sign. Is there any that will remember?

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And We left it as a sign, so is there any who will remember?

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We made (the story of Noah) as evidence of the Truth. However, is there anyone who would take heed?

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And certainly We left it as a sign, but is there anyone who

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We have left this as a sign: but will anyone take heed?

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And We have left this as a Sign (for all time): then is there any that will receive admonition?

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: How was then My punishment and My commination!

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: So how did My punishment turn out, and My threats?

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: How then were My chastisement and My warnings?

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: So how dreadful have been My torment and My warning!

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Then how (terrible) was My Torment and My Warnings?

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: So how were My punishment and My warnings?

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: So how awesome were My chastisement and My warnings!

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Then how (terrible) was My torment and My warnings

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Then see how (dreadful) was My punishment after My warnings!

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: How then were My punishment and My warnings!

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And how [severe] were My punishment and warning.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: How terrible was My torment and the result of (their disregard) of My warning.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: How (great) was then My punishment and My warning!

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: How terrible then was My punishment and My warning.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: But how (terrible) was My Penalty and My Warning?

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: There were surely signs in this; We will surely put you to the test.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Surely in that are signs, and surely We put to the test.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Verily in that are signs; verily We have ever been provers.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
