translation.text: Verily, in this [what We did as regards drowning of the people of Nuh (Noah)], there are indeed Ayat (proofs, evidences, lessons, signs, etc. for men to understand), for sure We are ever putting (men) to the test.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Surely in that are signs. We are always testing.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: There are great Signs in this story; and surely We do put people to test.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Verily, in this, there are indeed Ayat, for sure We are ever putting (men) to the test.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Lo! herein verily are portents, for lo! We are ever putting (mankind) to the test.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: There are indeed signs in this; and indeed, We have been testing.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Surely, in that are signs, and surely We will put (nations) to the test.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Indeed in that are signs, and indeed, We are ever testing [Our servants].

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: In this story there is enough evidence (of the Truth); thus do We try (mankind)

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Most surely there are signs in this, and most surely We are ever trying (men).

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Surely there are clear signs in that. In this way We put people to the test.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Verily in this there are Signs (for men to understand); (thus) do We try (men).

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: O you, the offspring of those We bore (in the ark) with Noah, he was indeed a grateful votary.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: The seed of those We bore with Noah; he was a thankful servant.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: O progeny of those whom We bare with Nuh: verily he was a bondman grateful.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "O offspring of those whom We carried (in the ship) with Nuh (Noah)! Verily, he was a grateful slave."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: The descendants of those We carried with Noah. He was an appreciative servant.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: You are the descendants of those whom We carried (in the Ark) with Noah. He was truly a thankful servant.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "O offspring of those whom We carried (in the ship) with Nuh! Verily, he was a grateful servant."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: (They were) the seed of those whom We carried (in the ship) along with Noah. Lo! he was a grateful slave.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: descendants of those whom We carried [in the ark] with Noah. Indeed, he was a grateful servant.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: (You are) the descendants of those whom We carried (in the Ark) with Noah. He was a truly thankful worshiper'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: O descendants of those We carried [in the ship] with Noah. Indeed, he was a grateful servant.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: (We made it a guide for) the offspring of those whom We carried in the Ark with Noah, a thankful servant (of God).

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: The offspring of those whom We bore with Nuh; surely he was a grateful servant.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: you who are the descendants of those whom We carried in the Ark with Noah. He was a truly thankful servant."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: O ye that are sprung from those whom We carried (in the Ark) with Noah! Verily he was a devotee most grateful.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Noah had verily called to Us. And how gracious was He who answered (him).

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Noah called to Us; and how excellent were the Answerers!

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And assuredly Nuh cried Unto us; and We are the Best of answerers!

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And indeed Nuh (Noah) invoked Us, and We are the Best of those who answer (the request).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And Noah called out to Us, and We are the Best of responders.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Noah had called upon Us (earlier). See, how excellent We were in answering him!

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And indeed Nuh invoked Us, and We are the best of those who answer.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And Noah verily prayed unto Us, and gracious was the Hearer of his prayer

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Certainly Noah called out to Us, and how well did We respond!

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Noah called to Us, and We are the Best to answer.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And Noah had certainly called Us, and [We are] the best of responders.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Noah called for help. How blessed was the answer which he received.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And Nuh did certainly call upon Us, and most excellent answerer of prayer are We.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Noah cried to Us, and how excellent was Our response!

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: (In the days of old), Noah cried to Us, and We are the best to hear prayer.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: We saved him and his family from the great affliction,

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And We rescued him and his household from the great calamity.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: And We delivered him and his people from the great distress,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And We delivered him and his people from the great affliction.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And We rescued him and his family from the great distress (i.e. drowning),

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And We saved him and his family from the great calamity.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: We delivered him and his household from the great calamity;

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And We rescued him and his family from the great distress,

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And We saved him and his household from the great distress,

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: We saved him and his people from the great distress,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And We saved him and his family from the great affliction.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We rescued him and his people from the greatest affliction

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And We delivered him and his followers from the mighty distress.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We saved him and his people from great distress,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And We delivered him and his people from the Great Calamity,

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And made his progeny survive,

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And We preserved his descendants.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and We made his seed the survivors,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And his offspring! them We made the survivors.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And, his progeny, them We made the survivors (i.e. Shem, Ham and Japheth).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And We made his descendants the survivors.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: and made his offspring the only ones to survive,

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And, his progeny, them We made the survivors.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And made his seed the survivors,

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: and We made his offspring the survivors.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And We made his descendants those remaining [on the earth]

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: and We made his offspring the only survivors.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And We made his offspring the survivors.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: and We made his offspring the only survivors.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And made his progeny to endure (on this earth);

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And left (his name) for posterity.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And We kept his praise among the latter generations.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and left for him among the later folk

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And We left for him among the posterity.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And left for him (a goodly remembrance) among generations to come in later times:

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And We left mention of him among those who succeeded.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: and We established for him a good name among posterity.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And left for him (a goodly remembrance) among the later generations

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And left for him among the later folk (the salutation):

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And We let it remain upon him in the latter:

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And left for him [favorable mention] among later generations:

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We perpetuated his praise in later generations.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And We perpetuated to him (praise) among the later generations.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: We left mention of him among later generations.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And We left (this blessing) for him among generations to come in later times:

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Peace on Noah among all men.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: 'Peace be upon Noah among all beings!'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Peace be on Nuh among the worlds.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Salamun (peace) be upon Nuh (Noah) (from Us) among the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns and all that exists)!"

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Peace be upon Noah among all people.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Peace be upon Noah among all the nations.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "Salam (peace!) be upon Nuh among the all creatures!"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Peace be unto Noah among the peoples!

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: ‘Peace to Noah, throughout the nations!’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: 'Peace be upon Noah among all the worlds'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: "Peace upon Noah among the worlds."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Peace be with Noah among all men in the worlds.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Peace and salutation to Nuh among the nations.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Peace be upon Noah among all the peoples!

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "Peace and salutation to Noah among the nations!"

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: That is how We reward those who do good.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: This is how We reward the virtuous.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Even so We recompense the good-doers;

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Verily We! thus We recompense the well-doers.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Verily, thus We reward the Muhsinun (good-doers - see V. 2:112).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: We thus reward the righteous.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Thus do We reward all those who do good.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Verily, thus We reward the gooddoers.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Lo! thus do We reward the good.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: As such We recompense the gooddoers,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Indeed, We thus reward the doers of good.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Thus do We reward the righteous ones.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Thus do We surely reward the doers of good.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: That is how We recompense the righteous:

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Thus indeed do we reward those who do right.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Surely he was among Our faithful creatures.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: he was among Our believing servants.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Verily he was of Our bondmen believing.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Verily, he [Nuh (Noah)] was one of Our believing slaves.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: He was one of Our believing servants.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Surely he was one of Our truly believing servants.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Verily, he [Nuh] was one of Our believing servants.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Lo! he is one of Our believing slaves.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: He is indeed one of Our faithful servants.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: he was one of Our believing worshipers.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Indeed, he was of Our believing servants.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: He was one of Our believing servants.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Surely he was of Our believing servants.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: he was truly one of Our faithful servants.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: For he was one of our believing Servants.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And Abraham indeed was of his inducement,

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Of his party was also Abraham;

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And verily of his sect was Ibrahim.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And, verily, among those who followed his [Nuh's (Noah)] way (Islamic Monotheism) was Ibrahim (Abraham).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Of his kind was Abraham.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Abraham was on the self-same way (as Noah).

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And verily, among those who followed his way was Ibrahim.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And lo! of his persuasion verily was Abraham

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Indeed Abraham was among his followers,

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Of his party was Abraham.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And indeed, among his kind was Abraham,

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Abraham was one of his followers.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And most surely Ibrahim followed his way.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Abraham was of the same faith:

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Verily among those who followed his Way was Abraham.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: After them We raised a new generation.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Thereafter, after them, We produced another generation,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Then, after them We brought forth anot her generation.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Then, after them, We created another generation.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Then, after them, We established another generation.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Then, after them, We brought forth another generation;

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Then, after them, We created another generation.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Then, after them, We brought forth another generation;

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Then after them We brought forth another generation,

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Then We produced after them another generation

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Then We produced after them a generation of others.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We brought another generation into existence after the people of Noah.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Then We raised up after them another generation.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Then We raised another generation after them,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Then We raised after them another generation.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Afterwards We sent many messengers who brought clear proofs to their peoples. But they were not prepared to believe what they had once denied. That is how We seal the hearts of the iniquitous.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Then We sent forth, after him, Messengers to their people, and they brought them the clear signs; but they were not men to believe in that they had cried lies to before. So We seal the hearts of the transgressors.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Then, We raised after him other apostles to their people, and they brought them evidences, but they were not such as to believe that which they had belied afore. Thus We seal the hearts of the transgressors.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Then after him We sent Messengers to their people, they brought them clear proofs, but they would not believe what they had already rejected beforehand. Thus We seal the hearts of the transgressors (those who disbelieve in the Oneness of Allah and disobey Him).

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Then, after him, We sent messengers to their people. They came to them with the clear proofs, but they would not believe in anything they had already rejected. Thus We set a seal on the hearts of the hostile.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Then We sent forth after him other Messengers, each one to his people. They brought to them clear signs, but they were not such as to believe in what they had rejected earlier as false. Thus do We seal the hearts of those who transgress.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Then after him We sent Messengers to their people. They brought them clear proofs, but they would not believe what they had already rejected beforehand. Thus We seal the hearts of the transgressors.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Then, after him, We sent messengers unto their folk, and they brought them clear proofs. But they were not ready to believe in that which they before denied. Thus print We on the hearts of the transgressors.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Then after him We sent [other] apostles to their people. They brought them manifest proofs, but they would not believe something they had denied before. Thus do We seal the hearts of the transgressors.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: After him We sent Messengers to their nation. They brought them clear signs, but they were not of those to believe because they had belied it previously. SoWe seal the hearts of the evildoers.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Then We sent after him messengers to their peoples, and they came to them with clear proofs. But they were not to believe in that which they had denied before. Thus We seal over the hearts of the transgressors

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: After (Noah) We sent other Messengers to their people with clear authoritative evidence proving their prophetic claims). But how could the people believe what they had previously called lies? Thus do We seal the hearts of the transgressors.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Then did We raise up after him apostles to their people, so they came to them with clear arguments, but they would not believe in what they had rejected before; thus it is that We set seals upon the hearts of those who exceed the limits.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: After him We sent other messengers to their respective peoples, and they brought them clear proofs. But they would not believe in the truth, because they had rejected it before. Thus We seal up the hearts of the transgressors.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Then after him We sent (many) messengers to their peoples: they brought them Clear Signs, but they would not believe what they had already rejected beforehand. Thus do We seal the hearts of the transgressors.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: "How many generations have We laid low after Noah, for your Lord knows and notices well enough the sins of His creatures.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: How many generations We have destroyed after Noah! Thy Lord suffices as one who is aware of and sees the sins of His servants.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: How many a generation have We destroyed after Nuh; and sufficeth for the offences of His bondmen thy Lord as the Aware, the Beholder.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And how many generations have We destroyed after Nuh (Noah)! And Sufficient is your Lord as an All-Knower and All-Beholder of the sins of His slaves.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: How many generations have We destroyed after Noah? Your Lord is sufficient as Knower and Beholder of the sins of his servants.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Many a generation has been destroyed by Our command since Noah's time. Your Lord is well aware and fully observant of the sins of His servants.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And how many generations have We destroyed after Nuh! And sufficient is your Lord as All-Knower and Seer of the sins of His servants.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: How many generations have We destroyed since Noah! And Allah sufficeth as Knower and Beholder of the sins of His slaves.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: How many generations We have destroyed since Noah! Your Lord is sufficient as [a witness who is] well aware and percipient of His servants’ sins.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: How many generations have We destroyed since Noah! Your Lord suffices as One who is Aware of and sees the sins of His worshipers.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And how many have We destroyed from the generations after Noah. And sufficient is your Lord, concerning the sins of His servants, as Acquainted and Seeing.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We have destroyed many generations after the time of Noah. Your Lord is All knowing and Well Aware of the sins of His servants.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And how many of the generations did We destroy after Nuh! and your Lord is sufficient as Knowing and Seeing with regard to His servants' faults.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: How many generations have We destroyed since Noah's time. Your Lord is well aware of the sins of His servants and observes them all.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: How many generations have We destroyed after Noah? and enough is thy Lord to note and see the sins of His servants.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Has not the account of those before them come to them, -- of the people of Noah and 'Ad and Thamud, of Abraham and Midian, and all the habitations that were destroyed? Their apostles had come to them with clear proofs; and God did not surely wrong them, they wronged themselves.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Has there not come to you the tidings of those who were before you -- the people of Noah, Ad, Thamood, the people of Abraham, the men of Midian and the subverted cities? Their Messengers came to them with the clear signs God would not wrong them, but themselves they wronged.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Have not come to them the tidings of those before them: the people of Nuh and 'Ad and Thamud and the people of Ibrahim and the dwellers of Madyan and of the overturned cities! There came unto them their apostles with evidences. Wherefore Allah was not one to wrong them, but themselves they were wont to wrong.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Has not the story reached them of those before them? - The people of Nuh (Noah), 'Ad, and Thamud, the people of Ibrahim (Abraham), the dwellers of Madyan (Midian) and the cities overthrown [i.e. the people to whom Lout (Lot) preached], to them came their Messengers with clear proofs. So it was not Allah Who wronged them, but they used to wrong themselves.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Have they not heard the stories of those before them? The people of Noah, and Aad, and Thamood; and the people of Abraham, and the inhabitants of Median, and the Overturned Cities? Their messengers came to them with the clear proofs. God never wronged them, but they used to wrong their own selves.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Have they not heard the accounts of those who came before them - of the people of Noah and 'Ad and Thamud, and the people of Abraham and the dwellers of Madyan (Midian), and the cities that were overturned? Their Messengers came to them with Clear Signs. Then, it was not Allah Who caused them any wrong; they rather wronged themselves.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Has not the story reached them of those before them -- The people of Nuh,`Ad, Thamud, the people of Ibrahim, the dwellers of Madyan and the overturned cities; to them came their Messengers with clear proofs. So it was not Allah Who wronged them, but they used to wrong themselves.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Hath not the fame of those before them reached them - the folk of Noah, A'ad, Thamud, the folk of Abraham, the dwellers of Midian and the disasters (which befell them)? Their messengers (from Allah) came unto them with proofs (of Allah's Sovereignty). So Allah surely wronged them not, but they did wrong themselves.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Has there not come to them the account of those who were before them—the people of Noah, ‘Ad, and Tham£d, and the people of Abraham, the inhabitants of Midian, and the towns that were overturned? Their apostles brought them manifest proofs. So it was not Allah who wronged them, but it was they who used to wrong themselves.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Have they not heard the news of those who have gone before them? The news of the nations of Noah, Aad and Thamood, of Abraham's nation, and the people of Midian and the ruined cities? Their Messengers showed them clear signs. Allah did not harm them, but they harmed themselves.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Has there not reached them the news of those before them - the people of Noah and [the tribes of] 'Aad and Thamud and the people of Abraham and the companions of Madyan and the towns overturned? Their messengers came to them with clear proofs. And Allah would never have wronged them, but they were wronging themselves.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Have they not heard the stories of the people of Noah, the tribe of Ad, Thamud, the people of Abraham, the dwellers of the city of Midian, and those of the Subverted Cities? God's Messengers came to each of them with miracles. God did not do any injustice to them, but they wronged themselves.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Has not the news of those before them come to them; of the people of Nuh and Ad and Samood, and the people of Ibrahim and the dwellers of Madyan and the overthrown cities; their apostles came to them with clear arguments; so it was not Allah Who should do them injustice, but they were unjust to themselves.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Have they never heard the stories about their predecessors, the peoples of Noah, 'Ad, Thamud, Abraham, Midian, and of the ruined cities? Their messengers brought them clear evidence of the truth. It was not God who wronged them; they wronged themselves.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Hath not the story reached them of those before them?- the People of Noah, and 'Ad, and Thamud; the People of Abraham, the men of Midian, and the cities overthrown. To them came their messengers with clear signs. It is not Allah Who wrongs them, but they wrong their own souls.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Has not the news of those before you, the people of Noah and 'Ad and Thamud, and those who came after them, come to you? None knows (about) them except God. Their apostles came to them with clear proofs, but they tried to silence them and said: "We do not believe in what you have been sent with, and we are in doubt of what you call us to, about which we are in disquiet.'

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Has there not come to you the tidings of those who were before you -- the people of Noah, Ad, Thamood, and of those after them whom none knows but God? Their Messengers came to them with the clear signs; but they thrust their hands into their mouths, saying, 'We certainly disbelieve in the Message you have been sent with, and we are in doubt, concerning that you call us unto, disquieting.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Hath not the tidings come to you of those before you: the people of Nuh and the 'Aad and the Thamud and those after them! None knoweth them save Allah. There came unto them their apostles with the evidences, but they put their hands to their mouths, and said: verily we disbelieve in that where- with ye have been sent, and verily regarding that to which ye call Us We are in doubt disquieting.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Has not the news reached you, of those before you, the people of Nuh (Noah), and 'Ad, and Thamud? And those after them? None knows them but Allah. To them came their Messengers with clear proofs, but they put their hands in their mouths (biting them from anger) and said: "Verily, we disbelieve in that with which you have been sent, and we are really in grave doubt as to that to which you invite us (i.e. Islamic Monotheism)."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Has not the story reached you, of those before you, the people of Noah, and Aad, and Thamood—and those after them? None knows them except God. Their messengers came to them with the clear proofs, but they tried to silence them, and said, “We reject what you are sent with, and we are in serious doubt regarding what you are calling us to.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Have the accounts of your predecessors not reached you: the people of Noah, the Ad, the Thamud, and those who came after them - they whose number is not known to any except Allah? Their Messengers came to them with Clear Signs, but they thrust their hands in their mouths, and said: "We do surely reject the Message you have brought, and we are in disquieting doubt about what you are summoning us to."

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Has not the news reached you, of those before you, the people of Nuh, `Ad, and Thamud And those after them None knows them but Allah. To them came their Messengers with clear proofs, but they put their hands in their mouths and said: "Verily, we disbelieve in that with which you have been sent, and we are really in grave doubt as to that to which you invite us."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Hath not the history of those before you reached you: the folk of Noah, and (the tribes of) A'ad and Thamud, and those after them? None save Allah knoweth them. Their messengers came unto them with clear proofs, but they thrust their hands into their mouths, and said: Lo! we disbelieve in that wherewith ye have been sent, and lo! we are in grave doubt concerning that to which ye call us.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Has there not come to you the account of those who were before you—the people of Noah, ‘Ad and Thamud, and those who were after them, whom no one knows [well] except Allah? Their apostles brought them manifest proofs, but they did not respond to them, and said, ‘We disbelieve in what you have been sent with. Indeed we have grave doubts concerning that to which you invite us.’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Have you not heard what befell the nations that have gone before you? The nations of Noah, Aad, and Thamood, and those who came after? None knows them but Allah. Their Messengers came to them with clear signs, but they thrust their hands into their mouths and said: 'Without doubt we disbelieve the Message you have been sent with. That which you call us to is disquieting'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Has there not reached you the news of those before you - the people of Noah and 'Aad and Thamud and those after them? No one knows them but Allah. Their messengers brought them clear proofs, but they returned their hands to their mouths and said, "Indeed, we disbelieve in that with which you have been sent, and indeed we are, about that to which you invite us, in disquieting doubt."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Have you (believers) ever heard the news about those who lived before you, like the people of Noah, Ad, Thamud, and those who lived after them? No one knows about them except God. Messengers were sent to them with miracles, but they put their hands to their mouths and said, "We do not believe in whatever you preach and we are also doubtful and uncertain about that to which you invite us."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Has not the account reached you of those before you, of the people of Nuh and Ad and Samood, and those after them? None knows them but Allah. Their apostles come to them with clear arguments, but they thrust their hands into their mouths and said: Surely we deny that with which you are sent, and most surely we are in serious doubt as to that to which you invite us.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Has not news come to you concerning those who preceded you, such as Noah's people, and the 'Ad and Thamud, as well as those who came after them? Only God knows who they were. The messengers came to them with clear signs, but they put their hands to their mouths saying, "We deny the message you have been sent with. We have grave doubts about what you are inviting us to do."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Has not the story reached you, (O people!), of those who (went) before you? - of the people of Noah, and 'Ad, and Thamud? - And of those who (came) after them? None knows them but Allah. To them came messengers with Clear (Signs); but they put their hands up to their mouths, and said: "We do deny (the mission) on which ye have been sent, and we are really in suspicious (disquieting) doubt as to that to which ye invite us."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: If they accuse you of falsehood, (remember that) the people of Noah, 'Ad and Thamud had accused (their apostles) before,

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: If they cry lies to thee, so too before them the people of Noah cried lies, and Ad and Thamood,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And if they belie thee, then surely there have belied before them the people of Nuh and the 'Aad and the Thamud.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And if they belie you (O Muhammad SAW), so were belied the Prophets before them, (by) the people of Nuh (Noah), 'Ad and Thamud,

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: If they deny you—before them the people of Noah, and Aad, and Thamood also denied.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: (O Prophet), if they give the lie to you, then before them the people of 'Ad and Thamud, also gave the lie (to the Prophets),

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And if they deny you, so did deny before them the people of Nuh, `Ad and Thamud.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: If they deny thee (Muhammad), even so the folk of Noah, and (the tribes of) A'ad and Thamud, before thee, denied (Our messengers);

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: If they impugn you, the people of Noah had impugned before them and ‘Ad and Thamud,

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: If they belie you, so too before them, the nation of Noah belied, and Aad and Thamood

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And if they deny you, [O Muhammad] - so, before them, did the people of Noah and 'Aad and Thamud deny [their prophets],

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: If they have called you, (Muhammad), a liar, (remember that) the people of Noah, Ad, Thamud,

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And if they reject you, then already before you did the people of Nuh and Ad and Samood reject (prophets).

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: If your opponents deny you, remember that, before them, the people of Noah and the tribes of 'Ad and Thamud denied their messengers likewise.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: If they treat thy (mission) as false, so did the peoples before them (with their prophets),- the People of Noah, and 'Ad and Thamud;

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And the people of Abraham and Lot,

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And (so did) the people of Ibrahim and the people of Lut.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and the people of Abraham, the people of Lot,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And the people of Ibrahim and the people of Lut.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And the people of Ibrahim (Abraham) and the people of Lout (Lot),

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And the people of Abraham, and the people of Lot.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: and so too did the people of Abraham and the people of Lot;

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And the people of Ibrahim and the people of Lut,

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And the folk of Abraham and the folk of Lot;

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: and the nation of Abraham and the nation of Lot;

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And the people of Abraham and the people of Lot

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: and the people of Abraham, Lot,

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And the people of Ibrahim and the people of Lut,

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: So did the people of Abraham and the people of Lot,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Those of Abraham and Lut;

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: The people of Noah had denied before them, and many factions after them. Every nation has intrigued against its apostle and afflicted him, and argued with false arguments to condemn the truth. Then I seized them. How was then My retribution!

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: The people of Noah before them also cried lies, and the parties after them; every nation purposed against their Messenger to seize him, and disputed with falsehood that they might rebut thereby the truth. Then I seized them; and how was My retribution!

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: The people of Nuh and the confederates after them belied their apostles before these, and each disbelieving community advanced toward their apostle that they may lay hold of him and disputed with vain speech that they may confute the truth thereby. Wherefore I laid hold of them; and how terrible was My chastisement!

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: The people of Nuh (Noah) and the confederates after them denied (their Messengers) before these, and every (disbelieving) nation plotted against their Messenger to seize him, and disputed by means of falsehood to refute therewith the truth. So I seized them (with punishment), and how (terrible) was My punishment!

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Before them the people of Noah rejected the truth, as did the confederates after them. Every community plotted against their messenger, to capture him. And they argued with falsehood, to defeat with it the truth. But I seized them. What a punishment it was!

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Before them the people of Noah also gave the lie (to Messengers), and so did many parties after them. Each nation sallied forth against its Messenger to seize him, and they disputed with false arguments seeking therewith to repudiate the Truth. Then I seized them; and behold, how woeful was My retribution!

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: The people of Nuh and the groups after them denied before these; and every nation plotted against their Messenger to seize him, and disputed by means of falsehood to refute therewith the truth. So I seized them, and how was My punishment!

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: The folk of Noah and the factions after them denied (their messengers) before these, and every nation purposed to seize their messenger and argued falsely, (thinking) thereby to refute the Truth. Then I seized them, and how (awful) was My punishment.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: The people of Noah denied before them and the [heathen] factions [who came] after them. Every nation attempted to lay hands on their apostle, and disputed erroneously to refute the truth. Then I seized them; so how was My retribution?!

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: The nation of Noah before them belied, and so did the parties after them. Every nation strove against their Messenger to seize him, disputing with false arguments to refute the truth. Then I seized them, how was My punishment!

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: The people of Noah denied before them and the [disbelieving] factions after them, and every nation intended [a plot] for their messenger to seize him, and they disputed by [using] falsehood to [attempt to] invalidate thereby the truth. So I seized them, and how [terrible] was My penalty.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: The people of Noah who lived before and the Confederate tribes who lived after them rejected Our revelations. Every nation schemed against its Messengers to seize them and disputed against them to defeat the truth. But torment struck them and how terrible was their retribution!

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: The people of Nuh and the parties after them rejected (prophets) before them, and every nation purposed against their apostle to destroy him, and they disputed by means of the falsehood that they might thereby render null the truth, therefore I destroyed them; how was then My retribution!

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: The people of Noah and later factions also rejected the truth and every community plotted against the messenger sent to them, aiming to lay hands on him, and they contended [against his message] with fallacious arguments, so that they might defeat the truth, therefore I seized them. How terrible was My punishment.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: But (there were people) before them, who denied (the Signs),- the People of Noah, and the Confederates (of Evil) after them; and every People plotted against their prophet, to seize him, and disputed by means of vanities, therewith to condemn the Truth; but it was I that seized them! and how (terrible) was My Requital!

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Like the people of Noah, 'Ad and Thamud, and those that came after them. God does not want to be unjust to His creatures.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: the like of the case of Noah's people, Ad, Thamood, and those after them; and God desires not wrong for His servants.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Like the wont of the people of Nuh and A'ad and Thamud and those after them, and God intendeth not any wrong Unto His bondmen.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "Like the fate of the people of Nuh (Noah), and 'Ad, and Thamud and those who came after them. And Allah wills no injustice for (His) slaves.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Like the fate of the people of Noah, and Aad, and Thamood, and those after them. God wants no injustice for the servants.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: like the day that overtook the people of Noah and Ad and Thamud, and those who came after them. Allah does not wish to subject His servants to any injustice.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "Like the end of the people of Nuh, and `Ad, and Thamud and those who came after them. And Allah wills no injustice for (His) servants."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: A plight like that of Noah's folk, and A'ad and Thamud, and those after them, and Allah willeth no injustice for (His) slaves.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: like the case of the people of Noah, of Ad and Thamud, and those who came after them, and Allah does not desire any wrong for [His] servants.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: or, something similar to the circumstances of the nations of Noah, Aad, and Thamood, and those who came after them. Allah does not want to wrong His worshipers.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Like the custom of the people of Noah and of 'Aad and Thamud and those after them. And Allah wants no injustice for [His] servants.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: people of Noah, Ad, Thamud, and those after them. God did not want injustice for His servants.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: The like of what befell the people of Nuh and Ad and Samood and those after them, and Allah does not desire injustice for (His) servants;

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: like the fate of the people of Noah, 'Ad, Thamud, and those who came after them -- God never wills injustice on His creatures.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "Something like the fate of the People of Noah, the 'Ad, and the Thamud, and those who came after them: but Allah never wishes injustice to his Servants.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Even before them the people of Noah, 'Ad, the mighty Pharoah,

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Cried lies before them the people of Noah, and Ad, and Pharaoh, he of the tent-pegs,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Before them there have belied the people of Nuh and the 'Aad, and Fir'awn the owner of the stakes.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Before them (were many who) belied Messengers, the people of Nuh (Noah); and 'Ad; and Fir'aun (Pharaoh) the man of stakes (with which he used to punish the people),

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Before them the people of Noah denied the truth; as did Aad, and Pharaoh of the Stakes.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Before them the people of Noah, Ad, and Pharaoh of the tent-pegs gave the lie (to the Messengers)

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Before them denied -- the people of Nuh; and `Ad; and Fir`awn the man of stakes,

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: The folk of Noah before them denied (their messenger) and (so did the tribe of) A'ad, and Pharaoh firmly planted,

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Before them Noah’s people impugned [their apostle] and [so did the people of] ‘Ad, and Pharaoh, the Impaler [of his victims],

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Before them the nations of Noah, Aad and Pharaoh, and he of the tentpegs belied,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: The people of Noah denied before them, and [the tribe of] 'Aad and Pharaoh, the owner of stakes,

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: The people of Noah, Ad and the dominating Pharaoh had rejected Our revelations.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: The people of Nuh and Ad, and Firon, the lord of spikes, rejected (apostles) before them.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Before them the people of Noah denied the truth, as did the 'Ad and Pharaoh of the Stakes,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Before them (were many who) rejected messengers,- the people of Noah, and 'Ad, and Pharaoh, the Lord of Stakes,

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And the Thamud, the people of Lot, as well as the dwellers of the Wood, had denied. These were the hordes.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And the tribe of Thamud, and the people of Lut, and the People of the Woods; these are the groups.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and Thamood, and the people of Lot, and the men of the Thicket - those were the parties;

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And the Thamud, and the people of Lut, and the dwellers of the wood; these were the confederates.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And Thamud, and the people of Lout (Lot), and the dwellers of the wood; such were the confederates.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And Thamood, and the people of Lot, and the dwellers of the Woods—these were the confederates.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: and so did Thamud and the people of Lot and the people of Aykah. These were all leagued together.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And Thamud, and the people of Lut, and the Dwellers of Al-Aykah; such were the Confederates.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And (the tribe of) Thamud, and the folk of Lot, and the dwellers in the wood: these were the factions.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Thamood, the nation of Lot and the dwellers of the Thicket such were the confederates.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And [the tribe of] Thamud and the people of Lot and the companions of the thicket. Those are the companies.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: So also did the people of Thamud, Lot, and the dwellers of the Forest.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And Samood and the people of Lut and the dwellers of the thicket; these were the parties.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: and the tribe of Thamud, and the people of Lot, and the dwellers of the Wood -- these were the confederates.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: And Thamud, and the people of Lut, and the Companions of the Wood; - such were the Confederates.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: The people of Noah, Ar-Rass and Thamud denied before you,

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Cried lies before them the people of Noah and the men of Er-Rass, and Thamood,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And before them the people of Nuh belied, and so did the dwellers Of Rass and the Thamud.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Denied before them (i.e. these pagans of Makkah who denied you, O Muhammad SAW) the people of Nuh (Noah), and the dwellers of Rass, and the Thamud,

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Before them the people of Noah denied the truth, and so did the dwellers of Russ, and Thamood.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: In the past Noah's people, and the people of Rass and Thamud gave the lie (to Messengers),

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Denied before them the people of Nuh, and the Dwellers of Rass, and Thamud,

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: The folk of Noah denied (the truth) before them, and (so did) the dwellers at Ar-Rass and (the tribe of) Thamud,

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: The people of Noah denied before them, and [so did] the people of Rass and Thamud,

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Before them, the nation of Noah, the nation of ArRass belied and so did Thamood

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: The people of Noah denied before them, and the companions of the well and Thamud

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: The people of Noah, dwellers of the Ra's, Thamud,

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: (Others) before them rejected (prophets): the people of Nuh and the dwellers of Ar-Rass and Samood,

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Before them, the people of Noah and the people of Rass denied this truth; and so did the people of Thamud.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Before them was denied (the Hereafter) by the People of Noah, the Companions of the Rass, the Thamud,

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: As did the people of 'Ad and Pharaoh and the brethren of Lot,

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And the tribe of A’ad, and Firaun, and the fellowmen of Lut.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: and Ad and Pharaoh, the brothers of Lot,

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And the A-ad, and Fir'awn. and the brethren of Lut.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And 'Ad, and Fir'aun (Pharaoh), and the brethren of Lout (Lot),

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And Aad, and Pharaoh, and the brethren of Lot.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: and so did 'Ad, and Pharaoh and Lot's brethren,

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And `Ad, and Fir`awn, and the brethren of Lut,

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And (the tribe of) A'ad, and Pharaoh, and the brethren of Lot,

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: and Aad, Pharaoh and the brothers of Lot,

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And 'Aad and Pharaoh and the brothers of Lot

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Ad, the Pharaoh, Lot,

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And Ad and Firon and Lut's brethren,

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: And the tribe of 'Ad, and Pharaoh, and the brethren of Lot,

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: The 'Ad, Pharaoh, the brethren of Lut,

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And the dwellers of the Wood and people of Tubba'. Each of them denied the apostles. So My threat became a reality.

translation.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translation.text: And the Dwellers of the Woods, and the people of Tubb’a; each one of them one denied the Noble Messengers, so My promised punishment proved true.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: the men of the Thicket, the people of Tubba'. Every one cried lies to the Messengers, and My threat came true.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And the dwellers in the wood, and the people of Tubba. Each one belied the apostles, wherefore fulfilled was My judgement.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: And the dwellers of the Wood, and the people of Tubba'; everyone of them denied (their) Messengers, so My Threat took effect.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: And the Dwellers of the Woods, and the people of Tubba. They all rejected the messengers, so My threat came true.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: and the people of Aykah, and the people of Tubba'. They all gave the lie to the Messengers. Thereafter My threat of chastisement against them was fulfilled.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: And the Dwellers of Al-Aykah, and the people of Tubba`. Everyone of them denied (their) Messengers, so My threat took effect.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And the dwellers in the wood, and the folk of Tubb'a: every one denied their messengers, therefor My threat took effect.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: the dwellers of the Thicket and the nation of Tubba', all belied their Messengers, therefore My threat was realized.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And the companions of the thicket and the people of Tubba'. All denied the messengers, so My threat was justly fulfilled.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: dwellers of the forest, and the people of Tubba had all rejected the Prophets. Thus, they became subject to Our torment.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And the dwellers of the grove and the people of Tuba; all rejected the apostles, so My threat came to pass.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: and the dwellers of the Wood, and the people of Tubba': every one denied their messengers, and so My warning came true.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: The Companions of the Wood, and the People of Tubba'; each one (of them) rejected the messengers, and My warning was duly fulfilled (in them).

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Are the unbelievers among you any better than they? Or is there immunity for you in the Scriptures?

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: What, are your unbelievers better than those? Or have you an immunity in the Scrolls?

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Are your infidels better than these? Or is there an immunity for you in the Writs?

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: Are your disbelievers (O Quraish!) better than these [nations of Nuh (Noah), Lout (Lot), Salih (Saleh), and the people of Fir'aun (Pharaoh), etc., who were destroyed)? Or have you an immunity (against Our Torment) in the Divine Scriptures?

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Are your unbelievers better than all those? Or do you have immunity in the scriptures?

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Are your unbelievers (of Makkah) any better than they? Or have you been granted any immunity in the Scriptures?

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: Are your disbelievers better than these Or have you immunity in the Divine Scriptures

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Are your disbelievers better than those, or have ye some immunity in the scriptures?

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Are your faithless better than those? Have you [been granted] [some sort of] immunity in the scriptures?

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: What then, are your unbelievers better than those? Or do you have an immunity (written) in the Scrolls?

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Are your disbelievers better than those [former ones], or have you immunity in the scripture?

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Are you disbelievers mightier than those of the nations which We destroyed before or have you received amnesty through the ancient Scriptures?

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Are the unbelievers of yours better than these, or is there an exemption for you in the scriptures?

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Are your people who deny the truth better than those? Or have you been given immunity in the Scriptures?

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Are your Unbelievers, (O Quraish), better than they? Or have ye an immunity in the Sacred Books?

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: "O my people (I fear) lest your opposition to me should bring you the like of what befell the people of Noah or Hud or Saleh; and the people of Lot are not distant from you.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: O my people, let not the breach with me move you, so that there smite you the like of what smote the people of Noah, or the people of Hood, or the people of Salih; and the people of Lot are not far away from you.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: And, O my people! let not the cleavage with me incite you so that there befall you the like of that which befell the people of Nuh and the people of Hud and the people of Saleh; and the people of Lut are not from you far away.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "And O my people! Let not my Shiqaqcause you to suffer the fate similar to that of the people of Nuh (Noah) or of Hud or of Salih (Saleh), and the people of Lout (Lot) are not far off from you!

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: “O my people, let not your hostility towards me cause you to suffer what was suffered by the people of Noah, or the people of Hud, or the people of Saleh. The people of Lot are not far away from you.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: My people! Let not your opposition to me lead you to guilt that would bring upon you the chastisement that struck earlier the people of Noah, and the people of Hud, and the people of Salih. And the land of the people of Lot is not far from you!

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "And O my people! Let not my Shiqaq cause you to suffer the fate similar to that of the people of Nuh or of Hud or of Salih, and the people of Lut are not far off from you!"

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: And, O my people! Let not the schism with me cause you to sin so that there befall you that which befell the folk of Noah and the folk of Hud, and the folk of Salih; and the folk of Lot are not far off from you.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: O my people, do not let your defiance toward me lead you to be visited by the like of what was visited on the people of Noah, or the people of Hud, or the people of Salih, and the people of Lot are not distant from you.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: And my nation, do not let your breaking with me bring upon you something similar to that which struck the nation of Noah, Hood, and Salih and it is not long since the people of Lot (were punished).

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: And O my people, let not [your] dissension from me cause you to be struck by that similar to what struck the people of Noah or the people of Hud or the people of Salih. And the people of Lot are not from you far away.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: My people, do not let your opposition to me lead you to commit sins or make you suffer what the people of Noah, Hud, and Salih suffered. Remember that the people of Lot were destroyed not very long ago.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: And, O my people! let not opposition to me make you guilty so that there may befall you the like of what befell the people of Nuh, or the people of Hud, or the people of Salih, nor are the people of Lut far off from you;

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: O my people, do not let your opposition to me bring upon you a fate similar to the peoples of Noah or Hud or Salih; nor is it long since the people of Lot were punished!

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "And O my people! let not my dissent (from you) cause you to sin, lest ye suffer a fate similar to that of the people of Noah or of Hud or of Salih, nor are the people of Lut far off from you!

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: And We seized all of them for their crimes. Against some We sent a violent wind hurling stones, and some We seized with a mighty blast, and some We submerged under the earth, and some We drowned. It was not for God to wrong them, they wronged themselves.

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: Each We seized for his sin; and of them against some We loosed a squall of pebbles and some were seized by the Cry, and some We made the earth to swallow, and some We drowned; God would never wrong them, but they wronged themselves.

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Wherefore each We laid hold of for his sin. Of them were some on whom We sent a violent wind; and of them were some - who were overtaken by a shout; and of them were some - with whom We sank the earth; and of them were some - whom We drowned. Allah was not such as to wrong them, but themselves they were wont to wrong.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: So We punished each (of them) for his sins, of them were some on whom We sent Hasiban (a violent wind with shower of stones) [as the people of Lout (Lot)], and of them were some who were overtaken by As-Saihah [torment - awful cry, etc. (as Thamud or Shu'aib's people)], and of them were some whom We caused the earth to swallow [as Qarun (Korah)], and of them were some whom We drowned [as the people of Nuh (Noah), or Fir'aun (Pharaoh) and his people]. It was not Allah Who wronged them, but they wronged themselves.

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: Each We seized by his sin. Against some We sent a sandstorm. Some were struck by the Blast. Some We caused the ground to cave in beneath them. And some We drowned. It was not God who wronged them, but it was they who wronged their own selves.

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: So We seized each for their sin. We let loose upon some a violent tornado with showers of stones; some were overtaken by a mighty Cry; some were caused to be swallowed up by the earth, and some We drowned. Allah would not wrong them, but it is they who wronged themselves.

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: So, We punished each for his sins, of them were some on whom We sent a Hasib, and of them were some who were overtaken by As-Sayhah, and of them were some whom We caused the earth to swallow, and of them were some whom We drowned. It was not Allah Who wronged them, but they wronged themselves.

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: So We took each one in his sin; of them was he on whom We sent a hurricane, and of them was he who was overtaken by the (Awful) Cry, and of them was he whom We caused the earth to swallow, and of them was he whom We drowned. It was not for Allah to wrong them, but they wronged themselves.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: So We seized each [of them] for his sin: among them were those upon whom We unleashed a rain of stones, and among them were those who were seized by the Cry, and among them were those whom We caused the earth to swallow, and among them were those whom We drowned. It was not Allah who wronged them, but it was they who used to wrong themselves.

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Each of them We seized for his sin. On some We loosed a squall of pebbles, and others were seized by the Cry. Some We caused to be swallowed up by the earth, and some We drowned. Allah would never wrong them but they wronged themselves.

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: So each We seized for his sin; and among them were those upon whom We sent a storm of stones, and among them were those who were seized by the blast [from the sky], and among them were those whom We caused the earth to swallow, and among them were those whom We drowned. And Allah would not have wronged them, but it was they who were wronging themselves.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We punished all of these people because of their sin. Some of them were struck by a violent sand-storm, some by a blast of sound, others were swallowed up by the earth, and some were drowned (in the sea). God did not do injustice to them, but they had wronged themselves.

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: So each We punished for his sin; of them was he on whom We sent down a violent storm, and of them was he whom the rumbling overtook, and of them was he whom We made to be swallowed up by the earth, and of them was he whom We drowned; and it did not beseem Allah that He should be unjust to them, but they were unjust to their own souls.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: So We seized each one for his sins; some We struck with a violent storm; some of them were overcome by a sudden blast, some were swallowed up by the earth and some We drowned. God did not wrong them: they wronged themselves.

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: Each one of them We seized for his crime: of them, against some We sent a violent tornado (with showers of stones); some were caught by a (mighty) Blast; some We caused the earth to swallow up; and some We drowned (in the waters): It was not Allah Who injured (or oppressed) them:" They injured (and oppressed) their own souls.

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: Do you wonder that a warning has come to you from your Lord through a man who is one of you and warns you? Remember, He made you leaders after the people of Noah, and gave you a greater increase in your stature. So think of the favours of God; you may haply be blessed."

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: What, do you wonder that a reminder from your Lord should come to you by the lips of a man from among you? That he may warn you; and remember when He appointed you as successors after the people of Noah, and increased you in stature broadly; remember God's bounties; haply you will prosper.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: Marvel ye that an admonition from your Lord should come unto you upon a Man from amongst you in order that he may warn you? Remember what time He made you successors after the people, of Nuh, and increased you amply in stature Remember the benefits of Allah, that haply ye may fare well.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "Do you wonder that there has come to you a Reminder (and an advice) from your Lord through a man from amongst you that he may warn you? And remember that He made you successors after the people of Nuh (Noah), and increased you amply in stature. So remember the graces (bestowed upon you) from Allah, so that you may be successful."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: “Are you surprised that a reminder has come to you from your Lord, through a man from among you, to warn you? Remember how He made you successors after the people of Noah, and increased you greatly in stature. And remember God’s blessings, so that you may prosper.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: Do you wonder that an exhortation should come to you from your Lord through a man from amongst yourselves that he may warn you? And do call to mind when He made you successors after the people of Noah and amply increased you in stature. Remember then the wondrous bounties of Allah, that you may prosper.'

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "Do you wonder that there has come to you a Reminder (and an advice) from your Lord through a man from among you to warn you And remember that He made you successors (generations after generations) after the people of Nuh and increased you amply in stature. So remember the graces (bestowed upon you) from Allah so that you may be successful."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: Marvel ye that there should come unto you a Reminder from your Lord by means of a man among you, that he may warn you? Remember how He made you viceroys after Noah's folk, and gave you growth of stature. Remember (all) the bounties of your Lord, that haply ye may be successful.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: Do you consider it odd that there should come to you a reminder from your Lord through a man from among yourselves, so that he may warn you? Remember when He made you successors after the people of Noah, and increased you vastly in creation. So remember Allah’s bounties so that you may be felicitous.’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: Do you wonder that a remembrance should come to you from your Lord to a man from yourselves in order that he may warn you? Remember, that He has made you the heirs of Noah's nation and increased you in broad stature, remember the favors of Allah in order that you prosper'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: Then do you wonder that there has come to you a reminder from your Lord through a man from among you, that he may warn you? And remember when He made you successors after the people of Noah and increased you in stature extensively. So remember the favors of Allah that you might succeed.

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: Does it seem strange to you that a reminder from your Lord should be sent to a man among you so that He may warn you? Recall when God appointed you as successors of the people of Noah and increased your power over other people. Give thanks to God for His blessings so that perhaps you will have everlasting happiness."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: What! do you wonder that a reminder has come to you from your Lord through a man from among you that he might warn you? And remember when He made you successors after Nuh's people and increased you in excellence in respect of make; therefore remember the benefits of Allah, that you may be successful.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translation.text: Do you find it strange that a Message should come from your Lord, through one of your own men, to warn you? Remember how He made you successors of Noah's people, and increased you greatly in stature. Remember the favours of God, so that you may prosper."

translation.author: Yusuf Ali
translation.text: "Do ye wonder that there hath come to you a message from your Lord through a man of your own people, to warn you? call in remembrance that He made you inheritors after the people of Noah, and gave you a stature tall among the nations. Call in remembrance the benefits (ye have received) from Allah: that so ye may prosper."

translation.author: Ahmed Ali
translation.text: We shall only be guilty of blaspheming God if we accept your way after the people of Noah, and gave you a greater increase in your stature. So think of the favours of God; you may haply be blessed."

translation.author: Arberry
translation.text: We should have forged against God a lie if we returned into your creed; after God delivered us from it. It is not for us to return into it, unless God our Lord so will. Our Lord embraces all things in His knowledge. In God we have put our trust. Our Lord, give true deliverance between us and our people; Thou art the best of deliverers.'

translation.author: Daryabadi
translation.text: We must have been fabricating a lie against Allah if we returned to your faith after Allah hath delivered us therefrom. And it is not for us to return thereunto except that Allah our Lord so willed; everything our Lord comprehendeth in His knowledge; in Allah we place our trust. O our Lord! decide Thou between us and our people with truth, Thou art the Best of the deciders.

translation.author: Hilali & Khan
translation.text: "We should have invented a lie against Allah if we returned to your religion, after Allah has rescued us from it. And it is not for us to return to it unless Allah, our Lord, should will. Our Lord comprehends all things in His Knowledge. In Allah (Alone) we put our trust. Our Lord! Judge between us and our people in truth, for You are the Best of those who give judgment."

translation.author: Itani
translation.text: “We would be fabricating falsehood against God, if we were to return to your religion, after God has saved us from it. It is not for us to return to it, unless God, our Lord, wills. Our Lord embraces all things in knowledge. In God we place our trust. Our Lord, decide between us and our people in truth, for You are the Best of Deciders.”

translation.author: Maududi
translation.text: If we return to your faith after Allah has delivered us from it we would be fabricating a lie against Allah. nor can we return to it again unless it be by, the will of Allah, our Lord. Our Lord has knowledge of all things, and in Allah we put our trust. Our Lord! Judge rightly between us and our people, for You are the best of those who judge.'

translation.author: Mubarakpuri
translation.text: "We should have invented a lie against Allah if we returned to your religion, after Allah has rescued us from it. And it is not for us to return to it unless Allah, our Lord, should will. Our Lord comprehends all things in His knowledge. In Allah (Alone) we put our trust. Our Lord! Judge between us and our people in truth, for You are the best of those who give judgment."

translation.author: Pickthall
translation.text: We should have invented a lie against Allah if we returned to your religion after Allah hath rescued us from it. It is not for us to return to it unless Allah our Lord should (so) will. Our Lord comprehendeth all things in knowledge. In Allah do we put our trust. Our Lord! Decide with truth between us and our folk, for Thou art the best of those who make decision.

translation.author: Qarai
translation.text: We would be fabricating a lie against Allah should we revert to your creed after Allah had delivered us from it. It does not behoove us to return to it, unless Allah, our Lord, should wish so. Our Lord embraces all things in [His] knowledge. In Allah we have put our trust.’ ‘Our Lord! Judge justly between us and our people, and You are the best of judges!’

translation.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translation.text: We would have invented lies about Allah if we returned to your creed from which Allah has saved us, it is not for us that we return into it again except if Allah, our Lord wills. For our Lord has embraced everything with knowledge, and in Allah we have put our trust. Our Lord, open between us and our nation with truth, You are the Best of openers'

translation.author: Saheeh International
translation.text: We would have invented against Allah a lie if we returned to your religion after Allah had saved us from it. And it is not for us to return to it except that Allah, our Lord, should will. Our Lord has encompassed all things in knowledge. Upon Allah we have relied. Our Lord, decide between us and our people in truth, and You are the best of those who give decision."

translation.author: Sarwar
translation.text: We would certainly be inventing falsehoods against God if we were to accept your way of life, when God has already saved us from it. We do not have to accept it unless God, our Lord, wills it. Our Lord's knowledge covers all things. We trust in Him and ask Him to judge among us and our people, for He is the best Judge."

translation.author: Shakir
translation.text: Indeed we shall have forged a lie against Allah If we go back to your religion after Allah has delivered us from It, and it befits us not that we should go back to it, except if Allah our Lord please: Our Lord comprehends all things in His knowledge; in Allah do we trust: Our Lord! decide between us and our people with truth; and Thou art the best of deciders.

translation.author: Wahiduddin Khan
